title: Few-Sample GNSS Signal LOS/NLOS Identification via Physical-Semantic Distillation from Large Language Models
authors: Qi Yao, Jingjing Yang, Shunfang Wang, Zhe Xiao, Ming Huang
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) are widely used for positioning and navigation, yet NLOS signals severely degrade performance in urban environments. Existing deep learning-based LOS/NLOS identification methods require large labeled datasets and lack physical interpretability. To address this issue, we propose a few-sample GNSS signal LOS/NLOS identification framework based on physical-semantic distillation from large language models (LLMs), where physics-informed reasoning generates soft NLOS labels to guide a lightweight student model. Experiments show a 4.5% accuracy improvement on a public dataset with 1% training data, and 2.4% gain on a self-collected dataset under the same setting.

title: Dependence of optical refractive index of seawater on temperature, salinity, pressure, and wavelength in near-infrared waveband for warm and shallow sea_supp6-3693439.mp4
authors: Shanshan Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Dependence of optical refractive index (RI) of seawater on temperature, salinity, and pressure is crucial for optical fiber sensors operated in seawater. Expressions of RI of seawater on temperature, salinity, pressure, and wavelength applicable to near-infrared waveband are developed based on a great deal of RI data measured by a semi-encapsulated microfiber Mach-Zehnder interferometer sensor. To verify the accuracy of these expressions, RI measured by infrared refractometer, theoretical calculated sensitivity and experimental measured sensitivity are used, by which the most accurate expression is determined. Based on this expression, a variable sensitivity matrix is established to demodulate temperature, salinity and pressure simultaneously with errors of 10.91 %, 6.09 % and 18.80 %, respectively. The expression proposed here will provide valuable references for theoretical and experimental study on optical fiber sensors operated in infrared waveband and push the applications of fiber optical sensors in ocean investigations.

title: A Thyristor-Switched Pulsed Power System With 78-ns Jitter for a Coaxial Plasma Gun
authors: Neil P. Laya, Tetiana Tymoshevska, David L. Chesny, Mark B. Moffett, Kirk J. Boehm, Kunning G. Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: A fiber-optically controlled and thyristor-switched coaxial plasma gun (CPG) pulsed power system has been designed, built, and fired at up to 2 kV and 6.4 kA, with a 6.65-MW momentary peak power output from the capacitor. Total system firing jitter from initial digital signal to 90% peak current rise was measured at 78.15 ns, representing 0.18% of the total pulse time of 44.10 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s. This includes both thyristor turn-on and CPG breakdown. Standard deviation of peak current over repeated firings was measured to be 21.09 A (0.33%). The system has experienced 100+ firings at 2 kV without a single misfire. A voltage profiling analysis has been performed where voltage differentials across system components, including the thyristor, were collected temporally for the entire pulse. High-speed and long-exposure photography were used to verify CPG operation and mass-injection/gas-puff system timing. This study sets the groundwork for plasma-load pulsed power systems using thyristors, as well as informing expanded voltage and current capability, enabling fine timing control and multisystem synchronization, allowing greater repeatability and reliability, and informing multithyristor array systems.

title: Tackling Expressive Feature-Modeling Constructs with Pseudo-Boolean d-DNNF Compilation
authors: Chico Sundermann, Stefan Vill, Elias Kuiter, Sebastian Krieter, Thomas Thüm, Matthias Tichy
publish: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering
abstract: Configurable systems typically consist of reusable assets that have dependencies between each other. To specify such dependencies, feature models are commonly used. As feature models in practice are often complex, automated reasoning is typically employed to analyze the dependencies. Here, the de facto standard is translating the feature model to conjunctive normal form (CNF) to enable employing off-the-shelf tools, such as SAT or #SAT solvers. However, modern feature-modeling dialects often contain constructs, such as cardinality constraints, that are ill-suited for conversion to CNF. This mismatch between the input of reasoning engines and the available feature-modeling dialects limits the applicability of the more expressive constructs. In this work, we shorten this gap between expressive constructs and scalable automated reasoning. Our contribution is twofold: First, we provide a pseudo-Boolean encoding for feature models, which facilitates smaller representations of commonly employed constructs compared to Boolean encoding. Second, we propose a novel method to compile pseudo-Boolean formulas to Boolean d- DNNFs. With the compiled d-DNNFs, we can resort to a plethora of efficient analyses already used in feature modeling. Our empirical evaluation shows that our proposal substantially outperforms the state-of-the-art based on CNF inputs for expressive constructs. For every considered dataset representing different feature models and feature-modeling constructs, the feature models can be significantly faster translated to pseudo-Boolean than to CNF. Overall, deriving d-DNNFs from a feature model with the targeted expressive constraints can be substantially accelerated using our pseudo-Boolean approach. For instance, the Boolean approach only scales for group cardinalities with up-to 13 features while pseudo-Boolean d-DNNF compilation can compile cardinalities with thousands of features. Furthermore, our approach is competitive on feature models with only basic constructs.

title: Optimal Output Synchronization of Heterogeneous MASs: A Computationally Efficient Data-Driven Learning Approach
authors: Shicheng Huo, Zhipeng Wang, Chao Deng, Ya Zhang, Hao Shen
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: In this paper, we study the data-driven H∞ optimal control problem for output synchronization of heterogeneous multi-agent systems via a computationally efficient reinforcement learning (RL) method. Unlike the existing RL control results which solve for the optimal controller through the vectorization and Kronecker product (VKP) operations where the large size of the Kronecker product increases computational complexity, a computationally efficient data-driven RL control method is studied in this paper. First, the optimal output synchronization problem is transformed into an H∞ optimal control problem by constructing an internal model-based distributed control protocol. Then, the corresponding algebraic Riccati equation for the H∞ optimal control is reformulated as a generalized Sylvester-transpose matrix equation (GSTME) at each iteration. To solve the GSTME, the matrix form of the conjugate gradient least squares algorithms that do not require the VKP operations is proposed based on the system dynamics and measurement data of multi-agent systems, respectively. Furthermore, the initial stabilizing gain required for policy iteration learning is proposed based on a data-based solution method. Finally, the feasibility of the designed algorithms is demonstrated through a numerical simulation, and its better computational efficiency compared to existing methods is shown.

title: Event-Triggered Control for T-S Fuzzy of Multi-Area LFC Power Systems under Energy-Limited DoS Attacks
authors: Zhilin Yang, Yajuan Liu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Load Frequency Control (LFC) is imperative in ensuring the stability of power systems in terms of frequency, and its efficacy is frequently impacted by device nonlinearities and cyber attacks. This paper investigates the security control of multi-area LFC power systems under energy-limited denial-of-service (DoS) attacks. Firstly, a unified Takagi-Sugeno (T-S) fuzzy model for multi-area LFC power systems is developed by analysing the nonlinear components in the dynamic characteristics of the turbine and governor. Secondly, a DoS-dependent novel event-triggering mechanism (NETM) is proposed, and the impact of DoS attacks is incorporated into the design of the triggering mechanism. Sufficient conditions for the uniform ultimate boundedness stability of the power system are provided, and an algorithm is devised to determine the time required for a power system state trajectory to enter the stable region. The efficacy of the proposed control method is subsequently validated through the utilization of simulation examples.

title: A Vertically Stacked Dual-Junction SPAD for Wide Spectral Response
authors: Bing Zhang, Pengfei Hu, Congzhen Hu, Yiyun Xie, Chi Wang, Youze Xin, RuBin Yan, Li Geng
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: This article presents a wide spectral response single-photon avalanche diode (SPAD) fabricated in a 110 nm CMOS image sensor (CIS) technology. The device integrates two vertically stacked avalanche multiplication regions comprising a shallow P++/ N-enrichment layer junction and a deeper n-type buried layer (NBL) / deep p-type buried layer (DPBL) junction, respectively. Measurement results show a peak photon detection probability (PDP) of 82% at 600 nm, with a broadband PDP exceeding 18% across 400–850 nm under an excess bias voltage of 5.0 V. At room temperature, the SPAD exhibits a moderate dark count rate (DCR) of ∼65 cps/μm². The temporal resolution corresponds to a full-width-at-half-maximum (FWHM) jitter of 69 ps at 450 nm and 110 ps at 905 nm, respectively. Owing to its vertically stacked dual-junction design, the proposed SPAD provides a compact pixel solution suitable for applications requiring operation from visible to NIR wavelengths.

title: Simpler is Better: Feature Guard and Interaction for Semantic Correspondence_supp1-3693144.mp4
authors: Songlin Du
publish: N/A
abstract: Semantic correspondence establishes keypoint correspondences between different instances of the same category. Fusing texture and semantic features from vision foundation models like stable diffusion (SD) and DINO significantly improves matching performance. However, we found an unnoticed yet essential problem: current feature fusion enhances the edge and semantic information in SD features with fine textures and DINOv2 features with fine semantics, but it destroys the semantic and structural information in SD features with weak and coarse semantics. We propose guard features (GuFT), a simple yet efficient method, to prevent feature degradation. Moreover, matching methods designed for traditional deep neural networks can be simplified based on two key insights: 1) vision foundation models provide rich visual knowledge; and 2) GuFT yields high-quality feature descriptors. We propose a bottleneck-style non-shared aggregation and backward interaction (NABI) module to efficiently capture intra- and inter-feature relationships, instead of common self- and cross-attention. The resulting framework, SimBetter, embodies a “simpler is better” design philosophy. It achieves state-of-the-art results with lower computation on SPair-71k, AP-10K, and PF-PASCAL, excelling in geometry-aware, cross-species, cross-family, and cross-dataset tasks. SimBetter also shows excellent potential in the applications of image-video semantic correspondence and sticker editing. Code is available at https://github.com/wzhlearning/SimBetter.

title: Characterization of DC Partial Discharge in SF6 Using PMT and Photon Counting Method
authors: Yixuan Zhou, Weiqi Qin, Qian Wang, Weiguo Li, Xu Yang, Changhong Zhang, Chuanyang Li, Jinliang He
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: Partial discharge (PD) light signals of two defects, including conductor protrusion and surface-attached metal, were measured simultaneously using a PMT and a photon counting head at DC voltage in SF6. The defect features were extracted using pulse sequence analysis (PSA) and two-dimensional kernel density estimation (KDE). Experimental results demonstrate that different defect types and voltage polarities produce distinctive PSA – KDE patterns. Photon-counting results show clear correlation with applied voltage, enabling quantitative defect assessment. The results verify that photon-based detection provides high sensitivity and strong electromagnetic immunity, offering an effective approach for DC PD characterization and early insulation diagnosis in DC GIE.

title: Innovative Monitoring System Open Source Based: A Case Study of a Microgrid Lab with Digital Twin, PV and BESS Integration
authors: Riccardo Loggia, Roberto Menichelli, Lorenzo Frattale Mascioli, Andrea Golino, Cristina Moscatiello, Luigi Martirano
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: As microgrids and modern low-voltage electrical systems continue to evolve, the demand for advanced monitoring tools has grown significantly. Ensuring reliable operation now requires systems capable of overseeing all functional aspects to maintain safety, stability, and a high degree of automation. To address these needs, SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) solutions have emerged, offering diverse structures and protocols, but ultimately converging on a common purpose: monitoring and managing power flows effectively. The Digital Twin, based on a BIM (Building Information Modeling) approach, is the best tool for managing microgrids because it is built during the 3D digital design process, following the entire life cycle of the system. The purpose of this document is to analyze the possibilities and limitations of the monitoring and supervision systems on the market, introducing our completely open-source alternative. Through the analysis of a case study within a university laboratory microgrid, different scenarios will be analyzed in order to evaluate the behavior of the system under the various conditions presented and propose solutions for future software updates.

title: Switch-Multiplexed Quasi-Two-Stage Boost-DAB Converter Realizing Zero-Voltage Switching Across the Full Power Range
authors: Zhengmei Lu, Wenjie Bi, Miao Zhu, Chuanchuan Hou
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: A switch-multiplexed quasi-two-stage Boost-DAB (QTBDAB) converter is presented in this work, which is designed to maintain balanced voltage amplitudes across the DAB leakage inductor under single-phase-shift (SPS) modulation. Through a split-bus configuration at the intermediate DC link, the Boost stage and the DAB stage are merged into a quasi-two-stage structure that inherently enables partial power processing, while the total switch count is minimized by sharing switching devices between the two stages. To guarantee voltage balance across the DAB leakage inductor, a dedicated modulation scheme is employed: the duty cycle serves as the voltage-regulation variable, while the phase-shift ratio governs both the amplitude and the direction of the transferred power. Series DC-blocking capacitors are inserted on both the primary and secondary sides to eliminate the DC offset present in the full-bridge output waveforms, thereby preventing magnetic saturation of the inductor and transformer cores. Based on the partial power transfer architecture, the voltage stress and current stress of the front-stage Boost converter are reduced, which improves conversion efficiency as well as higher power density.

title: An Evaluation of Few-Shot Learning Strategies for Essential Oil Classification: Balancing Data Augmentation and Meta-Learning
authors: Ranggi Praharaningtyas Aji, Bayu Priya Mukti, Primandani Arsi, Aulia Safitri, Gilang Satria Mahardika
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: Classifying essential oil quality from visual data is a challenging few-shot learning problem due to extreme data scarcity and class imbalance. Previous studies often rely on single train-test splits, leading to statistically unreliable conclusions. This paper presents a rigorous comparative analysis of three distinct strategies—Simple Transfer Learning (STL), Fine-tuned Prototypical Networks (ProtoNet), and Augmented Transfer Learning (ATL)—on a dataset of 63 essential oil images. To ensure statistical robustness, we employ Stratified 5-Fold Cross-Validation and report mean performance metrics with standard deviations. Our results demonstrate that while standard Prototypical Networks suffer from optimization difficulties in low-data regimes (81.3 % accuracy), the Augmented Transfer Learning strategy yields superior and most stable performance (91.7 % accuracy, 90.5% Macro F1-score). An ablation study confirms that aggressive data augmentation is the dominant factor in improving performance over architectural or metric-learning variations. We conclude that for industrial applications with severely limited data, resources may be better invested in developing robust data augmentation pipelines than in implementing complex meta-learning algorithms.

title: Explainable AI for Eye Tracking-Based Prediction: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Tio Eko Prabowo, Warsun Najib, Sunu Wibirama
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: The integration of deep learning with eye tracking data has enabled the development of robust classification systems. However, their black box nature hinders adoption in safety-critical and healthcare domains, where model interpretability is paramount. To address this knowledge gap, this systematic literature review synthesized 21 peer-reviewed studies (2020-November 2025) from IEEE Xplore and Scopus databases following Kitchenham's methodology and PRISMA guidelines. This study examined various explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) techniques applied to eye tracking models, application domains, and evaluation approaches. SHAP (62%) and LIME (19 %) were the predominant techniques, with gradient-based and specialized methods forming the minority. Various applications primarily focused on virtual reality (38%) and healthcare (29 %), driven by the need for trust and regulatory compliance. Previous studies have indicated that XAI-guided feature selection reduces model complexity by 3 - to 5-fold, enabling resource-constrained deployment. Although quantitative validation is universal, human-centered evaluations remain limited (29 %), and formal assessment of explanation quality metrics such as faithfulness, sensitivity, stability, and comprehensiveness is largely absent. Major challenges include the absence of standardized evaluation frameworks, demographic generalization gaps, and the dominance of post-hoc over intrinsic interpretability approaches. Future studies should establish domain-specific benchmarks, integrate cognitive science perspectives, and embed ethical accountability as explainability transitions from a feature to a requirement.

title: Design of an Experimental Model for Transportation Process Analysis of People with Sensory Disabilities using Process Mining
authors: Hanung Nindito Prasetyo, Bayu Rima Aditya, Ferra Arik Tridalestari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: Modern public transportation systems increasingly rely on information systems to support trip planning, service transactions, and real-time information delivery. However, existing transportation information systems are generally designed based on assumptions of non-disabled users, resulting in the needs of persons with sensory disabilities often not being explicitly represented in system process design. This study aims to design and evaluate an experimental model that captures transportation processes experienced by persons with sensory disabilities while identifying process-based requirements for the development of transportation information systems. This study adopts a Design Science Research approach combined with role-playing-based experimentation and process mining analysis. Persons with sensory disabilities are involved as primary actors in the role-playing activities to enact predefined transportation journey scenarios, while researchers conduct structured participatory observations to record travel-related activities as process data. The resulting data are then represented as event logs and analyzed using process mining techniques to derive actual process flows. The results demonstrate that the simulation stages consisting of Role Playing, activity recording, event log construction, and process mining analysis are able to systematically represent transportation processes experienced by persons with sensory disabilities. This approach enables the identification of information requirements, system design failure points, and system dependencies on users' sensory capabilities. The study concludes that this process-based simulation framework can serve as an effective mechanism for evaluating and designing more adaptive and inclusive transportation information systems, contributing to the improvement of public transportation service quality for all users in Indonesia.

title: Explainable Multimodal AI System for Early Prediction of Kidney Failure Using Clinical and CT Imaging Data
authors: Hassan Alruqi, Hossam Meshref
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: This work presents a multimodal artificial intelligence framework for the early prediction of kidney failure during the critical initial hours of hospital admission, when timely intervention can still alter disease progression. The proposed system integrates structured electronic health records with renal CT imaging, leveraging large-scale data from the MIMIC-IV critical-care database and the Kaggle CKD dataset to capture both biochemical and structural indicators of renal dysfunction. Extensive comparisons with traditional machine-learning and deep-learning models show that multimodal feature fusion delivers a clear performance advantage over unimodal approaches, achieving an AUC of 0.982 and a sensitivity of 97.4 %. Explainable AI analyses verify that predictions are driven by clinically relevant biomarkers and anatomically meaningful renal regions identified through Grad-CAM, while real-time inference below 20 ms supports practical deployment as an effective decision-support tool in emergency and ICU settings.

title: It's a Dirty Job: Defining and Mitigating Dust and Sand Hazards on the Dragonfly Mission to Titan
authors: Ralph D. Lorenz
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: Vehicles on or near a planetary surface are exposed to interactions with surface material which may degrade functions of vehicle equipment or scientific instruments. The Dragonfly rotorcraft lander for Titan confronts a wide range of such potential interactions, as commentators frequently observe. In this paper, we identify these interactions, making the critical distinction between what we term dust, sand and mud. Making such a decomposition allows the different failure mechanisms to be assessed and mitigated. Dust refers to particulates that can be suspended in air, and generally presents a hazard by optical or mechanical blockage. Sand affects only areas directly impinged upon by energetic flow (which includes the downwash from Dragonfly's rotors) but can cause erosion by impact. Mud refers to material that may be cohesive/adhesive, but is unlikely to be lofted by airflow. Other related interactions such as melting and triboelectric charging are briefly discussed.

title: Integration of AI into Islamic Finance and Its Impacts: A Literature-Driven Analysis- The Case of United Kingdom
authors: Shehzad Ahmed, Hassan Ashraf Usmani
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: In the global financial field, artificial intelligence (AI) has become a lever of rapid transformation. Artificial intelligence proposes solutions and opportunities to strengthen the Islamic financial system. In certain aspects, the application of AI in Islamic fintech is still in its early stages; several institutions are currently experimenting with or piloting it. At this stage, the bibliographic study is the safest method, given the limitation of real-time operational information. By relying on previous research, this study evaluates the current level of the integration of artificial intelligence in the Islamic financial system, in order to judge the possibilities, the risks, and the possible ethical considerations. The United Kingdom is used as a case study because it constitutes one of the most important Western centers for Islamic banking and fintech innovation, with a legal framework that favors financial development and technological experimentation. This study proposes a framework grounded in the principles of Maqasid al-Shari'ah and the basic foundations of Islamic finance. This article differs from previous research that examined only the applications of AI from a technical or regulatory perspective; it integrates, in an innovative way, the Maqasid al-Shari'ah as an important foundation for the analysis of Islamic financial innovations driven by AI. And this research is presented as the first step toward the development of an artificial intelligence application primarily for Islamic financial products, which aim to incorporate technological innovation into financial solutions in conformity with Sharia.

title: Explainable AI for Automated Emergency Triage of Brain CT Pathologies: A Multi-Level Classification Framework
authors: Khalid Aljuaid, Hossam Meshref
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: The quick and accurate distinction between intracranial hemorrhage (ICH) and ischemic stroke is very important for emergency neurological triage since these diseases necessitate treatments that are totally opposite from each other. The present study gives a detailed deep learning framework for automated brain CT pathology classification which is tested on three complementary case studies. We performed a thorough comparison of traditional machine learning (XGBoost, Random Forest, SVM, Logistic Regression) versus state-of-the-art deep learning architectures (EfficientNet-B0/B7, ResNet18/50/152, DenseNet121, ConvNeXt-Large, Swin Transformer) using 8,097-13,500 CT images classified into seven hemorrhage categories (Normal, Ischemia, Intraventricular, Intraparenchymal, Subarachnoid, Epidural, Subdural). In Case Study 1, EfficientNet-B0 accomplished seven-class accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90.33\%}$</tex> with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.0M$</tex> parameters, surpassing traditional ML by 17.79 percentage points. In Case Study 2, three-window CT preprocessing (Brain/Subdural/Bone windows) was applied with ensemble stacking achieving clinical-grade 97.26% four-class accuracy. In Case Study 3, a near-perfect 99.21% three-class accuracy was reached through Swin Transformer-based voting ensemble. Explainable AI integration (Grad-CAM, Grad-CAM++, LIME) confirmed that the models accurately concentrated on the sites of hemorrhage and ischemia, thus excluding artifacts. The real-time inference <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<20 ~\text{ms}$</tex> per image) makes it suitable for emergency situations. The findings prove that the triaging process can be fully automated for neurological emergencies, which need very quick human-like expert-level decisions.

title: A Survey of Neuro-Symbolic AI: Integration Paradigms, Taxonomy, and Formal Specification
authors: Mohamed Hamdy, Khaled Bahnasy, Mohamed Dahab, Doaa Hegazy
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: Explainable AI is not feasible by applying most the common methods of low-level deep perception models. Deep learning (DL) excels at picking out patterns, but let's be real: it is a total data hog, it's impossible to see inside of it, and it usually lacks even basic logic. It has a lack of reasoning abilities. Symbolic systems using different Knowledge representation like Knowledge Graphs (KG) and rules can provide a good complement option towards hybrid AI systems that can provide explainable AI. The integration of low-level data deriven deepl learning and symbolic systems can shape a path to realize future hybrid AI systems. This paper introduce a survey that introduce a taxonomy and shows a systematic review on the different synergistic integration approaches.

title: Fed-CASQ: Enhancing Class-Wise Accuracy in Pervasive Federated Learning with Class-Aware Scaling and Quantization
authors: Emon Dey, Anuradha Ravi, Gaurav Shinde, Garvit Chugh, Indrajeet Ghosh, Archan Misra, Nirmalya Roy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Communications (PerCom)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative machine learning across decentralized devices and data sources, but resource constraints on pervasive devices necessitate efficient model compression. Existing approaches, such as quantization for on-device training, often degrade accuracy, especially for classes that are difficult to learn due to imbalance, poor-quality samples, or inherent complexity. This results in persistent accuracy gaps across classes. We propose Fed-CASQ's a novel framework that couples class-aware strategies into the quantization process to jointly improve efficiency and accuracy in pervasive FL. Unlike prior works that address quantization and imbalance separately, Fed-CASQ adaptively selects quantization levels based on device resources and leverages Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (LRP) to assess class-relevant convolutional neural network (CNN) filters on the client side. An adaptive weight scaling mechanism is then applied to amplify critical information for low-accuracy classes before aggregation. At the server, a complementary novel aggregation strategy mitigates global imbalance across clients, ensuring that underperforming classes receive proportional attention during model updates. We theoretically establish that Fed-CASQ achieves a convergence rate of ${\mathcal{O}}\left({\frac{{\kappa *\hat \sigma *\hat \delta }}{{\sqrt T }}}\right)$ under non-convex settings. We empirically establish that quantization directly influences the performance of under sampled (minority) classes. Experimental results further show that Fed-CASQ substantially narrows the performance gap for low-accuracy classes, improving their accuracy by ≈30%, while reducing training latency by over 56% on resource-constrained pervasive devices.

title: Storage Duration and Thermal Capacity Trade-offs in Carbon-Constrained Wind–PV–Thermal Systems
authors: Yuyang Yan, Husam I. Shaheen, Bo Yang, Yi Zuo, Ghamgeen I. Rashed, Vinko Lešić
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: This paper investigates the capacity interaction between energy storage and thermal generation in carbon-constrained wind–PV–thermal power systems. A robust planning framework with fixed energy-to-power (E/P) storage ratios is developed to evaluate system configurations under an annual carbon emission limit. Using a large-scale case study, we examine how storage duration influences required thermal capacity and operational behavior. Results show that shorter-duration storage significantly increases the minimum thermal capacity required to maintain feasibility under worst-case renewable uncertainty. When storage duration decreases from E/P=5 to E/P=1, required thermal capacity rises by 63.8%. A Thermal Dependency Index (TDI) is introduced to quantify system reliance on firm generation across storage configurations. Operational analysis further reveals that storage primarily performs multi-hour energy shifting during high-load, low-renewable periods while remaining inactive during favorable conditions. The findings demonstrate that storage alone cannot fully replace firm generation under stringent reliability requirements, and highlight the importance of coordinated planning between storage duration and residual thermal capacity in low-carbon power systems.

title: Design and Analysis of PCB Winding Permanent Magnet Linear Motor
authors: Jorge Ulises Ramirez Carrasco, Fuat Kucuk
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: High-precision positioning systems increasingly demand linear actuators that balance high thrust density with compact form factors and ease of manufacturing. However, traditional iron-core linear motors suffer from significant cogging forces, while existing coreless designs are often limited by complex and costly wire-winding processes. This paper proposes a novel coreless linear motor topology designed to address these manufacturing challenges. The proposed system utilizes a modular Printed Circuit Board (PCB) stator architecture combined with a flux-concentrating Halbach permanent magnet array. By employing a modular stacking strategy, the design allows for scalable thrust generation without the fabrication complexity of monolithic high-layer-count boards. The electromagnetic performance of the motor was analyzed and validated through dynamic Finite Element Analysis (FEA) under motion conditions. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed topology achieves highly linear force characteristics with almost zero cogging torque. Furthermore, the design realizes a significant reduction in volumetric profile compared to conventional wire-wound actuators, proving that the modular PCB approach is a viable solution for scalable, high-precision, and low-profile motion systems.

title: Context-Dependent Value of Ccus: Roles of Economy-Wide Carbon Caps, Power-Sector Decarbonization, and End-Use Electrification
authors: Jundai Koketsu, Aya Ishigaki, Ryuta Takashima
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: Carbon Capture, Utilization and Storage (CCUS) can contribute to both emissions avoidance and carbon dioxide removal (CDR) toward carbon neutrality; however, its societal value strongly depends on the context, including the stringency of carbon constraints, the degree of power-sector decarbonization, and the progress of end-use electrification. This study develops an integrated model that jointly represents (i) the power supply chain, (ii) fossil-fuel supply, (iii) CO2 capture, transport, storage, and conversion into synthetic fuels, and (iv) sectoral production and demand calibrated from inputoutput (IO) tables, all within a single social profit maximization problem formulated as a convex quadratic program. We define the context along three axes: (i) an economy-wide carbon cap level (as a percentage of baseline emissions), (ii) power-sector decarbonization (represented by the fuel input coefficient and generation cost, which determine the carbon intensity of electricity), and (iii) end-use electrification rates (sectoral electricity shares). The results indicate that, under moderately stringent caps, post-combustion capture (PCC) from flue gas and CCS are widely adopted, whereas under extremely stringent caps, direct air capture (DAC) becomes indispensable. Moreover, the contribution of CCU (synthetic fuels) is not universal: it ranges from negligible to substantial, and increases sharply as electricity becomes low-carbon. These findings highlight that the value of CCUS, especially CCU, varies markedly across contexts, underscoring the importance of comprehensive and context-specific strategies for carbon neutrality.

title: X-MedVIT: Interpretable Vision Transformers for Clinically Reliable Medical Imaging
authors: Akshaya V, Kishore V G, Harshini M, Hemanth Kumar J
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Artificial intelligence plays the key role of being an integral part of the new generation of computer-assisted medical diagnostic tools, while deep learning algorithms often need better transparency, which reduces their reliability in the field. This paper introduces X-MedVIT, which focuses on the development of an interpretable medical imaging solution technology that uses a new version of the computer vision model, the Vision Transformers (ViT), in the process of conducting diagnostics on X-Ray images taken on the human chest. Unlike the existing ViT algorithm, which primarily relies on the prediction capabilities of the ViT model, the proposed solution comes with the advantage of showing the parts of the image used in decision-making. Using the proposed model yields an image analysis accuracy of about 95%, thus providing reliable support towards vast healthcare development, including global healthcare objectives like those set out in the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals.

title: Intelligent Judicial Decision System Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Chinnam Akhil Sai, Borra Jaswanth Kumar, Borra Hemanth, Chelli Sasikanth, R. Arichandran
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: The growing number of pending cases in lower courts has caused delays in the delivery of justice. The highest-volume court cases are located in the district courts and they need better analytical assistance. The majority of the available literature concentrates on the motion deep learning models which have been trained using aggregated information of the court levels. There is little literature on the preparation of effective and explainable models in a specific design to be applied in lower courts. This paper presents a judicial decision support system based on the NyayaAnumana Legal Judgment Dataset. The data set comprised 7,02,945 preprocessed cases and the binary classification involved the selection of cases in district court cases. TF-IDF feature extraction and pre-processing of the texts were used. The data set was separated into 80 and 20 % training and testing, respectively. The performance of a number of different algorithms, SVM, Random Forest, XGBoost, Gradient Boosting, and TextCNN algorithms, was tested. The highest accuracy was obtained with TF-IDF with Gradient Boosting, which was 83.1 %. The results show that classical machine learning models are more efficient in generalization and practical applicability to predict the outcomes of courts. The TextCNN model was shown to overfit smaller data scales. More research is required to reduce overfitting and improve model generalization.

title: A Soft Everting Pneumatic Deployment Cap for Secure Payload Delivery in Robotic Endoscopy
authors: Vishnu P. Nair, Muhammad A. Khalid, Burak Ozdemir, Alistair Bacchetti, Alexander Brown, Joshua Davy, Yahia Hassanin, Benjamin Calmé, James Avery, Pietro Valdastri, James H. Chandler
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: Payload delivery in endoscopic and robotic interventions has the potential to extend diagnostic and therapeutic capabilities through selective deployment of drugs, micro-robots or other medical devices. However, reliably delivering small and delicate objects through long tool channels associated with flexible instruments or continuum robot platforms remains challenging. Approaches that retain payloads at the tool tip and enable selective deployment represent a promising solution. Previous systems following this strategy have commonly relied on complex magnetically triggered designs or material changes to deliver cargo on demand. These can be slow to trigger or require specific payload designs, limiting versatility. In this work, we propose a fully soft pneumatically driven deployment cap based on a hybrid combination of an eversion skin and an elastomer body. We introduce the concept, develop a model capturing the deployment profile, and present a prototype design of 13.5 mm diameter, 26 mm length, and 2.5 g. We demonstrate repeatable deployment of various objects within the scale range of $2-5 \mathrm{~mm}$, and show sequential delivery of two objects in a simulated endoscopic task in under 45 s.

title: AI-Driven Decision Support Systems (DSS) for Strategic IT Management
authors: Ahmed Alshaflut
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: The rapid pace of technological development has contributed to making the management of Information Technology more complicated. Moreover, the amount of data to be managed has continued to increase and new cyber threats are constantly emerging. These complications arise even as the need to align strategic IT initiatives with an organization's long-range strategy remains paramount. In this context, the current use of traditional decision support systems (DSS) that rely on static rule sets, deterministic modeling approaches, and limited adaptability to changing environments has resulted in a lack of effective predictive (forward looking) capabilities. This paper describes the development of an AI-based DSS for the effective strategic management of information technology by combining machine learning-based predictive analytics and explainable AI (XAI) techniques. Consequently, it can support various strategic IT decision-making domains including (but not limited to): IT investment and portfolio management, risk and security planning, resource allocation, and infrastructure/cloud strategies. The proposed architecture is modular/layered and combines enterprise-based data with predictive models, decision logic, and explanations for transparency. The effectiveness of the proposed DSS was validated using the publicly available Kaggle IT Service Management (ITSM) Data Set, which was evaluated for specific strategic decision indicators derived from aggregated operational data. AI-based decision support systems are superior to traditional decision support systems, achieving greater accuracy and lower uncertainty, while yielding trustworthy and interpretable output. The research shows that including explainable and predictive artificial intelligence in decision support system design can improve the quality, transparency, and effectiveness of strategic IT decision-making within complex business settings.

title: Design and Development of a Multi-Extinguisher Autonomous Fire Fighting Robot
authors: BV Saagarika, Divya E, Gaganashree D, Shreya S, Prof. Jayashree N
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a multi-extinguisher autonomous fire-fighting robot that integrates machine learning, computer vision, and embedded systems for real-time fire detection and suppression. The system utilizes multiple camera modules to continuously monitor the environment and employs a deep learning-based approach to identify fire and smoke conditions. Based on the detection results, the system activates appropriate extinguishing mechanisms such as water spraying, mist generation, or mud application. A lightweight communication framework ensures efficient data exchange between system components, while a monitoring interface provides real-time system updates. The proposed system aims to reduce human involvement in hazardous firefighting environments and improve response efficiency. Experimental observations indicate reliable detection performance and acceptable response time, demonstrating the effectiveness of the system.

title: An Enhanced Model of an Inter-Hospital Data Exchange Platform with Intelligent Referral Recommendations
authors: Salma Elhag, Arwa Alsulami, Waad Alshehri, Khulud Alsubhi
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: This paper presents a conceptual design and simulation study of patient admission and referral processes within a multi-hospital enterprise. It includes an analysis of the current As-Is model that focuses predominantly on paper-based referrals, manual transfer of data from one health institution to another, repeated admissions, delays, errors, and use of resources. On the contrary, our To-Be model uses secure and automated digital referral tokens and AI-based hospital recommendations, cutting out the “non-value-added” activities such as manual re-entry and paper report handling. Both quantitative and qualitative analysis of simulation outcomes demonstrate a predicted downtrend in referral cycle time from days to minutes and a decrease in data entry errors. These advances drive major improvements in efficiency, data accuracy, and patient experience throughout the healthcare enterprise. These developments make Federated AI a potential scalable solution for modernizing medical institutions. To overcome this divide, future research should investigate federated learning and standard models to connect disconnected AI solutions and digital hospital infrastructures.

title: Transformer-Enhanced Constrained Reinforcement Learning for Load Frequency Control in High- Renewable Multi-Area Grids
authors: Huili Ye, Rui Zhang, Shuli Wen, Miao Zhu, Yuchen Zhang
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: The integration of renewable energy resources, particularly grid-forming virtual synchronous machines (VSMs), into power systems introduces complex dynamics that challenge conventional load frequency control (LFC) schemes. This paper proposes a Transformer-based Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) with Lagrangian constraints (TLSAC) for load frequency control in high-renewable multi-area power systems integrating synchronous generators and grid-forming VSMs. The approach leverages a Transformer encoder to capture temporal dependencies from historical observations, enabling coordinated control among heterogeneous generation units. The SAC framework optimizes a stochastic policy while a primal- dual multiplier adaptively enforces operational safety constraints, such as frequency and area control error limits, through primal-dual updates. This mechanism ensures that control actions remain within secure ranges without compromising dynamic performance. Extensive simulations under diverse disturbance scenarios demonstrate that TLSAC outperforms baseline reinforcement learning algorithms by achieving smoother control actions, reduced frequency and voltage deviations, and effective coordination between synchronous and inverter-based resources. Thus, the proposed method offers a promising solution for frequency regulation in future power grids with high shares of renewable energy.

title:
Machine Learning and AI-based Analysis of Economic and Environmental Factors on CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Emissions: A Comparative Study of China, India, and the United Kingdom
authors: Lijo P Thomas, Sateesh Kumar T K, Juby Thomas, Vishnu Achutha Menon
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Trends in Advanced Computing (ICCTAC)
abstract: The purpose of this study is to assess the vital factors that enhances the emission of CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> in India, China and the United Kingdom, with special emphasis on GDP per capita, demographic changes, and environmental factors. The research findings show that GDP per capita is the major factor in China and India, whereas in the United Kingdom, the major factor in the emissions of CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> is the rolling mean of historical values. In all experiments conducted in this research, the results show that the XGBoost model outperforms the Random Forest model in accurately predicting the values. The findings of this research can be used to formulate strategies for the reduction of emissions in different countries in accordance with global climate change policy.

title: Predictive Analysis in Public Transport: A Cloud-Based Intelligent Driver Assistance and Maintenance Framework
authors: V. Sudharsanam, K.T. Renu, R. Poonkuzhali
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Public transport vehicles face harsh, unpredictable conditions, no surprise. Packed roads, rough surfaces, changing passenger weights. All this strain speeds up wear and tear, raising the odds of breakdowns, delays, even accidents. Existing maintenance tools? Clunky. They lean on pricey custom telematics gear, built-in sensors, closed systems. Tough to install, harder to upgrade older buses and trams. Enter a smarter way. This paper offers a cloud-based aid for drivers and upkeep, predicting issues without fancy IoT gadgets. It crunches past driving data, routine stats, and light real-time inputs. Spots oddities in speed, distance, driver habits. Sends early warnings, then taps Google Maps to suggest trusted nearby garages-fast. A flexible web and mobile dashboard shows vital stats, past alerts, and smart repair tips based on routes. Tests using fake fleet info hit 92.4% accuracy spotting problems, under 3 seconds to flag them, and just 0.41 km off on repair shop locations. Fewer sudden failures, better calls on the go. That means safer rides, steadier service, lower costs. All pushing toward the UN's global goals: sturdy cities, tough infrastructure.

title: Adaptive and Multi-object Grasping via Deformable Origami Modules
authors: Peiyi Wang, Paul A. M. Lefeuvre, Shangwei Zou, Zhenwei Ni, Daniela Rus, Cecilia Laschi
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: Soft robotics grippers have shown great promise in handling fragile and geometrically complex objects, as a less complex alternative to sensor-heavy conventional gripper systems. In this work, we present a multi-finger hybrid gripper featuring passively deformable origami modules that generate constant force and torque output. All fingers are driven by a 1-DOF actuator mechanism, and equipped with parallel origami modules enabling passive shape adaptability and stable grasping force without active sensing or feedback control. More importantly, we demonstrate an interesting capability in simultaneous multi-object grasping, which allows stacked objects of varied shapes and sizes to be picked, transported and placed independently, significantly improving manipulation efficiency compared to single-object grasping. These results highlight the potential of origami-based compliant structures as scalable modules for adaptive, stable and efficient multiobject manipulation in domestic and industrial pick-and-place scenarios.

title: SenseLess: Minimal Vision, Maximum Insight for Smart Homes
authors: Norah Albazzai, Omer Rana, Charith Perera
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Communications (PerCom)
abstract: We present SenseLess, a hybrid anomaly detection framework for smart homes that, during the training phase, automatically labels images without manual annotation by combining sensor-guided detection, self-supervised visual clustering, and unsupervised multi-sensor delay estimation for precise alignment. During operation, the system relies primarily on non-vision sensors and activates a confidence-aware vision model only under low-confidence, thereby preserving privacy while maintaining adaptability. Evaluated in real home monitoring, SenseLess achieved an average label coverage of 97.65% with 94.9% accuracy and reduced vision usage to less than 4% of wall-clock operating time. Calibration mechanisms and minimal configuration requirements support scalability and deployment across diverse residential environments.

title: Historical Scenario Probability Modeling and SAO-DBSCAN-Based Scenario Reduction for Wind Power Systems
authors: Jiawei Gao, Zhaobin Du, Guoduan Zhong, Zhi He, Xinyu Chang, Renqi Yang, Di Cai, Zirui Huang
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: The growing integration of wind power systems introduces stochastic volatility to modern power grids, posing significant challenges to system reliability and security. While multi-dimensional probabilistic analysis quantifies these uncertainties, multiple operating scenarios increase the computational cost of subsequent security assessment. Therefore, accurate historical scenario probability modeling and efficient scenario reduction methods are urgently needed. This paper proposes a data-driven multi-dimensional scenario generation and reduction framework. First, this study synthesizes the Weibull distribution, Kernel Density Estimation, and Copula frameworks to effectively model multidimensional nonlinear dependencies among wind speed and power, enabling high-fidelity conditional resampling. To reduce the computational burden of subsequent scenario-based analysis, an adaptive SAO-DBSCAN clustering method is proposed. Snow Ablation Optimizer (SAO) algorithm dynamically optimizes DBSCAN parameters to extract representative routine scenarios while strictly preserving extremely high-risk boundary states. Simulation results validate that this approach significantly reduces scenario scale while maintaining spatial distribution and statistical consistency, providing a reliable basis for enhancing system resilience.

title: RISs as Enablers for Adaptive and Modular Space Communication Architectures
authors: Ivan Iudice, Domenico Pascarella, Sonia Zappia, Vincenzo Galdi
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: The increasing demand for agile, energy-efficient, and software-defined space communication systems has brought intelligent electromagnetic surfaces into the spotlight as key enabling technologies. This paper explores the role of reconfigurable and intelligent metasurfaces in shaping next-generation space architectures. By passively manipulating wavefronts through programmable sub-wavelength elements, metasurfaces can enhance link quality, provide dynamic coverage steering, and reduce onboard processing and energy demands-particularly beneficial in small satellite and distributed Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) constellations. We discuss architectural scenarios where metasurfaces support modular and adaptive connectivity in space-to-space and space-to-ground systems. Furthermore, we explore how metasurfaces, when controlled via Artificial Intelligence (AI) or embedded logic, contribute to the vision of interoperable, software-defined, and updateable space infrastructures. The paper concludes with a roadmap for integrating intelligent metasurfaces into space digital twins, onboard optimization loops, and standardized interfaces for future cognitive space systems.

title: Proactive Vehicle Safety and Driver Assistance System
authors: V P Kalaiyarasi, Sajithaa R, Pooja Sri B
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: We are proposing a Proactive Vehicle Safety and Driver Assistance System that combines IoT based sensing with embedded intelligence and cloud based analytics in order to provide the ability to continuously prevent road accidents and driver related risks in real-time. By combining the capabilities of GPS tracked speed and location, ignition controlled alcohol detection, seatbelt compliance monitoring, driver health/drowsiness analysis using physiological and behavioral characteristics, and real-time engine diagnostics into one single operating framework, our system is able to detect unsafe driving conditions early and automatically take safety actions. The Proactive Vehicle Safety & Driver Assistance System is capable of processing sensor input locally and transmitting validated information to the cloud for real-time data analysis and visualization. Additionally, the Proactive Vehicle Safety & Driver Assistance System includes an emergency mode that allows drivers to use voice commands to activate emergency alerts hands free, enabling live GPS coordinates and critical event information to be automatically transmitted via the cloud to registered contacts and authorities in the event of an emergency. Continuous monitoring of engine parameters including temperature and oil pressure enables the Proactive Vehicle Safety & Driver Assistance System to provide predictive maintenance for enhanced vehicle reliability and operational safety. Overall, the Proactive Vehicle Safety & Driver Assistance System represents a complete, integrated architecture that presents a proactive, scalable, and intelligent safety solution for both personal vehicles and commercial fleets, as well as emphasizing prevention, rapid response, and data driven decision making.

title: Enhancing CNN Resilience: A Dual-Layer Defense Framework Integrating Neural-Cleanse and JPEG Compression
authors: Mutaz Abdel Wahed, Basem Zughuol, Maen Alzubi, Murad Bashabsheh
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: The widespread deployment of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) across critical domains has raised growing security concerns, particularly regarding their vulnerability to backdoor attacks and adversarial perturbations. Backdoor attacks embed hidden trigger patterns during training, causing targeted misclassification when activated, while adversarial attacks introduce subtle, imperceptible perturbations at inference time to mislead model predictions. This study proposes a dual-layer defense framework that integrates NeuralCleanse (NC) during the training phase with JPEG-Compression (JC) during deployment. Neural-Cleanse is employed to identify and neutralize potential backdoor triggers through trigger reverse-engineering and anomaly-based detection, ensuring model integrity prior to deployment. Complementarily, JPEGCompression acts as a preprocessing filter that suppresses highfrequency adversarial noise, thereby reducing inference-time attack rates. The framework is evaluated using CNN models trained on the CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets under FGSM and PGD adversarial attacks. Experimental results demonstrate that Neural-Cleanse significantly improves backdoor robustness by suppressing trigger-conditioned misclassification, while JPEG Compression enhances adversarial robustness by filtering highfrequency perturbations. The combined NC+JC configuration achieves the highest overall resilience while maintaining competitive clean accuracy and acceptable computational overhead.

title: Scope-3 Intelligence: A Business Intelligence System for Carbon Accounting and Sustainable Supply Chains
authors: Maen Alzubi, Murad Bashabsheh, Basem Zughoul, Mutaz Abdel Wahed
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: This study introduces Scope-3 Intelligence, a datadriven BI framework that integrates three complementary datasets: (1) a logistics dataset of 2,000 shipments including weight, distance, cost, and transit attributes; (2) NAICS-based emission factors providing supplier-related carbon intensities; and (3) macroeconomic indicators (GDP, energy indices, population growth) that contextualize emission forecasting. Results demonstrated that logistics operations generated 15,070 kg CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>e across all shipments, with an average of 7.5 kg CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>e per shipment and highly skewed outliers exceeding 1,100 kg CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>e. Supplierrelated emissions dominated, with manufacturing spend contributing 0.42 kg CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>e per USD, leading to more than 770,000 kg CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>e in aggregated supplier Scope-3 impacts. Forecasting experiments compared a baseline model (logistics + supplier factors) with an enriched model incorporating macroeconomic variables. The results showed comparable performance (NRMSE = 0.116 in both cases), indicating that simple linear regression is insufficient to capture the nonlinear relationship between emissions and macroeconomic drivers. Optimization results indicated that 10–12% cost savings could be achieved simultaneously with 15–18% emission reductions, with only marginal delays in transit times, highlighting that sustainability and efficiency are not mutually exclusive. The contributions of this study are threefold: validation of Scope-3 accounting through multi-source real datasets, integration of emissions as a core BI metric alongside cost and service, and prescriptive optimization dashboards that align regulatory compliance with competitive advantage. This research demonstrates how BI systems can evolve from descriptive analytics to prescriptive intelligence, supporting carbon-neutral decision-making in global supply chains.

title: Machine-Learning-Driven Operational Optimisation of Electrolyser-Based Hydrogen Production Using a Physics-Informed Digital Twin for UK Industrial Clusters
authors: Muhammad Hamza Tariq, Irfan Ahmed
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: The article investigates the optimization of the process of producing hydrogen by electrolyser through machine learning (ML) and physics-informed digital twin (DT) technologies to increase efficiency and decrease costs in industrial sectors in the United Kingdom. The study utilizes simulation-based assessment to predict the performance of the system and optimize the working parameters through a digital twin that replicates the behavior of the electrolyser in real-time and allows adapting optimization. The methodology involves collecting a PEM the electrolyser system's operating parameters and using machine learning models to forecast system performance metrics like energy consumption and hydrogen production rate. A digital twin with physics knowledge is created to mimic the behavior of the electrolyser under various operating circumstances. Through simulation experiments, the proposed MLDT framework is evaluated by comparing optimized operation with a conventional baseline system. The system was tested within the framework of the UK industrial clusters, and the specific were decarbonization and the energy efficiency. The main results based on simulations are the decrease in energy usage by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15-20 \%$</tex>, downtime and maintenance reduction by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25-35 \%$</tex>, and better operational reliability. The suggested optimization scheme states strong possibilities to promote the development of sustainable hydrogen production and sustainability of the UK net-zero objectives.

title: Predicting Postpartum Depression Using Questionnaire -Based Data and Machine Learning
authors: Sreeji S, Shirley C P
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Postpartum Depression: Postpartum depression or PPD is one of the common mental illnesses amongst women who have recently given birth to their children. Nevertheless, PPD may cause very dire consequences for the mother and the baby if it is not distinguished at an early age. The last several years have seen the development of the application of machine learning methods to the detection of mental health conditions at the earlier stages. The research paper presents a simple and effective machine learning model targeted at the prediction of postpartum depression using the responses of mothers to the questions in the questionnaire. Simple and interpretable classification models, including Logistic Regression, SVM, the Random Forest technique, and so on, are created. The data is cleaned, and the attributes were chosen in the process of separating mothers into depressed and non-depressed categories. The statistical data obtained in the experiment indicate that the accuracy and F1 measure of the Random Forest method are improved compared to the other models. The project can be considered as a starting point of the research based on larger datasets to predict postpartum depression.

title: Comparative Analysis of Zero-Shot Voice Cloning for Digital Sibling AI
authors: Hanif Nugroho Jati, Ridi Ferdiana, Risanuri Hidayat
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: Voice cloning technology is a critical component of the Digital Sibling AI (DiSiAI) platform, which aims to create personalized voice and lifelike digital representations of individuals. This study addresses the challenge of selecting an optimal voice cloning model through a proposed two-stage evaluation framework. The first stage employs the MCDM-TOPSIS method to rank models based on academic impact and community engagement, shortlisting six models. The second stage involves empirical testing to measure computational efficiency (Real-Time Factor) and synthesis quality (Word Error Rate, Speaker Similarity, and Naturalness). The results reveal a distinct trade-off between linguistic precision and acoustic fidelity; XTTS-V2 emerged as the most credible candidate with a TOPSIS score of 0.857, providing the fastest real-time performance (RTF: 0.543), making it ideal for latency-sensitive applications. However, CosyVoice 2 achieved the highest voice cloning quality, with the highest speaker similarity (SIM-o: 0.963) and naturalness validated by both objective (UTMOS: 4.429) and human evaluations (MOS: 3.933), despite requiring more computational resources (RTF: 1.089). This research establishes a methodological foundation for selecting voice cloning models based on specific application priorities. The empirical evaluation concludes that while XTTS-V2 emerges as the optimal model for operational responsiveness and inference stability due to its best real-time factor, CosyVoice 2 is the preferred candidate for maximizing vocal identity and personality by providing a more authentic auditory experience through the highest speaker similarity and perceived naturalness. Accordingly, these findings provide a significant contribution to the development of a more immersive DiSiAI Platform.

title: OLTL: An Optimization Extension of Linear Temporal Logic
authors: Mohammad Mehdi Pourhashem Kallehbasti, Matteo Rossi (SI GE)
publish: Formal Aspects of Computing
