title: Highlights of the IMAP Integration and Test Campaign
authors: Justin Hahn, Sarah Bucior, Rosanna Smith, Evan Smith, Musad Haque
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: NASA's Interstellar Mapping and Acceleration Probe (IMAP) mission will provide extensive new observations of the inner and outer heliosphere and investigate some of the most important questions in heliophysics to date. Integration and Test (I&T) of the IMAP spacecraft was led by the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory (APL), with extensive support and involvement from various institutions participating in the mission. This paper describes the highlights of system I&T, including hardware assembly and integration, system testing, environmental testing, and the launch campaign. This process began with an initial I&T plan developed early in the mission, with detailed planning and procedure development taking place as the spacecraft design matured and subsystems and instruments were built and tested. Early I&T went according to plan, including spacecraft subsystem integrations, initial system testing, and integration of the first few instruments. However, at that point several major changes to the planned I&T flow were required due to facility conflicts and instrument development schedule challenges, but these were successfully navigated by the team. Once all components were integrated and baseline testing completed, including functionality testing of all components, Mass Properties, EMC testing, Phasing testing, and RF compatibility testing, the spacecraft proceeded through dynamics testing, including Acoustic, Vibration, and Shock testing. This was followed by Thermal Vacuum testing, including Thermal Balance and Thermal Cycling. Post-environmental testing was performed at the launch site, including repeats of many of the tests performed as part of the baseline testing. Finally, pre-launch preparations and launch vehicle combined operations culminated in the successful launch and initial post-launch operations.

title: Data-Centric Dataset Characterization for Tabular Learning Using Uniqueness Domains Knowledge Profiling
authors: Khaled Bahnasy, Mohamed Hamdy, Doaa Hegazy, Mohamed Dahab
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: While dataset learning complexity has traditionally been measured using statistical meta-features or by the predictive ability of an empirical model, the volume of explicitly defined, deterministic knowledge inside a dataset has usually remained unmeasured. In this paper, a knowledge-based method is presented to evaluate dataset learning complexity using the Uniqueness Domain, defined as the set of all <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 \%}$</tex> accurate class association rules of the dataset. The UniqAR algorithm is used to extract the uniqueness domains of twelve tabular datasets [1]. Three knowledge-volume metrics are introduced: Absolute Knowledge Volume <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(A K V)$</tex>, Case-Normalized Knowledge Volume (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C K V$</tex>), and Feature-Value-Normalized Knowledge Volume <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(F K V)$</tex>. In addition, a composite index, the Knowledge Challenge Index <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(K C I)$</tex>, is proposed to relate extraction effort to extracted deterministic knowledge. It is shown that the number of deterministic symbolic patterns per unit information can vary by up to four orders of magnitude across datasets, and that the size of the uniqueness domain is driven mainly by feature-value diversity, rather than by the number of rows. Based on these observations, a dataset “Complexity Profile” is constructed, which can distinguish datasets that are transparent with respect to their logical structures from datasets that are relatively opaque.

title: The Unpredictability of the Traffic Sources Model on Instagram Content Using Tree-Based Models Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Hujjatullah Fazlurrahman, Bima Setyo Nugroho, Restu Eri Adinata, Novita Aprilia, Devangga Putra Adhitya Pratama, Thusy Tiara Saraswati, Reza Ayu Palupi, Noris Bagus Mulyo, Amirusholihin A.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: This study evaluates the effectiveness of tree-based machine learning models for classifying dominant traffic sources of Instagram content using 29,999 posts characterized by engagement, exposure, and structural content metrics. Decision Tree, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting algorithms were evaluated against random and majority baselines. Hyperparameter tuning and ablation experiments were conducted to examine model robustness and feature contribution. All models achieved accuracy levels near the theoretical random probability (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.167$</tex>), with only marginal improvement. Ablation results indicate that neither engagement metrics nor structural content attributes substantially improve predictive performance. The findings suggest that traffic source dominance is structurally unpredictable when relying solely on standard Instagram analytics variables and is likely influenced by latent platform mechanisms. This study emphasizes the importance of benchmark baselines, dataset transparency, and cautious interpretation in social media predictive modeling.

title: Fully Distributed DMPC–UKF Framework for Cyber-Resilient Control of Islanded AC Microgrids
authors: Saima Ali, Laiq Khan, Amin Mahmoudi
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: This research presents a fully distributed Model Predictive Control (DMPC) approach for AC microgrids, coupled with a residual-based Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF) for speedy anomaly and cyber-attack detection. Each distributed generator (DG) makes its own control decision based on the shared state information through a consensus algorithm. It offers voltage and frequency restoration at the secondary level and power sharing and generation cost improvement at the tertiary level. UKF accurately tracks nonlinear system states, thus increasing the reliability and resilience of the system. MATLAB/Simulink simulations validate that the proposed control strategy maintains stable and secure operation of microgrids under normal and cyber-attack conditions.

title: Enhanced Phishing Detection Using Gradient Boosting Classifier: A Machine Learning Approach
authors: Vandana Ahuja, Nidhi Dua, Manoj S, K Dinesh Kumar, D Justin Xavier, Nafeez Ahmed L
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: This paper deals with the increasing risk of phishing attacks through the suggestion of a machine learning-based solution to recognize fraudulent URLs with the help of the Gradient Boosting (GB) classifier. As online services grow quickly, criminals are also using Internet scams much more often to trick users and steal their personal data. To address this question, a dataset of 11,055 cases of URL samples having 31 cases of discriminating features was used in order to categorize websites as phishing or legitimate. MinMax normalization was used to preprocess the dataset to improve the model performance and stability. The proposed model was tested with the conventional performance measurements, such as accuracy, precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score. The experiments illustrate that Gradient Boosting classifier performs best with the accuracy of 97.4 which is much higher than that of other classifiers including the Random Forest, Decision Tree, Logistic Regression and Naive Bayes. Moreover, a phishing prevention system Phish Guard AI was created based on real-time phishing detection, and within a web application built on the Flask framework, which enables users to confirm the safety of the URL in real time. The findings indicate that ensemble learning methods are effective in enhancing the accuracy of phishing detection and false positives. This piece of work helps to improve cybersecurity and safety of users against the changing phishing threats.

title: Europa Clipper Cruise Phase: From Integration and Test to Early Operations
authors: Andres F. Rivera, Erisa Stilley, Thaddeus M. Para, May Chong Chan
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: Jupiter's icy moon Europa is a prime target in our exploration of potentially habitable worlds beyond Earth. The Europa Clipper mission will characterize Europa's habitability as the first step in the search for potential life at the Jovian moon by conducting approximately four dozen flybys. Europa Clipper launched on October 14, 2024, aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy, beginning its 5.5 -year journey to the Jovian system. This paper describes the mission's transition from the implementation phase—highlighting the execution of system-level Verification and Validationin—to launch and early cruise operations. It covers key milestones such as the flight system characterization campaign, the first low data rate operational period, the first gravity assist maneuver at Mars, and the initiation of Earth-pointed inner cruise operations. The paper focuses on the major challenges during planning and implementation, the strategies adopted to address them, and the lessons learned to inform future deep space missions.

title: Hierarchical Energy Flow Coordination for Dual-Motor All-Electric Helicopter Propulsion Under Dynamic Flight Conditions
authors: Yuhang Yang, Yaozu Lai, Wang Huaizhi
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: All-electric helicopters require highly coordinated energy management due to the coupled operation of main and tail rotor propulsion units under rapidly varying flight conditions. This paper proposes a hierarchical energy flow coordination strategy for dual-motor all-electric helicopter propulsion systems. The proposed framework consists of a fast inner-loop torque control layer, a DC bus stabilization layer, and an upper-level energy optimization layer that dynamically redistributes power between propulsion units and the energy storage system. A bidirectional power flow mechanism is developed to enable regenerative energy reutilization during transient maneuvers and rapid load variations. The coordination strategy aims to minimize DC bus voltage fluctuation, reduce battery stress, and improve transient response under dynamic flight profiles. To validate the proposed approach, a 160 kW-class dual-dynamometer experimental platform is constructed to emulate realistic helicopter operating conditions. Experimental results demonstrate enhanced DC bus stability, improved power redistribution efficiency, and superior transient performance compared with conventional independent motor control schemes. The proposed hierarchical coordination method provides a scalable and experimentally validated solution for high-power electric vertical flight propulsion systems.

title: Soft-Shell Crab-Inspired Inflatable Limbs Formed from a Continuous Tube
authors: Seita Yamaguchi, Ryuma Niiyama
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: Soft skeletal systems allow soft robots to remain compliant while maintaining structural stability and producing endpoint forces. Inspired by the soft-shell stage of crabs, where multi-jointed legs originate as continuous tubular forms before hardening, we explore the formation of articulated limb geometries from a single continuous inflatable tube. We propose a design principle that forms hinge joints by adding mechanical constraints to a dual-layer inflatable tube. Joint positions, orientations, and link lengths can be arranged freely, unlike conventional inflatable joint designs that require complex fabrication and result in fixed joint layouts. Tendon-driven actuation is implemented using wire-reel mechanisms analogous to musculoskeletal systems. The prototype demonstrations confirm that a variety of joint configurations can be realized. Experiments characterize the anisotropic stiffness of the hinge joints and show that internal pressure enables controllable adjustment of stiffness, allowing both structural support and foldability. We further demonstrate multi-joint tendon-driven motion in a single leg and estimate endpoint payload and bodysupport capability. This approach provides a soft skeletal system that is simple to fabricate yet mechanically functional, offering a promising foundation for scalable multi-jointed and multilegged soft robots.

title: A Hybrid CNN-CCT Based Road Surface Anomaly Detection System for Real-Time Pothole Identification: A Comparative Study with Deep Learning Models
authors: Maadhava Kumar L, Mohammed Samiq Khan R, Suthi G, Rajan C
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: The paper focuses on detecting potholes on roads using deep learning techniques to support safer and smarter road monitoring. It is designed to identify damaged road surfaces automatically through input images and video streams. A CCT-90 (Compact Convolutional Transformer) based on the Convolutionally Neural Network is implemented to classify the road surface precisely. The system classifies between a smooth surface and a pothole surface. The implemented solution is a streamlit application that allows users to upload media files and receive automated analysis. It gives immediate detection results for a quick evaluation of road safety condition. Two classes, smooth road and pothole road are used in the training dataset. Images and video data are collected to improve the reliability of pothole detection. This helps to enhance the model's ability to identify real-world road damages. The approach does not depend on additional expensive sensors or specialized hardware. It relies only on visual data processing for detecting surface deterioration. The method aims to make road inspection more efficient and accessible. It provides a scalable detection mechanism that can be used by authorities and monitoring systems. Automating the detection process can reduce the manual inspection effort. The system supports continuous infrastructure monitoring for safety improvements. Its lightweight deployment ensures easy use in practical environments. This solution contributes to better decisionmaking in the maintenance of roads. It improves transportation safety by alerting about hazardous road segments. The model allows for a faster response to surface damage and prevents accidents. The purpose is to promote intelligent road monitoring based on deep learning.

title: AI-Driven Quantum Entanglement Classification: A Novel Noise-Robust Approach
authors: Tahmina Akter Anondi
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: This paper introduces a new method to identify quantum entanglement in diverse quantum states by leveraging artificial intelligence. We establish a robust classifier by training a machine learning model on a synthetically generated dataset, enabling accurate differentiation of entangled states even amidst significant noise. The technique employs classical features derived from quantum state measurements, optimizing for sample efficiency. Numerical evaluations demonstrate this approach achieves high accuracy in classifying entangled multi-qubit states under various noisy environments.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Based Transient Voltage Stability Emergency Control
authors: Weigang Jin, Tao Lin, Jiayi Wang, Jun Li, Zhengyang Lin, Chen Li, Jiawei Zhang
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: In novel power systems at high-voltage direct current (HVDC) receiving ends, the integration of HVDC and renewable energy power sources reduces synchronous machine operation, tightens dynamic reactive power availability, and significantly increases transient voltage instability risks. Addressing transient voltage stability challenges, this paper proposes emergency control method through HVDC modulation, i.e. utilizing HVDC converter station as emergency reactive power sources for HVDC receiving-end power grid. Specifically, to efficiently and rapidly obtain transient voltage stability emergency control strategy which is adaptable to grid topology change, this paper modifies the network architecture of Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) model by incorporating Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN), which results in the GCNDDPG hybrid DRL model. Finally, the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method are validated through a modified IEEE 14-node benchmark.

title: Design and Critical Evaluation of a Postmodern-Aware Chatbot Using Retrieval-Augmented Generation and Discourse Metrics
authors: Feri Agustian Sukarno, Puguh Hiskiawan, Jason Jesse Joel Polii, Marco Stefanus Indarwan, Theresia Puspa Wijayanti
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have expanded the role of chatbots from task-oriented assistants to agents participating in complex social discourse. Conventional evaluation frameworks, however, remain largely centered on accuracy and task completion, limiting their ability to assess behavior in pluralistic and non-convergent conversational settings. This study presents the design and empirical evaluation of a postmodern-aware chatbot built on a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture with multi-persona interaction design. Four personas; modernist, postmodernist, sociologist, and technologist; engage in structured debate sessions across three thematic topics. Discourse principles are operationalized into measurable indicators, including Polarization Index, Persona Consistency Score, Keyword Density, and Turn Length. Experimental results demonstrate sustained semantic divergence, stable epistemic differentiation among personas, and context-sensitive lexical and structural variation. The findings indicate that discourse-level metrics provide a reproducible framework for analyzing ideological contrast and non-convergent dynamics in chatbot-mediated interaction. By integrating discourse theory with data-driven evaluation, this work contributes a scalable alternative to accuracy-centered benchmarks for assessing AI systems in socially complex environments.

title: VigiGuard: An AI-Driven Real-Time Surveillance System for Retail Theft Prevention
authors: Mohammed A. Banafe, Mustafa M. Aleid, Shaden K. Althagafi, Basil H. Alharbi, Sara E. Alhilali, Bushra A. Al-Anesi, Soha A. Bahanshal
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: This paper presents VigiGuard, an intelligent surveillance system designed to address the persistent challenge of shoplifting in retail environments which poses a significant threat to business profitability, operational effectiveness, security and other parties like government, police, courts and society at large. The proposed system leverages a deep learning architecture combining Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to analyze real-time video streams from IP cameras. It extracts both spatial and temporal features to distinguish between normal customer behavior and subtle shoplifting actions. Upon detection of a potential theft, the system automatically generates an alert, saves a clipped video sequence as evidence, and notifies store management via a secure web dashboard. The proposed solution integrates critical cybersecurity measures, including data encryption and secure access protocols, to protect sensitive footage. Our development methodology encompasses data collection and annotation, model training, and the implementation of a full-stack web application for system management. VigiGuard aims to enhance monitoring efficiency, reduce reliance on manual observation, and provide a scalable, user-friendly tool for loss prevention.

title: Dynamic Client Selector: A Double Deep Q-Learning Framework for Efficient Federated Learning
authors: Sangeen Khan, Md Monjurul Karim, Muhammad Muzammal, Qiang Qu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Communications (PerCom)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) is a method for training a shared machine learning model without transferring raw data to a central server. This approach protects privacy while enabling collaborative learning. It is difficult to integrate FL with the Internet of Things (IoT). The computing power, communication capabilities, and network dependability of devices vary, as may the quality of the data they generate. We thus need reliable client selection techniques that choose the best devices to be included in the training process. This paper presents DCS, a client selection approach in FL using Double Deep Q-Learning (DDQL) that optimizes client choices based on factors such as trust, latency, and performance. Key innovations include a balanced scoring mechanism, utilizing a decay factor to prioritize recent updates, and incremental principal component analysis (PCA) for dimensionality reduction, ensuring only quality updates are integrated. Additionally, the Approximate Nearest Neighbor (ANN) search method is used for outlier detection, filtering out unreliable clients to enhance global model convergence and reduce computational costs. Experimental results demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed method on two datasets, MNIST and CIFAR-10, in heterogeneous settings. On the MNIST dataset, the proposed approach achieved a final accuracy of 99.06%, surpassing existing methods named Favor, FLASH-RL, and FedAvg. Furthermore, on the Fashion-MNIST (IID), DCS achieves up to 89.5% test accuracy while converging in approximately 110–115 rounds.

title: MentalCare: Contrastive Disentanglement and Positive Transfer for Psychiatric Disorder Detection with a Wristband–Smartphone System
authors: Yufei Zhang, Wenting Kuang, Zite Huang, Changhe Fan, Yongpan Zou
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Communications (PerCom)
abstract: Low-cost wrist-worn sensing for scalable, low-burden assessment of mental-health symptoms is a central topic in mobile health. In this paper, we propose MentalCare, a multi-class psychiatric disorder (PD) detection system that integrates ubiquitous multi-source sensors, including Pulse sensor, LMT70, Grove GSR, and Max30102. These sensors capture physiological signals such as photoplethysmography (PPG), skin temperature (SKT), galvanic skin response (GSR), and blood oxygen (OXY). To address physiological heterogeneity among individuals, we design a contrastive disentanglement with a positive transfer framework (CDPT), which consists of a stacked convolutional neural network module (SCNN), a cross-modal attention interaction module (CMAI), a subject-level contrastive disentanglement module (SCD), and an adaptive feature selector module (AFS). In particular, the SCD module employs supervised contrastive learning to promote the separation of heterogeneous features among different subjects in the latent subspace. Furthermore, the proposed AFS module selects features from the abundant source-domain heterogeneous representations that are most similar to the target domain, thereby facilitating feature alignment and enhancing detection performance. We collect a multi-class PD dataset comprising 74 participants diagnosed with depression, bipolar disorder, anxiety, or verified as healthy controls. The experiment result demonstrates that CDPT achieves 83.96% overall accuracy under the leave-one-subject-out cross-validation protocol, significantly outperforming existing state-of-the-art methods. At the system level, we further validate the effectiveness and rationality of the proposed system when deployed on edge devices.

title: Reconfigurable Compensation Topology for Efficient Wireless Energy Conversion in Electric Vehicle CCCV Charging with Zero Voltage Switching
authors: Liangxi He, Wenkai Ye, Yalun Li, Hewu Wang
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: This paper proposes a reconfigurable compensation topology using only passive inductors and capacitors to achieve efficient constant current and constant voltage charging for electric vehicles through wireless power transfer. The core contribution employs two AC switches to reconfigure the transmitter matching network, enabling smooth mode transitions between constant current and constant voltage without complex control or frequency variation. The method uses first harmonic approximation and Kirchhoff’s laws to derive resonant conditions that ensure load-independent output current and voltage, zero phase angle for unity power factor, and zero voltage switching across the full load range. Compared with conventional approaches—such as secondary-side DC-DC converters that introduce extra losses, variable-frequency techniques prone to bifurcation, or series-series and double-sided LCC topologies that require complex couplers or fail zero phase angle in constant voltage mode—this passive solution offers simpler implementation, reduced cost, and improved reliability. Simulations at 85 kHz with DD coils confirm stable constant current output of 15.7 A, constant voltage output of 420 V, reliable zero voltage switching, and robust response under load steps from 6 kW to 3.2 kW. The topology positions electric vehicles as mobile energy storage units supporting vehicle-to-grid interactions, with potential to enable bidirectional energy exchange, enhance grid stability, facilitate renewable integration, and promote electric vehicle adoption through cost-effective, resilient energy conversion solutions.

title: Visual SLAM with DEM Anchoring for Long-Range Lunar Surface Navigation
authors: Adam Dai, Guillem Casadesus Vila, Grace Gao
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: Future lunar missions will require autonomous rovers capable of traversing tens of kilometers across challenging terrain while maintaining accurate localization and producing globally consistent maps. However, the absence of global positioning systems, extreme illumination, and low-texture regolith make long-range navigation on the Moon particularly difficult, as visual-inertial odometry pipelines accumulate drift over extended traverses. To address this challenge, we present a stereo visual simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) system that integrates learned feature detection and matching with global constraints from digital elevation models (DEMs). Our front-end employs learning-based feature extraction and matching to achieve robustness to illumination extremes and repetitive terrain, while the back-end incorporates DEM-derived height and surface-normal factors into a pose graph, providing absolute surface constraints that mitigate long-term drift. We validate our approach using both simulated lunar traverse data generated in Unreal Engine and real Moon/Mars analog data collected from Mt. Etna. Results demonstrate that DEM anchoring consistently reduces absolute trajectory error compared to baseline SLAM methods, lowering drift in long-range navigation even in repetitive or visually aliased terrain.

title: Autismsense: Leveraging Cnns for Early Visual Diagnosis
authors: Vasumathi A, Hiruthick B S, Indeeswaran C, Kabilan M, Karthick V
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Autism Spectrum disease (ASD) is a complicated neurodevelopmental disease that impacts social interactions, behavioral patterns, or emotional displays. The conventional approach to diagnosing ASD is highly dependent on clinical observation and tends to be subjective and time-consuming. In this regard, this study attempts to construct an automated ASD detection system utilizing deep learning techniques according to the VGG16 model and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). The autism dataset employed in this research includes essential behavioral symptoms, including gender, age, jaundice history, family background of ASD, and results of the screening test. The proposed model improves the classification accuracy of ASD using an optimized preprocessing technique, which involves data cleaning, normalization, augmentation, and efficient feature learning. The proposed system is computationally efficient, allowing for fast convergence and high accuracy even with a small amount of data. The experimental outcomes show that the system has stable training performance, improved generalization capability, and is able to distinguish well between ASD and non-ASD classes.

title: Maximizing van der Waals Contact in Gecko-Inspired Grippers for Complex Object Geometries via Compliant Backing
authors: James Farmer, Ibragim Atadjanov, Arsen Abdulali
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: Gecko-inspired adhesives exploit van der Waals (vdW) forces for high-adhesion, low-preload grips, but their performance is often limited to ideal surfaces. System-level success depends on a macro-micro coupling between the compliant backing (macro) and the adhesive microstructure (micro). We hypothesize that compliant backings increase macro-conformity, engaging a larger real vdW contact area. To quantify the separate contributions of compliance and adhesion, we evaluate a $2 \times 2$ design, crossing pad type (silicone vs. gecko) with backing stiffness (rigid PLA vs. compliant TPU). Experiments confirmed this coupling: the Gecko+TPU configuration required significantly less normal force on curved objects and succeeded where friction-only grippers slipped. A reducedorder model incorporating non-linear seating dynamics was developed, which validates the “saturated” linear regime of our experiments and predicts a critical performance crossover on rough surfaces. Our results quantify the benefits of compliance, yielding a clear design guideline: the Gecko+TPU pairing is most efficient, but its advantage is limited to smooth or mildly curved objects.

title: Non-invasive Lithium Battery Diagnosis by Magnetic Field Sensing and Machine Learning Assisted Recognition
authors: Qiqi Chen, Keyu Duan, Wenchao Miao, Xuecheng Sun
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of renewable energy technologies, lithium-ion batteries are increasingly adopted in electric vehicles and energy storage systems. However, battery faults may lead to severe safety incidents, even fire and explosion hazards. In this paper, a non-invasive diagnosis method for lithium-ion batteries, integrating magnetic-field sensing with machine-learning-assisted recognition, is proposed. The magnetic characteristics of cylindrical and pouch-type lithium-ion batteries are analyzed to elucidate the underlying principles of magnetic field generation. A tunnel magnetoresistive sensor is employed to measure the magnetic field distributions of three representative battery states—healthy, aged, and swollen. Since the magnetic field images of batteries under different conditions exhibit significant variations, machine learning algorithms are adopted to extract discriminative features and achieve accurate state identification. The proposed non-invasive, low-cost, and effective method is a promising candidate for diagnosing faults in lithium-ion batteries during quality inspection and recycling processes for electric vehicles and energy storage systems. It can contribute to the reliability of the next generation of power systems.

title: Solar Powered LoRa Mesh IoT System for Real Time Forest Fire Prediction, Detection and Alerting System
authors: P. Kokila, Punithavathi B, Prisy H, Prasanna Kumar R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: A forest fire is one of the most severe environmental disasters as it can result in deaths, destruction, climate change, and loss of biodiversity. To address these concerns, this study proposes a solar powered LoRa mesh Internet of things (IoT) system of real time forest fire monitoring, forecasting and warning. The proposed system will have a low-power IoT sensor node network that has temperature, humidity, smoke, and gas sensors, to constantly monitor environmental changes in forest environments. The nodes run continuously and run on solar panels, therefore, working even in isolated or inaccessible places. The gathered data is sent via a LoRa (Long Range) mesh network that enables reliable and long passive transfer on a distance. Data collected at the central gateway is analyzed in order to detect any unusual conditions that may indicate potential fire hazards. The device automatically sends alerts to the local coordinating centers and forest authorities (including fire service responders) when the conditions are identified to improve response time and reduce damage. The sensor would also be able to sustain the use of energy and increase the reliability, accuracy, and efficiency of communication in the forest ecosystem. The system is a smart, cost effective, eco-friendly modality in order to reduce fire loss and enhance forest safety.

title: Smart Mirror with AI-Powered Facial and Body Language Recognition for Health Tips and Contextual Emotion Response
authors: Vinoly G, Rathika P, Priyadharshini A, Bharath R, Prabaharan V, Umamageshwari K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Aim: To develop an IoT-based Smart Mirror capable of achieving over 90% effectiveness in real-time monitoring of users' physical and emotional well-being. Materials and Methods: Group 1 represents CNN + LSTM for recognising emotions expressed only through facial expression for RGB images, but accuracy degrades for cases with poses, occlusions, or lighting. Group 2 represents CNN + LSTM with OpenPose/MediaPipe and IoT for combining facial expression and body position to achieve a more accurate and reliable real-time emotional analysis. Result: Experimental evaluation demonstrates 89.5% accuracy in detecting subtle emotional variations in identifying posture deviations under real-world conditions. Conclusion: The proposed Smart Mirror is a non-invasive, user-friendly, and adaptable health companion, improving daily wellness awareness by over 85% and supporting preventive healthcare efficiency by approximately 30.6% through AI- and IoT-driven continuous monitoring.

title: Operationalizing IEC 62443 Through NIST SP 800 82 Revision 3: A Localized OT Security Framework for Indonesia's Energy Sector
authors: Dyah Ayu Citra Maharani, Septia Ulfa Sunaringtyas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: Indonesia's energy sector is becoming increasingly digital, with many utility operations now relying on interconnected Operational Technology (OT) systems, Industrial IoT devices, and remote automation. While this transition improves efficiency, it also expands the sector's exposure to cyber risks. Existing national regulations provide high-level guidance for critical-infrastructure protection, but they do not offer detailed technical controls for securing OT environments. As a result, energy-sector operators lack a clear reference model for aligning their practices with well-established international standards such as IEC 62443 and NIST SP 800-82. This study proposes a Localized OT Security Framework (LOSF) that adapts global OT security principles to Indonesia's regulatory and operational context. The study applies a structured design-science approach that includes standards alignment, regulatory mapping, gap analysis, control consolidation, and maturity-model development. IEC 62443 is used as the structural foundation, while NIST SP 800-82 provides implementation-level guidance. These are then compared with national requirements from Presidential Regulation No. 82/2022, Presidential Regulation 47/2023, and BSSN Regulations No. 8/2023 and No. 10/2023 to identify missing or insufficient technical controls. The resulting LOSF consists of five security domains and a five-level maturity model that support practical adoption across different types of energy-sector operators. The framework helps organizations establish clear governance, secure critical OT assets, strengthen network protections, and improve monitoring and incident-response capabilities. Overall, this study offers a structured and applicable approach for enhancing OT security in Indonesia's energy sector and provides a foundation that can be further expanded by policymakers, researchers, and industry practitioners.

title: Dynamic Open-Loop Trajectory Tracking Control of a Soft Robot Using Servo-Constraints
authors: Jana Dielhenn, Malte Grube, Robert Seifried
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: As the areas of application for soft robots broaden, increasing control demands and challenges arise. Control concepts are required to be reliable and accurate while being computationally efficient. The control of soft robots is, however, particularly demanding, as most soft robots can deform continuously, resulting in infinitely many degrees of freedom. Thus, soft robots are typically intrinsically underactuated. In addition, they are often redundantly actuated. Large deformations, and a possibly infinite number of degrees of freedom make modeling especially challenging. In the field of soft robotics, kinematic controllers neglecting the system dynamics are most commonly used. Data-based control concepts are particularly popular. With an increasing variety of applications, soft robots are, however, required to operate with higher velocities and increased accuracy. Such demands cannot be met by relying on solely kinematic controllers. Furthermore, due to typically extensive training-data requirements, datadriven kinematic controllers cannot easily be extended to dynamic control. In this paper, a new open-loop dynamic controller for a redundantly actuated beam-shaped soft robot is presented. The dynamic modeling of the robot is done using the piecewise constant curvature (PCC) model with multiple segments. A dynamic controller is employed based on model inversion using servo-constraints, which is an emerging technique for model inversion from multibody dynamics. First experimental results on a soft robot show the strength of this approach.

title: Artichoke-Inspired Gripper with Changeable Morphology for Adaptive and Delicate Handling
authors: Long Duy Nguyen, Nhan Huu Nguyen, Nam Phuong Dam, Sota Matsumoto, Van Anh Ho
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: Soft robotics has emerged as a promising field due to its inherent flexibility and adaptability, allowing structures to deform and reconfigure for various functional tasks. Current soft grippers demonstrate significant flexibility in handling objects of diverse morphologies and materials; however, they often lack selective morphologies that allow adaptation to various sizes and shapes of the target objects. To overcome these limitations, we present the Artichoke-inspired Gripper that can actively change the gripping levels for delicate object handling. The gripper consists of multiple layers, containing tapered, petal-shaped fingers distributed radially around the center. Driven by an inward-pulling actuator mechanism, the grasping behavior is realized when the petal tiers are sequentially pulled inward into the body one by one. This mechanism not only offers adjustable grasping characteristics based on the predesigned morphology of each petal layer but also reveals a unique grasping process that is sequential and hierarchical. A series of experiments is conducted to validate such arguments. The proposed device promotes an approach that purposefully forces the soft body deformation to its full extent to expose functionality.

title: Impact of Slow Data Poisoning Attacks on Smart Traffic Light Control Systems
authors: Ahlam Elsayed, Raghad Alghamdi, Sara Ismail, Wejdan Badwood, Ehab Abou-Bakr, Mostafa Nofal
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: Smart traffic light control systems increasingly rely on adaptive decision-making based on real-time traffic observations, making them vulnerable to integrity attacks targeting their sensing and control workflows. This paper investigates the impact of a slow data poisoning attack on an adaptive traffic signal controller implemented in a SUMO-based smart traffic simulation environment. The proposed attack gradually manipulates lane-level traffic features, including queue length, waiting time, and vehicle count, in order to bias the controller's prioritization behavior without directly modifying the physical infrastructure. To ensure statistical validity, the evaluation was conducted over 10 independent simulation runs using randomized seeds. The results show that the attack significantly distorts the controller's internal behavior, increasing the average green time from 31.86 s to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$76.59 ~\mathrm{s}(+140.40 \%)$</tex> and the decision score from 11.86 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$181.78(+1433.07 \%)$</tex>. In addition, a moderate increase in network-level waiting time <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(+1.31 \%)$</tex> and halting vehicles (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+0.82 \%$</tex>) was observed. These findings demonstrate that slow data poisoning attacks can effectively compromise adaptive traffic control logic while remaining relatively stealthy in early stages, leading to gradual performance degradation. This highlights a critical security vulnerability in intelligent transportation systems and emphasizes the need for robust anomaly detection mechanisms.

title: Guidance and Control Assessments of Psyche Mission's Monthly Operations Sequences
authors: David Sternberg, Abby Couto, Michael Ying
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: The Psyche mission, launched in October 2023, relies on its Guidance, Navigation, and Control (GNC) subsystem for reaching and orbiting the (16) Psyche asteroid. Once there, the GNC subsystem will facilitate the mission return valuable science data on the metal-rich asteroid. The Psyche spacecraft includes multiple sensors and actuators as part of its GNC subsystem, and each component has unique operational capabilities and behaviors that, together with mission requirements, influence GNC algorithm designs. The GNC subsystem is part of the broader mission's flight system, necessitating that its operational sequences, built approximately monthly, are reviewed by GNC and the rest of the flight subsystems. This monthly sequence review process is designed to ensure that the inputs to the sequence build from each spacecraft subsystem have been collected and implemented in a safe and effective manner. Each subsystem has a unique process for assessing the draft sequences, which are collections of relative-timed commands, and may include periods of thrusting using electric propulsion thrusters, coasting, performing science activities, performing maintenance and characterization activities, momentum management activities, and other behaviors necessary for maintaining the health of the spacecraft. This paper presents the process by which the GNC subsystem reviews the monthly sequences. It also provides an overview of the tools used to assess the sequences' readiness for flight, including a high-fidelity simulation of the GNC commands using the Controls Analysis System Testbed/GNC Integrated Systems Testbed (CAST/GIST), the vector fit tool (VFT2), constraint checker, and Small Forces Tool. Additionally, the paper describes the individual checks that the responsible GNC engineer performs to ensure the sequenced commands meet the GNC subsystem's flight rules and guidelines. Lastly, the paper describes the products that the GNC review process provides to the other subsystems to aid their own review processes.

title: Design of an IoT-Controlled Solar Powered Cold Storage Unit for Horticultural Product
authors: Prakash P, Dineshkumar B, Dhivya Tharshini M, Dhivyadharshini V, Deekshana E
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Post-harvest losses in horticultural products are still a major issue. This is mainly due to poor storage conditions and a lack of reliable cold storage facilities, particularly in rural areas. This paper presents the design of a smart, solar-powered cold storage system that uses Internet of Things (IoT) technology. This system aims to maintain the quality and extend the shelf life of horticultural produce. The operational performance of each of distinct crop's respective storage sections is monitored through real time check of core environmental parameters, such as their temperature, moisture, and any indicators of deterioration. A remote monitoring application allows users access to storage area monitoring databases along with real time variation alerts, so remedial actions can take place before any loss occurs. Using renewable energy helps ensure sustainability, making the system accessible in many rural areas; providing farmers and smallholder producers with a business model that has the potential for scalability through reduced post-harvest losses and increased storage efficiencies. These problems can, in large part be attributed to the lack of sufficient and dependable refrigerated storage options within these rural communities. This paper presents the design of a smart, solar-powered cold storage system that uses Internet of Things (IoT) technology. This system aims to maintain the quality and extend the shelf life of horticultural produce. It continuously checks and controls key environmental factors like temperature, humidity, and signs of spoilage within separate storage sections that meet the needs of different crops.infrastructure for modern horticultural supply chains.

title: Expanding Venus Entry Accessibility through a Resonance Transfer
authors: Maxwell Jacobson, Ye Lu
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: Aerial platforms and landers have been popular choices for Venus exploration. However, there are many constraints that limit the entry accessibility to specific latitude and longitude, such as heating and deceleration loads, launch window, direct-to-Earth communication, and time of Venus day at arrival. The accessibility restriction is especially constrained upon direct entry following Earth-Venus interplanetary transfer. Though additional orbit insertion prior to entry may provide more flexibility, it inevitably requires more mass for the propulsion system, which is otherwise at a minimum for direct entry. In this work, we consider a mission with resonance transfer after the first encounter of Venus following Earth launch. The resonance transfer ensures that the spacecraft will rendezvous with Venus a second time allowing a much wider range of arrival geometries, resulting in much broader access on the Venusian surface. The methodology compares the entry access of ballistic Earth-Venus Interplanetary to the access enabled by the additional resonance transfer across multiple constraint conditions and Earth-Venus synodic periods. Results show that the resonance transfer provides consistent improvements to entry accessibility and global coverage across multiple synodic periods. The improvement is most notable for the most constrained conditions of entry. Modeling shows that the percentage of surface locations accessible by at least a three-week launch window increases from 9.09 % using direct entry to 98.36 % using the additional resonance transfer for the 2031 synodic period with the most constrained conditions of entry.

title: iZEN: An Energy World Model Framework Bridging Planning and Operation in Industrial Microgrids
authors: Donglai Sun, Weiwei Shen, Weiming Zhang, Xiang Gao
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: The global energy transition necessitates managing unprecedented systemic complexity within industrial microgrids. Currently, techno-economic analyses predominantly decouple capacity planning from real-time operations, severely limiting the ability to capture dynamic arbitrage opportunities and mitigate risks in fluctuating electricity and carbon markets. This methodological fracture inevitably results in conservative system sizing and suboptimal asset utilization. To overcome these critical limitations, we introduce the intelligent Zero-Carbon Energy Network (iZEN), an Energy World Model framework that seamlessly bridges planning and operation through a two-stage optimization architecture. The primary stage establishes the optimal physical topology of the microgrid using long-term forecasting, strictly quantifying the nonlinear trade-offs between capital expenditures and lifecycle revenues. The secondary stage implements an IoT-driven, real-time dispatch strategy within these physical boundaries, proactively hedging against spot price volatility and punitive carbon tariffs to maximize long-term asset value. An empirical case study of a large-scale industrial enterprise—previously suffering from 100 GWh of annual solar curtailment—validates that the iZEN framework simultaneously achieves optimal system design and highly profitable real-time execution.

title: Comparative Analysis of Price Cap Mechanisms in U.S. Electricity Markets and Implications for China
authors: Yang Xu, Jiahua Hu, Ying Wu, Ying Zhang
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: With the growth of energy consumption demand, the diversification of supply structures, and the high penetration of renewable energy in China, price volatility in the electricity spot market has intensified. As a tool for price risk management, the rational design of price cap mechanisms is pivotal for "guaranteeing supply and stabilizing prices" and the optimal allocation of resources. Currently, the development of price cap mechanisms across Chinese provinces is uneven, and research on their underlying design logic is insufficient. Meanwhile, as a pioneer in electricity market construction, the United States has established differentiated price cap pathways in its three major regional markets: PJM, ERCOT, and MISO. From the dimensions of pricing benchmarks, application phases, and risk control, this paper systematically analyzes the price cap practices of these three markets. By comparing their design logics and functional positioning, this paper provides implications for China.

title: Distributed Area Coverage with High Altitude Balloons Using Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Adam Haroon, Tristan Schuler
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: High Altitude Balloons (HABs) can leverage stratospheric wind layers for limited horizontal control, enabling applications in reconnaissance, environmental monitoring, and communications networks. Existing multi-agent HAB coordination approaches use deterministic methods like Voronoi partitioning and extremum seeking control for large global constellations, which perform poorly for smaller teams and localized missions. While single-agent HAB control using reinforcement learning has been demonstrated on HABs, coordinated multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) has not yet been investigated. This work presents the first systematic application of multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) to HAB coordination for distributed area coverage. We extend our previously developed reinforcement learning simulation environment (RLHAB) to support cooperative multi-agent learning, enabling multiple agents to operate simultaneously in realistic atmospheric conditions. We adapt QMIX for HAB area coverage coordination, leveraging Centralized Training with Decentralized Execution to address atmospheric vehicle coordination challenges. Our approach employs specialized observation spaces providing individual state, environmental context, and teammate data, with hierarchical rewards prioritizing coverage while encouraging spatial distribution. We demonstrate that QMIX achieves similar performance to the theoretically optimal geometric deterministic method for distributed area coverage, validating the MARL approach and providing a foundation for more complex autonomous multi-HAB missions where deterministic methods become intractable.

title: CSI-Tuples-Based 3-D Channel Fingerprints Construction Assisted by Multimodal Learning
authors: Chenjie Xie, Li You, Ruirong Chen, Gaoning He, Xiqi Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Low-altitude communications can promote the integration of aerial and terrestrial wireless resources, expand network coverage, and enhance transmission quality, thereby empowering the development of sixth-generation (6G) mobile communications. As an enabler for low-altitude transmission, 3D channel fingerprints (3D-CF), also referred to as the 3D radio map or 3D channel knowledge map, are expected to enhance the understanding of communication environments and assist in the acquisition of channel state information (CSI), thereby avoiding repeated estimations and reducing computational complexity. In this paper, we propose a modularized multimodal framework to construct 3D-CF. Specifically, we first establish the 3D-CF model as a collection of CSI-tuples based on Rician fading channels, with each tuple comprising the low-altitude vehicle’s (LAV) positions and its corresponding statistical CSI. In consideration of the heterogeneous structures of different prior data, we formulate the 3D-CF construction problem as a multimodal regression task, where the target channel information in the CSI-tuple can be estimated directly by its corresponding LAV positions, together with communication measurements and geographic environment maps. Then, a high-efficiency multimodal framework is proposed accordingly, which includes a correlation-based multimodal fusion (Corr-MMF) module, a multimodal representation (MMR) module, and a CSI regression (CSI-R) module. Numerical results show that our proposed framework can efficiently construct 3D-CF and achieve at least 27.5% higher accuracy than the state-of-the-art algorithms under different communication scenarios, demonstrating its competitive performance and excellent generalization ability. We also analyze the computational complexity and illustrate its superiority in terms of the inference time.

title: A Hybrid Carrier-Based PWM With Optimized Output Quality and Reduced Common-Mode Voltage for Coupled Ten-Switch Three-Phase Three-Level Inverter
authors: Xiaojun Deng, Xiaonan Zhu, Zongheng Wu, Yang Chen, Hongliang Wang, Xi Gui, Mingyang Han, Botao Linghu, Yun Yang, Xiumei Yue
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Coupled ten-switch three-phase three-level inverter (TP-TLI) has attracted significant attention in industry due to reduced switch count and compact structure. However, existing carrier-based pulse-width modulation (CBPWM) strategies require at least one phase to operate under two-level (2L) at any modulation index. This article presents a hybrid CBPWM (HCBPWM) to improve the performance. The duty ratio relationships of high level for the three phases are analyzed to reveal the formation mechanism of undesired switching states, which are prevented by hybrid-level modulation (HLM) based on volt-second balance. In HCBPWM, the maximum and minimum phases are modulated under three-level (3L), while the middle phase is modulated using HLM, including HL, 3L, or 2L. This method improves output voltage and current quality, naturally reduces the common-mode voltage (CMV) by 33.3% without additional effort, and maintains the same DC-link voltage utilization as traditional CBPWM. Moreover, a zero-sequence voltage (ZSV) injection method, considering the neutral-point (NP) currents and NP voltage (NPV) deviation, is presented to balance the NPV under various modulation indexes and power factors. Experiments on a 3 kVA prototype verify the feasibility of the proposed HCBPWM, showing up to 53.64% and 74.44% reduction in total harmonic distortions (THDs) for phase voltage and phase current, respectively.

title: MGRT-Net: Multi-Gain Radiometric Response Transfer Network for On-Orbit Relative Radiometric Calibration of Micro Nighttime Remote Sensing Satellites
authors: Li T. Li, Jia Y. Cao, Yong H. Jiang, Cheng J. Wang, Xin Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Inconsistent radiometric response characteristics of nighttime sensor detectors can interfere with weak nighttime surface radiation, resulting in radiometric inconsistencies in captured images. This degrades image quality and compromises information extraction from such image products. Relative radiometric calibration involves calibrating response differences among imaging detectors to eliminate radiometric inconsistencies, which is particularly critical for nighttime sensors. However, existing on-orbit calibration methods require onboard calibration devices, reliable attitude control, and dedicated calibration imaging tasks. The limited hardware capabilities and attitude control capacity of micro nighttime satellites pose significant challenges for radiometric calibration. This study proposes a deep learning–based on-orbit relative radiometric calibration method for nighttime sensors to address these limitations, representing an effort to apply deep learning techniques to relative radiometric calibration of remote sensing satellites. A Multi-Gain Radiometric Response Transfer Network (MGRT-Net) was developed to characterize the radiometric response relationships of nighttime sensor across gain settings. A daytime low-gain radiometric calibration reference was established using a uniform calibration method. The calibration reference is transferred to nighttime images to calibrate the radiometric parameters of the nighttime sensor in high-gain mode. Using Luojia1-01 nighttime images, MGRT-Net accurately simulated radiometric responses across different gain settings and effectively transferred daytime low-gain radiometric calibration references to nighttime images. After MGRT-Net–based transfer correction, radiometric response errors among sensor detectors in nighttime high-gain images were effectively eliminated. Under low- and high- brightness, the streaking metrics exceeded 0.027% (76.5% improvement) and 0.028% (75.6% improvement), respectively, outperforming existing methods. Compared with analytical calibration methods, the proposed method requires no prior model or additional satellite requirements and demonstrates strong adaptability. This study provides a reference for micro-remote sensing satellites constrained by manufacturing costs and validates the applicability of deep learning methods for relative radiometric calibration.

title: Bearing-Only Tracking of Underwater Multiple Acoustic Sources Using Underwater Vehicles Under Latency and Packet Loss_supp1-3684218.mp4
authors: Tianming Gao
publish: N/A
abstract: Bearing-only tracking (BOT) of underwater acoustic sources is crucial for numerous applications in the underwater domain. A promising approach involves reconfigurable underwater wireless sensor networks (UWSNs) composed of a group of underwater vehicles that enable target state estimation via data fusion while enhancing observability. However, the uncertainty of measurement-target association and unstable acoustic communications pose significant challenges. This article presents a selective association probabilistic multi-hypothesis tracking (SA-PMHT) solution tailored for BOT of underwater targets under latency and packet loss. Specifically, a selective association mechanism is designed to determine measurement-target associations by synthesizing candidate measurements for each target. Then, a pseudo-linear equation is constructed to establish regression matrices for targets, and a sliding-window least-squares estimator is embedded for iterative state estimation. We demonstrate that the SA-PMHT tracking framework is a pseudo-posterior variant of the expectation-maximization (EM) algorithm. Meanwhile, theoretical analyses are provided for its computational complexity and sufficient conditions for target observability. Finally, both simulation and experimental results validate the effectiveness of our solution.

title: FOF-X: Towards Real-time Detailed Human Reconstruction from a Single Image
authors: Qiao Feng, Yuanwang Yang, Yebin Liu, Yu-Kun Lai, Jingyu Yang, Kun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: We introduce FOF-X for real-time reconstruction of detailed human geometry from a single image. Balancing real-time speed against high-quality results is a persistent challenge, mainly due to the high computational demands of existing 3D representations. To address this, we propose Fourier Occupancy Field (FOF), an efficient 3D representation by learning the Fourier series. The core of FOF is to factorize a 3D occupancy field into a 2D vector field, retaining topology and spatial relationships within the 3D domain while facilitating compatibility with 2D convolutional neural networks. Such a representation bridges the gap between 3D and 2D domains, enabling the integration of human parametric models as priors and enhancing the reconstruction robustness. Based on FOF, we design a new reconstruction framework, FOF-X, to avoid the performance degradation caused by texture and lighting. This enables our real-time reconstruction system to better handle the domain gap between training images and real images. Additionally, in FOF-X, we enhance the inter-conversion algorithms between FOF and mesh representations with a Laplacian constraint and an automaton-based discontinuity matcher, improving both quality and robustness. We validate the strengths of our approach on different datasets and real-captured data, where FOF-X achieves new state-of-the-art results.

title: A Streamlined Fault Detection and Selectivity Method based on a Normalized Current Difference Criterion and Apparent Impedance for Onshore Wind Farm Collector Grids
authors: Raphael T. Sakai, Silvio G. Di Santo, Nelson Kagan, Celestino P. T. Kalueyo, Carlos F. M. Almeida
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The large-scale integration of inverter-based resources (IBRs) has substantially altered the magnitude, phase-angle behavior, and transient evolution of fault currents in modern power systems. These changes pose significant challenges to conventional protection schemes in wind farm collector grids. In such systems, the limited short-circuit current contribution from converter-interfaced wind turbines, together with variations in apparent impedance, can compromise relay performance. In this context, this paper proposes a streamlined two-stage feeder protection approach for onshore wind farm collector grids. In the first stage, faults are detected using a normalized current-difference criterion computed from pre-fault and fault current samples. In the second stage, internal and external faults are discriminated using the apparent positive-sequence fault impedance evaluated in the R-X plane. The method was assessed through 24,120 PSCAD-simulated fault scenarios based on a realistic wind farm model. All simulated fault cases were successfully detected, and all analyzed cases were correctly classified as internal or external under the studied conditions.Additional validation using nine real fault records confirmed the practical feasibility of the proposed approach and showed faster fault detection than the installed feeder relays in the analyzed events. Overall, the results indicate that the method enables fast, reliable protection decisions in wind-farm collector grids while maintaining low computational complexity.

title: FSA-DFNet: A Diffusion-Enhanced Soft Sensor Prediction Model for BTP with Hybrid Frequency-Spatial Attention Mechanism
authors: Yuhan Gong, Chonghao Wang, Xin Yu, Ruikun Gong, Mohamad Tarmizi Abu Seman
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Sintering, as a critical process in iron and steelmaking, has a direct impact on the quality of sintered ore as well as the output and quality of blast furnace ironmaking. The burn-through point (BTP) is a key state parameter for assessing sinter quality, yet it remains difficult to accurately predict in actual production. Given the characteristics of the sintering process—such as multivariable dynamics, large time delays, nonlinearity, and strong coupling—this paper develops an intelligent BTP prediction model based on a hybrid attention FNet architecture.Its diffusion-enhanced preprocessing module can simultaneously collect various sensor time-series data (such as temperature, pressure, etc.) from the windbox, as well as process parameters like ignition temperature and bed thickness.By utilizing an iterative denoising mechanism, it mitigates noise in the windbox data and accomplishes predictive modeling and reconstruction of missing or anomalous sensor data. By integrating an FNet feature fusion network into a hybrid frequency-domain and spatial-domain attention module, the proposed model reduces computational complexity and achieves a 30% improvement in predictive performance over CNN-LSTM models. With an overall data fitting degree of 0.948, the model effectively addresses the scarcity of key abnormal operating condition samples in industrial scenarios and significantly enhances robustness and generalization capability under extreme operating conditions.

title: Wafer-scale GPU Memory Pool with In-Package Optics for Enhanced Capacity and Bandwidth
authors: Khakim Akhunov, Aakash Patel, Robert Kleijnen, Julien Ryckaert, James Myers, Dwaipayan Biswas
publish: IEEE Computer Architecture Letters
abstract: The escalating demands of modern AI applications push the limits of conventional GPU memory systems, where high-bandwidth memory (HBM) architectures face critical challenges in scalability and bandwidth efficiency. We propose a wafer-scale system integrating photonic interconnects to decouple GPU compute from memory, addressing capacity and bandwidth limitations of conventional HBM-based architectures. We develop an analytical framework, HBM-pool, blending in-package optical I/O (OIO) parameters with GPU memory traffic for high-density nodes. The system-technology co-optimization (STCO) study shows that 300 mm wafer-scale disaggregation with OIO can achieve a 4× increase in memory capacity and reduce bandwidth bottlenecks up to 8× compared to a 2.5D electrically packaged node, enabling larger training/inference and improving latency.

title: Exact Modal Decomposition of Axial Currents in Bulk and Hollow Conducting Cylinders
authors: N. Isernia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: The modal decomposition of eddy current problems is commonly treated numerically via integral formulations or via the imposition of arbitrary homogeneous boundary conditions at some distance from the sources in case of differential formulations. In this paper the eigenvalues and eigenvectors for axial currents in an infinitely long conducting cylinder surrounded by vacuum are computed analytically, rigorously accounting for the solution of the outer magnetostatic problem. The analytically derived eigenvalues and eigenvectors are compared against the results of a specifically developed numerical model based on an integral formulation. The two approaches mutually confirm each other’s validity. The procedure highlighted is general and could be extended to different geometries.

title: Reliability-Based Preventive Maintenance for a Humanoid Robot System under Dependent Competing Failure Processes
authors: Chunyan Ling, Lechang Yang, Jingzhe Lei, Suk Joo Bae
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: Modeling system reliability under dependent competing failure processes remains challenging due to the complex interactions among failure modes. In such systems, soft failures arise from the combined effects of continuous degradation and additional damage induced by abrupt external shocks, while hard failures are triggered by sudden and catastrophic stresses from the same shock process. In this work, a general reliability model is developed by incorporating coupling factors, permitting a formulation that is independent of specific degradation or shock models and does not rely on predefined failure mode classifications. Then, a tailored solution algorithm is designed for the proposed reliability model by decoupling the originally coupled structure and incorporating auxiliary variables and additional constraints. Building on this framework, a preventive maintenance strategy is developed to minimize the average long-run cost rate under periodic inspection. Finally, the reliability model and maintenance approach are implemented on a humanoid robot system, demonstrating the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed methodology.

title: Techno-Economic Analysis With Environmental Performance Considerations For Fluorescent-To-LED Lighting Retrofits
authors: Sultan Sh. Alanzi
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: Lighting systems represent a significant and persistent component of electrical demand in buildings, making them a practical target for energy efficiency improvements. This paper presents a comprehensive techno-economic assessment framework for fluorescent-to-LED lighting retrofits, developed to support evaluation across different buildings and market conditions. The proposed framework quantifies connected load reduction, annual energy savings, and key economic performance indicators, including simple and discounted payback periods. Scenario-based assumptions are incorporated to account for uncertainty in cost parameters and component lifetimes to enable scalable and transferable analysis. The framework is applied to a representative building as a case study, where conservative, moderate, and optimistic scenarios are evaluated. The results demonstrate that LED retrofitting achieves substantial reductions in electrical demand and annual energy consumption, leading to significant operating cost savings and short payback periods across all examined scenarios. In addition to the economic evaluation, the framework incorporates environmental performance indicators to facilitate future carbon impact assessment when emission data are available.

title: Optimizing Face Detection in Low-Resolution Images under Blur Condition Using Lucy Richardson Algorithm
authors: S. Hemalatha, V. Sowmitha, P. Nithya, V. Dharunraj, K. Dhinesh, S. Jayasurya
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Aim: The purpose behind this project is to optimize face detection performance in low-resolution images with blur and increase the clarity of the image using the Lucy Richardson deconvolution algorithm. Materials and Methods: The experimental evaluation is conducted using two groups. Group 1 is a classical deblurring process that uses traditional methods to deblur the image using the CNN Algorithm. According to the experiment data, Group 1 results in achieving a face detection rate of about 60.9%, a precision rate of 61.9%, recall of 58.9% and an F1 score of 59.9%, which gives a4 low-resolution image, and it takes a large amount of time to deblur the image. Group 2 uses modern methods to restore the image quality, using a Lucy-Richardson algorithm with NLM, TV regularization, Haar-cascade and GFPGAN, which helps to provide a better optimized image. Result: Performance metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score were used to measure the performance of the existing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C N N$</tex>-based approach and the proposed Lucy-Richardson-based approach for image restoration in blur conditions. The accuracy of the existing approach was as low as 56.2% in high blur conditions, whereas the proposed approach achieved a maximum of 92.3% accuracy in low blur conditions and 85.2% accuracy in high blur conditions with a statistical significance value of 0.001 (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<0.05$</tex>). Conclusion: The proposed system manages to obtain back the blurred images and maintain the stability against the Lucy-Richardson-based deconvolution is an efficient and reliable preprocessing solution that helps to improve the performance of face detection in the conditions of real-life surveillance and forensics.

title: Accelerating the Post-Quantum Transition: A GPU-Based Performance Comparison of ML-KEM and ML-DSA
authors: Sangeetha K, Shreya Khodanpur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Trends in Advanced Computing (ICCTAC)
abstract: As National Institute for Standards and Technology (NIST) moves towards completing its Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) standards (FIPS 203 ML-KEM & FIPS 204 ML-DSA), it leaves the cloud security industry with a complex, yet unavoidable challenge. These new lattice-based algorithms are designed to combat attacks from quantum computers, but they do come with a price. The performance degradation will be significant as well as impacting the overall infrastructure.To understand the impact of NIST's new algorithms we performed a benchmarking study to see how each of them compare side-by-side over multiple NIST security levels with an emphasis on the resources they consume and time taken to perform each operation.As expected, we found that both algorithms have a large amount of overhead due to their computational processes (specifically using Number Theoretic Transform and rejection sampling) which, while not necessarily apparent to end-users, are a factor users should be aware of.One thing we did not do in our testing is to only test in an ideal laboratory environment rather we also created a hybrid test system consisting of an AMD Ryzen 9 7950X and an NVIDIA H100 (to closely resemble a real-world modern cloud setup). Although there was a lot of variance in the results, ML-KEM yielded the best overall effectiveness, particularly due to its lower resource utilization (compared to ML-DSA) and optimized for high-volume key exchange very well. The challenge is still figuring out how to implement and implement correctly; that will require further research in the future.

title: A Comparison Analysis of Traditional Versus AI-Driven Tourism Ecosystems
authors: Marwan Aljijakli, Nadine Akkari
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: The tourism sector has gone through a paradigm shift from traditional to AI-based systems that use emerging technologies to transform customers' journeys and operating service models. Currently, the static distribution of information and the reactive delivery of services in traditional tourism ecosystems have progressively been replaced by digital platforms and intelligent systems that utilize machine learning with predictive analytics. Three key aspects of these ecosystems are critically compared in this paper, including operational performance, costs, customer experience, and sustainability. While AI-driven ecosystems are capable of scalability and realtime adaptability, hyper-personalization is crucial not just for data forecasting but also for environmental footprint. Yet the evolution poses some issues, such as data privacy and algorithmic biases that can lead to inefficient human experience. The comparative analysis recommends hybrid environments enabling artificial intelligence augmentation in combination with human monitoring to minimize ethical challenges and maximize digital advantages. Future trends are toward the convergence of smart tourism that addresses autonomous agents under responsible AI frameworks aiming for sustainability and ethical deployment of AI-driven solutions.

title: ECORE: Energy-Conscious Optimized Routing for Deep Learning Models at the Edge
authors: Daghash K. Alqahtani, Maria A. Rodriguez, Muhammad Aamir Cheema, Hamid Rezatofighi, Adel N. Toosi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Communications (PerCom)
abstract: Edge computing enables data processing closer to the source, significantly reducing latency, an essential requirement for real-time vision-based analytics such as object detection in surveillance and smart city environments. However, these tasks place substantial demands on resource-constrained edge devices, making the joint optimization of energy consumption and detection accuracy critical. To address this challenge, we propose ECORE , a framework that integrates multiple dynamic routing strategies, including a novel estimation-based techniques and an innovative greedy selection algorithm, to direct image processing requests to the most suitable edge device–model pair. ECORE dynamically balances energy efficiency and detection performance based on object characteristics. We evaluate our framework through extensive experiments on real-world datasets, comparing against widely used baseline techniques. The evaluation leverages established object detection models (YOLO, SSD, EfficientDet) and diverse edge platforms, including Jetson Orin Nano, Raspberry Pi 4 and 5, and TPU accelerators. Results demonstrate that our proposed context-aware routing strategies can reduce energy consumption and latency by 35% and 49%, respectively, while incurring only a 2% loss in detection accuracy compared to accuracy-centric methods.

title: Automated Crack Detection in Concrete Structures Using Image Processing and Deep Learning Techniques
authors: N. Mohanapriya, B. Varsha
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Concrete crack detection is an essential utility that guards the structural health since surface cracks are early warning of structural damages.
