abstract: A primary challenge in network defense is to mine potential attack campaigns from massive, continuously arriving alerts and telemetry data in real time. This paper presents HoSig-Align I, an edge side unsupervised method designed for network streams to discover homologous blocks. Our approach innovatively fuses heterogeneous features like Internet Protocol (IP) and Payload into a unified representation while strictly excluding temporal information from it, only incorporating time via a dual time scale decay model during graph construction to capture temporal proximity. A density robust similarity is computed using an isolation style random partition forest, leading to a sparse k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN) graph. The stream is then accurately segmented into internally cohesive and mutually isolated homologous blocks through spectral ordering and contrastive change point detection. Each block is encoded into a lightweight HoSig signature, forming the basis for cross organizational collaboration. Experiments on real network streams show that HoSig-Align I identifies coherent attack campaign blocks and improves separation and boundary clarity over baselines, while meeting low-latency and low-overhead requirements for edge processing.

title: IoT Based Integrated Design for Aeroponic Plant Cultivation Using LSTM Algorithm
authors: K.R. Gokul Anand, P. Dhivya, D. Faridha Banu, Boopathy S, M. Sivakumar, Dinesh E
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: To sustain the increasing global population, there is a need for new and innovative agricultural approaches that can minimize the negative impact of shortages in natural resources and environmental degradation, while ensuring that the basic human need of food security is met. Traditional soil-based agriculture is becoming unsustainable because of various factors such as soil erosion, over-irrigation, loss of fertilizers from the soil, and declining freshwater. As an alternative, aeroponic farming is a new and innovative growing process that is not resource dependent, provides greater crop yields, allows for more efficient use of natural resources, and is less susceptible to attacks from pests and diseases, as well as climate conditions. However, due to the lack of a reliable irrigation system, there can frequently be too much or too little water applied, resulting in poor growth and production of the plants. Recent research has used machine learning algorithms to monitor the health of the plant and to make decisions on when to irrigate; however, the accuracy of those predictions have been very low due to the inability of those algorithms to make long-term time-based correlations and to adapt to changing environmental conditions. In this study, a solution to these problems is proposed with the integration of an IoTenabled integrated aeroponic growing system using Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) networks. By utilizing IoT architecture to collect real-time measurements of environmental parameters including temperature, humidity, CO2 levels, and amount of light, various methods will enable prediction of the amount of water needed for irrigation and how fast plants are photosynthesizing with LSTM models. This is accomplished by linking these various forms of technology, allowing for efficient use of resources and increasing productivity per acre.

title: AI-Driven Consciousness Assessment, Recovery Forecasting & Hydration Management for Coma Patients
authors: T. Joby Titus, Reenasri S, Sadhana L, Sujaaya J
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Coma patients require continuous monitoring and timely medical intervention in intensive care units (ICU). Such patients need to be regularly and even continuously monitored, quickly intervened when the need arises, and, on time, accurately diagnosed to know the prognosis. Hence, the authors in the paper suggest an AI-based healthcare system that can determine the level of consciousness in real-time, predict recovery, and manage the hydration of coma patients. This system which is under the spotlight in the paper uses a set of biomedical sensors to record the patient's physiological signals such as EEG, heart rate, oxygen saturation, and hydration level. These physiological data are transferred through an IoT infrastructure to the cloud where machine learning models analyse the data for prediction. Patient consciousness interpretation, as well as recovery prediction, are performed by the K-Nearest Neighbours (KNN) classifier. The hydration monitoring module provides timely alerts to prevent dehydration-related complications. The experiment results have confirmed that the consciousness manifestation detection's precision reaches 88 %, the recovery prediction performance corresponds to a value of 0.81 for the area under the ROC curve, and the hydration accuracy is 92 %. On top of that, the average response time to an alert is only 1.2 s. The results indicate that the system presented here significantly supports the work of clinicians in making decisions and, thus, gives a great boost to personalized critical care.

title: Research on Dry wet Transition Control of Supercritical Coal fired Power Generation Unit Based on Fuzzy Weighted Model Predictive Control
authors: Zhuoyue Shi, Xiao Wu
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: During deep peak shaving, low-load operation can cause coal-fired power units to shift from dry to wet condition, making the transition difficult to control. To ensure safe and stable operation, smooth control during this transition is essential. This study addresses that challenge by developing a mechanism model that captures the core energy and mass conversion processes in the unit. A fuzzy weighted model predictive control (FWMPC) algorithm is proposed, which applies fuzzy weighting to control variables to handle the differing dynamics of dry and wet states. Simulation results show that FWMPC improves system performance during wet-dry transitions, reducing the mean absolute error of power generation by 0.68, enabling fast and accurate tracking of setpoints.

title: Design for Recycling: Prototyping a Rib-less Interior Permanent Magnet Rotor with Enhanced Disassembly
authors: Akbar Mohammadi Ajamloo, Vincent Van de Peer, Mohamed N. Ibrahim, Peter Sergeant
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: , end of life (EoL) design, interior permanent magnet (IPM), modular rotor, recycling, rib-less rotor

title: A Novel Fractional Order Slip Model for Dynamic Friction Estimation in Industrial Robot
authors: Tianzhu Xun, Ying Luo, Pengjie Huang, Wei Ai, Tiancai Liang, YangQuan Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Accurate friction modeling remains a critical challenge in high-precision robotic systems due to the complex, nonlinear nature of friction and the distinct behaviors characterizing the presliding and sliding regimes. In this article, a novel fractional-order generalized Maxwell-slip (FO-GMS) model is proposed, incorporating fractional-order elements to describe both elastic and viscous deformations in a unified and compact form. Key properties of the proposed model, including hysteresis, the nondrifting property, the Stribeck effect, and friction lag, are investigated. To efficiently decouple and estimate the static and dynamic parameters, a hybrid identification method combining constrained nonlinear least-squares and a linear decreasing weight particle swarm optimization algorithm is developed. Experimental validation on an industrial robot demonstrates that the FO-GMS model achieves an error reduction of over 50% while utilizing 23.08% fewer parameters compared to conventional GMS and existing fractional models. Furthermore, the sensitivity and generalization capability of the model have been verified across different robot joints and operating velocities. These results confirm the superior accuracy and computational efficiency of the FO-GMS framework.

title: Reliability Analysis and Improvement of Hermetic LTCC-CBGA Board-Level Interconnects
authors: Buran Wang, Wanli Zhang, Yangyang Li, Chengju Liao, Lu Lv, Xiaoyuan Bai, Borui Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: Focusing on the board-level interconnect structure of hermetic ceramic packages, this study establishes four finite-element analysis models to investigate the thermo-mechanical behavior of CBGA solder joints under thermal shock conditions. Four solder-ball configurations are considered: 0.45-mm-diameter Sn63Pb37 eutectic solder balls, 0.6-mm-diameter Sn63Pb37 solder balls, 0.45-mm-diameter Sn10Pb90 high-Pb solder balls, and 0.6-mm-diameter Sn10Pb90 high-Pb solder balls. The results indicate that employing Sn10Pb90 high-Pb solder balls can significantly reduce the maximum principal stress in the ceramic substrate near the CBGA joints while improving the thermo-fatigue lifetime of the solder balls. In addition, physical verification is conducted for the interconnect structure using 0.45-mm-diameter Sn10Pb90 solder balls; the in-situ measured thermo-fatigue life agrees well with the simulation prediction. To further enhance CBGA interconnect reliability, an underfill reinforcement scheme is applied to the ceramic package board-level interconnects, achieving a solder-joint thermo-fatigue life exceeding 500 cycles.

title: A Ground-based SAR Geocoding Method Based on Dimensional Decomposition Matching in Complex Monitoring Environments
authors: Hongli Huang, Zeyu Zhang, Jun Hu, Yuhan Chen, Haoran Wu, Shuangquan Li
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Geocoding of ground-based synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images remains a significant challenge. This difficulty is particularly pronounced in complex monitoring environments without ground control points (GCPs), where deviations in radar center coordinates further exacerbate geocoding uncertainties and render existing methods inadequate to meet accuracy requirements. To address this issue, this paper proposes a two-stage geocoding method based on dimensional decomposition matching. In the first stage, the Iterative Closest Point (ICP) algorithm aligns external digital elevation models (DEM) data with SAR images in the two-dimensional plane. In the second stage, a Gaussian kernel-based weighting model is constructed to optimize three-dimensional matching points by jointly incorporating intensity and distance information. Experimental results from Mayang County, Hunan Province, China, where dense forest cover, high atmospheric moisture, and prolonged rainfall create a complex environment, demonstrate that the proposed method effectively mitigates the impact of radar center coordinate deviations on geocoding accuracy. Compared with conventional methods, the proposed method achieves superior matching precision and robustness.

title: Modeling of Wide RF-Bandwidth PAs Based on Signal-Sequence Reconstruction
authors: Jikai Ye, Jun Peng, Yuchen Bian, Jiaxuan Zhu, Haozhe Zeng, Tianyang Zhong, Min Xiong, Xinyu Wang, Chenrui Liang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: To address the behavioral modeling challenge caused by the frequency-dependent nonlinear characteristics of power amplifiers (PAs) over a wide radio-frequency (RF) bandwidth, this letter proposes a novel framework based on signal-sequence reconstruction and neural-network retraining. Conventional methods usually establish the behavioral model first and then transfer the model parameters across frequency bands under the assumption that the parameters are interpolatable. To avoid relying on this assumption, the proposed framework employs transfer component analysis (TCA) to achieve cross-band output reconstruction in the signal-sequence space. Based on the reconstructed output signal sequences, an optimal real-valued time-delay neural-network (RVTDNN) model is then individually constructed for PAs under arbitrary carrier-frequency conditions. Experimental results show that, compared with segmented parameter-sharing and interpolation methods, the proposed framework improves the average normalized mean-square error by approximately 3.5 dB over the entire RF bandwidth.
