The implementation stack consists of a React-based user interface, a FastAPI service layer, SQLite for persistent storage, and the Spotify Web API for music delivery. In a pilot study of 45 sessions, 68% resulted in measurable mood elevation, with the mean pre-to-post session valence improvement reaching <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.2 \pm 1.8$</tex> valence units (95% CI: [0.22, 2.18]).

title: Classification of Wheat Disease Using Model-Level Graph Network with Parrot Optimizer
authors: P. Yogashree, M. Sangeetha
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: One of the most vital staple crops in the world, wheat plays a major role in both economic stability and human sustenance. However, a number of diseases pose a threat to wheat productivity, which can severely reduce yield and quality. Existing detection methods face limitations such as imbalanced datasets, noise in images, and high computational requirements, restricting timely and accurate disease identification. To overcome these challenges, the research presents a Model-Level Graph Network with Parrot Optimizer (MoLGNNet-ParO) for wheat disease classification. The approach begins with data collection from the wheat leaf disease dataset, ensuring representation of both healthy and diseased samples. Collected images undergo preprocessing using a Modified Square Root Sage-Husa Adaptive Kalman Filter (MSSHAKF) to reduce noise and enhance image quality. Feature extraction is performed using a Modified ResNet-152 with Hinge Attention Network (MR-152-HiAN), capturing discriminative and hierarchical disease features. Then, the Model-Level Graph Network (MoLGNNet) leverages structural relationships between features to classify wheat leaf diseases accurately, while the Parrot Optimizer (ParO) optimizes the loss function, improving convergence and overall model performance. The experiment yields the better results, which are out of the F1-score (99.79%), precision (99.62%), accuracy (99.84%), specificity (99.57%), and recall (99.45%); the proposal framework performs better than the traditional models. The suggested methodology offers an effective, highly accurate and efficient method of detecting early wheat diseases, promoting sustainable crop yield, and efficient management of the disease.

title: Phasor Measurement Unit with Particle Filtering Estimation
authors: Rithvik Reddy Adapa, Xin Wang
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs) are very sophisticated measuring devices that find amplitude, phase and frequency of various voltages and currents in a power system. Particle filter is a state estimation technique that uses Bayesian inference. Particle filters are widely used in pose estimation and indoor navigation and are very reliable. This paper studies and compares four different particle filters as PMUs namely, generic particle filter (GPF), genetic algorithm particle filter (GAPF), particle swarm optimization particle filter (PSOPF) and adaptive particle filter (APF). Two different test signals are used to test the performance of the filters in terms of responsiveness and correctness of the estimates.

title: Wearable Non-Invasive System for Gestational Diabetes Mellitus Monitoring
authors: Prabakar S, Manikandan K, S. Arunprasath, Bavithra M, Rajasekar M
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Gestational Diabetes Mellitus (GDM) needs a regular evaluation of glucose to minimize the threats to the maternal and fetal health. Traditional methods of blood glucose monitoring are intrusive and tend to discourage frequent tests in pregnancy conditions. This paper will report a wearable and non-invasive monitoring system that will be used in the management of GDM, based on the teargenerated glucose sensing. The system suggested combines a tear glucose biosensor with temperature and humidity detectors, ESP32 microcontroller, and Internet of Things (IoT) platform that will allow transmitting and visualizing real-time data. Signal stabilization and threshold-based warning system is applied to increase the reliability of measurements and to provide early warning of abnormal glucose levels. The system is also low-power architecture built in such a way that it is safe, comfortable and longlasting. The experimental findings indicate sound sensing behaviour, steady wireless connectivity, and sound continuous monitoring. The data indicate that wearable technology based on tears can be useful and painless as an alternative to invasive testing of glucose, which supports better prenatal care and patient adherence.

title: Evaluating AI-Powered Chatbot Acceptance in Indonesian Skincare E-Commerce: An Extended Technology Acceptance Model Approach
authors: Brianna Sanjaya, Mikael Bryan Ciputra, Joseph Setiawan, Antonius Felix, Julius Sutrisno, Johanes Fernandes Andry
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: This study investigates the acceptance and implementation of AI-powered chatbots for customer service in Indonesian skincare e-commerce using an extended Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) framework. The research develops and evaluates an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{A I}$</tex> chatbot prototype for L'Essaine, a pre-launch skincare brand, combining heuristic usability evaluation with TAM-based surveys. Data were collected from 10 participants through usability testing and surveys measuring perceived usefulness, perceived ease of use, attitude toward use, and behavioral intention. Results indicate high acceptance levels with perceived usefulness scoring 4.4/5 and perceived ease of use <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.5 / 5$</tex>. The chatbot successfully handled 45 customer consultations with a 92 % resolution rate. This research integrates chatbot-specific factors including response speed, natural language processing capability, knowledge base comprehensiveness, and interface design into the traditional TAM model. Findings contribute practical insights for skincare brands implementing AI chatbots for prelaunch customer engagement and extend theoretical understanding of conversational AI technology adoption in Indonesian e-commerce contexts. The study recommends phased implementation strategies focusing on domain-specific knowledge base training and continuous learning mechanisms to enhance service quality. As an exploratory study with a limited sample of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0}$</tex> participants, findings are indicative rather than statistically generalizable and are intended to inform future large-scale validation research.

title: Load Characteristic Analysis of eVTOL Propulsion Motors Based on Data-Model Hybrid-Driven Approach
authors: Zhu Chen, Wang Huaji, Wang Huaizhi
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: To ensure the high-reliability operation of electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft power systems, this paper proposes a data-model hybrid-driven approach for analyzing motor load characteristics. A multiphysics coupling mechanism model integrating aerodynamics, flight dynamics, and motor dynamics is established to accurately characterize the generation mechanisms of axial tension, radial unbalanced forces, and transient impact loads. Based on flight test and bench test data, variational mode decomposition (VMD) and long short-term memory (LSTM) networks are employed to identify and compensate for high-frequency random load components that are difficult to model accurately in the mechanistic model, thereby constructing a mechanism-data fused load model. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reveals the complex dynamic load characteristics of eVTOL motors during multi-mode flight, with composite load peaks reaching up to 130% of the rated value and impact dominant frequencies extending beyond 200 Hz. Furthermore, applying this high-fidelity load spectrum to structural health assessment reduces the prediction error of bearing remaining useful life (RUL) by approximately 25%. This study provides an effective analytical tool for the reliability design and airworthiness verification of eVTOL propulsion motors.

title: Distobee: Design and System Review of a Mobile Platform for Lunar Regolith Excavation
authors: Mateusz Wójcik, Adam Michalik
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: This work presents the design, development, and partial validation of a mobile robotic platform designed for the excavation and transportation of lunar regolith. The system was developed by members of the student research group AGH Space Systems as part of their participation in the AGH Lunar Resources Initiative. The team took part in Space Resources Challenge, an initiative launched by the European Space Agency (ESA) and the European Space Resources Innovation Centre (ESRIC) in 2021. The competition aims to foster challenge-driven innovation by involving academic and industrial teams in solving practical problems relevant to the future of lunar exploration. The proposed platform was designed with a strong emphasis on simplicity, modularity, and mechanical robustness to ensure reliable operation in simulated lunar conditions. These include high levels of dust, a vacuum environment, and reduced gravity. The paper outlines the system architecture, highlighting both mechanical and electronic subsystems, and details the design decisions and trade-offs made throughout the development process. A major engineering focus was the development of a custom wheel and drivetrain system tailored to maintain traction and maneuverability on loose regolith while minimizing dust dispersion during movement. Various geometries and tread patterns were explored, taking into account constraints on mass, mechanical complexity, and ease of manufacturing. The suspension system was also optimized to ensure stability during regolith acquisition, enabling smooth operation on uneven terrain. One of the significant challenges addressed was the reliable detection of the fill level inside the onboard excavation-storage container. Given the harsh environmental conditions expected during operation, a comparative evaluation of multiple sensor technologies, including capacitive sensors and radar-based solutions, was conducted to determine the most suitable approach. Energy management represented another critical aspect of the project, as the competition imposed strict limits on available electrical power. A hybrid energy management system was implemented, combining commercial off-the-shelf components with custom-designed modules to optimize energy consumption while maintaining system performance. The prototype developed within this project serves as a functional research platform for future iterations and scientific testing. The system will undergo final testing and validation in October 2025 at the LUNA analog lunar simulation facility, where its performance will be assessed under realistic Moon-like conditions.

title: ReFDrone: One-Shot Indoor Drone 4DoF Estimation via Rolling-Shutter AoA from Floor-Reflected Light
authors: Yusei Onishi, Hiroaki Murakami, Masanari Nakamura, Hiromichi Hashizume, Masanori Sugimoto
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Communications (PerCom)
abstract: Accurate indoor drone four degrees-of-freedom (4DoF) estimation, including both 3D position and heading, is essential for safe flight in GNSS-denied environments, opening up opportunities for fully autonomous IoT applications such as infrastructure inspection and surveillance. Approaches using a built-in camera are attractive due to their low cost and ease of deployment, however, existing methods face key limitations. Visual-Inertial Odometry suffers from drift and often fails in textureless environments, while Visible Light Positioning requires a direct line of sight to ceiling LEDs, which is challenging for drones with a limited field of view. We propose ReFDrone, a system that simultaneously estimates 3D position and heading from a single image using a downward-facing rolling-shutter (RS) landing camera and floor-reflected light from modulated ceiling LEDs. Our core insight is that RS distortion produces spectral lobes in the frequency domain whose orientation directly encodes the angle of arrival (AoA) of the LED. Leveraging this property, ReFDrone extracts AoA from a single RS image, enabling estimation of 2D position and heading from just two LEDs, and derives altitude using a virtual-view image with a distance model. We evaluated ReFDrone under 1296 conditions in 2.5 m x 2.5 m testbed, achieving a 90th percentile 3D position error of 0.335 m and a heading error of 8.101 degrees, demonstrating its accuracy and practicality.

title: A Prototype Low-Cost Hybrid-Actuated Mobile Spherical Terrain Exploration Rover
authors: Winnie Gao, Youyou Luo, Drew Schlauch, Stephen T. Kwok-Choon
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: This paper presents the design, development, and testing of a low-cost Hybrid-Actuated Mobile Spherical Terrain Exploration Rover (HAMSTER) intended for use on a lunar surface exploration mission. As current technologies develop and push boundaries of space exploration, the need for robotic systems capable of maneuvering across various obstacles and terrains is expected to increase. This paper explores the design considerations, manufacturing, and testing completed. HAMSTER uses a pendulum-based actuation method for steering, and an actuated internal shaft that propels the vehicle forward. The internal structure includes a two-tiered central case comprised of: the navigation control module, accelerometer, motor controller, battery, voltage regulators, Raspberry Pi, pendulum servo for steering, and a motor shaft. A camera is being considered to aid in navigation that shall be internally mounted; however, this has presented challenges that will be discussed. Additive manufacturing through 3D printing was selected for rapid prototyping fabrication of the HAMSTER chassis and exterior shell. This has allowed for the proof-of-concept phase of this design sequence, where the shape, form, and function of the vehicle are to be refined through iteration. In conclusion, this paper outlines the considerations found in the design, prototyping, fabrication, and assembly with a discussion on proposed work to be completed that includes integration and preliminary testing of the HAMSTER vehicle.

title: A Multi-Objective Optimization Framework for Chemical Synthesis Planning Using a Retrievalaugmented Language Model
authors: Priyanka More, Srushti Ghogare, Samiksha Surwase, Aditya Sonone, Piyush Waghjale, Ayush Kale
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Chemical synthesis planning is a complex and multi-dimensional decision-making process that goes beyond identifying chemically feasible series of reaction. Traditional Computer-Aided Synthesis Planning (CASP) systems consider feasibility as the main center of focus, often overlooking critical real-world considerations such as economic cost and environmental sustainability. This paper proposes a hybrid decision-support framework that integrates a RetrievalAugmented Generation (RAG)-enabled Large Language Model (LLM) with a Multi-Objective Optimization (MOO) engine to explicitly simulate trade-offs in synthesis planning. The framework generates candidate retrosynthetic routes and evaluates them across three competing objectives: (i) feasibility, (ii) sustainability, quantified using Process Mass Intensity (PMI), and (iii) economic cost. A Pareto front of synthesis pathways is built with an NSGA-II-based optimization module; this module allows chemists to search trade-offs in a systematic manner or building a Pareto front instead of depending on single-objective feasibility. To validate the optimization capability of the proposed architecture, we conduct a proof-ofconcept study using a simulated route-generation agent that generates a realistic distribution of candidate pathways for a target molecule (Ibuprofen). Results demonstrate that the optimization framework can successfully determine the nondominated synthesis paths and can indicate the significant trade-offs between feasibility, cost, and sustainability. The paper presents the prospect of integrating generative AI and formal optimization to convert CASP systems from systems that are based on feasibility planning into systems that undertake decision-based synthesis design.

title: Load Signature Modeling and Charging Guidance for Electric Vehicles Based on Virtual Features Labelling
authors: Chaochuang Wang, Yu Liu, Zhenhuan Li, Ping Wang, Lu Zhou, Fengshun Zhang, Qinran Hu, Tao Qian
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: With the continuous growth of electric vehicle deployment, the temporal and spatial aggregation of charging demand has become increasingly significant. The mismatch between vehicles and charging facilities is becoming more prominent, posing new challenges to user charging experience and distribution network operation. Charging guidance involves not only grid side operating conditions, but also traffic side factors such as detour, queuing, and route choice. As a result, user charging decisions exhibit clear multi source coupling characteristics. Therefore, how to incorporate user loss arising from the transportation system into a unified electrical modeling framework has become a key issue in charging guidance research. To address the difficulty of existing methods in jointly representing multi source factors from the road network side and the grid side, this paper proposes a virtual impedance based charging guidance method for electric vehicles. First, a virtual impedance model composed of road network cost and grid cost is constructed to provide a unified representation of the overall charging cost. Then, a charging guidance model is established by incorporating heterogeneous user preferences. Finally, a two-layer charging guidance strategy is designed, and case studies are conducted to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. The results show that the proposed method can improve the rationality of user charging decisions, relieve local distribution network pressure, and enhance the overall guidance performance.

title: TATERS (Tool for Autonomous Terrain Exploration of Remote Spaces)
authors: Richard Chase, Tyler Doiron, Ryan Navarre, Meryl Spencer, Zane Almquist, Nathan Lemus
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: This paper reports on the development of TATERS (Tool for Autonomous Terrain Exploration of Remote Spaces), a software framework for planning and risk analysis of single and multirobot traversals. TATERS is designed to aid in mission planning workflows by merging planning methodologies with execution of autonomy on board robotic systems. This is done by developing coarse and high-fidelity simulations as well as tying in features of autonomy stacks into Monte Carlo simulations during pre-mission studies. Further, we investigate and report on methods for curating live data, incorporating into the planning activities, and representing and updating optimal paths on autonomous systems without the need for updating large amounts of data. This is achieved by a data reduction scheme called skeletonization which compresses spatio-temporal cost cube data derived from geographic information system (GIS) data layer sets. The cost cube derivation strategy is designed using cost metrics that can be adjusted and guided by intuition. We identify and focus on three key metrics: scientific merit, traversability, and survivability.

title: Ai-Feedback Carton Packaging Customization and Circular Remanufacturing From Thresholding to Recovery Grading
authors: Chen Jiaqi, Muhizam b Mustafa
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: Mass production efficiency often conflicts with the bespoke requirements of modern e-commerce, creating a tension that frequently results in excessive packaging waste. To resolve this, we present a circular design framework for paperbased packaging governed by an AI-driven decision layer. This system functions as a central coordinator, converting complex variables-including product sizing, structural reinforcement needs, SKU imposition, and recovery grading-into executable manufacturing protocols and auditable metrics. We validated the framework using a Digital Twin, which incorporates a virtual converting line subject to manufacturing constraints and a recovery station that generates feedback via QR/UID tracking. Within this environment, a structural threshold model weighs physical risks and logistics uncertainty against reuse potential to dynamically optimize packaging designs. Experimental validation, involving both a ceramics microbenchmark and cross-category comparisons, demonstrates that this approach effectively minimizes material input. Under fixed constraints, the simulation yielded a 12.1 % increase in sheet utilization and reduced assembly requirements by 2.0 steps for high-risk groups. By isolating gross sheet input from material-in-product, this protocol establishes a verifiable method for reporting nesting-driven savings, effectively closing the loop between initial design and remanufacturing to support continuous process Optimization.

title: A multi-Degrees-of-Freedom Soft Robotic Chamber that Mimics Muscles to Stimulate Myoblasts
authors: Atsushi Takata, Yuta Kurashina
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: Mechanical stimulus is a critical factor in myoblast maturation. The effect of one degree of freedom (1DoF) stimulus on myoblasts has been previously investigated by medical engineering researchers; however, mechanical stimuli should be applied in multi-DoF in vivo. This is because a single bone is driven by many muscles, and each muscle actuates each other. In this study, we propose a multi-DoF soft robotic chamber (multi-DoF SRC) to stretch cells with multiple actuators. To this end, we designed and fabricated the chamber composed of a poly-dimethylsiloxane flexible cell carrier and three pneumatic actuators. The collagen-based scaffolds in the carriers were able to transmit actuation to the cells. Next, the actuation characteristics were measured by image processing. Finally, we conducted a preliminary experiment in which myoblasts were cultured for two days while applying 1-DoF and 2-DoF stretches ($\mathbf{n} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1}$). As a result, fluorescence imaging visualized the orientation of actin filaments in each sample. This result demonstrates the ability of the proposed chamber to culture myoblasts with multi-DoF stimulation, allowing for the observation of immunostaining in cells. Future evaluations will be conducted with a larger sample size.

title: Federated Reinforcement Learning for PV Inverters Enabled Voltage Regulation of Medium-Low Voltage Unbalanced Distribution Grids
authors: Xiao Liu, Youbo Liu, Yongdong Chen, Zhiyuan Tang, Hongjun Gao, Zhengbo Li
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: To guarantee operational security in distribution grids by PV inverter scheduling, conventional cloud-based methods require massive data transmission, causing high bandwidth consumption and privacy issues. This study develops an federated reinforcement learning (FRL)-based approach for PV power regulation to enhance distribution grid security. Each low-voltage (LV) station acts as an agent that coordinates local PV inverters via reinforcement learning. A decentralized training framework is developed using edge computing and federated learning, where each LV station is equivalent to a load node via a linear power flow model. Agents learn voltage control strategies using local data and shared model parameters without raw data exchange. This structure preserves data privacy, reduces communication overhead, and weakens the data-sharing dependence of traditional RL. Simulation results validate the superior voltage control performance of FRL.

title: Surface-Tension-Controlled Molding of Large Suction Cup Arrays for Adaptive Adhesion
authors: Qifan Yu, Kaitlyn Becker
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: In this paper, we present a scalable and low-cost fabrication process that uses surface-tension-controlled (STC) molding to generate suction cups, enabling adaptive adhesion of soft robotic surfaces through large suction cup arrays. The STC molding process leverages the surface tension of liquid rubbers to form fillets, or menisci, around open molds and fixtures. The menisci create smooth and curved surfaces that are used as both the molding surfaces and the bodies of suction cups, allowing us to generate both push-on and vacuum-powered suction cups in large arrays. This STC molding process allows us to create larger numbers of suction cups in a single array, supported by a soft underlying body without complex or manually intensive molding processes. Direct integration of suction cups into a soft underlying body and vacuum manifold can allow the arrays to adapt to irregular, compliant, and deforming surfaces. We explore the potential uses of STC molding for applications like bio tagging, manipulation, and locomotion by prototyping a sheet with 70 push-on suction cups, a 19 -cup vacuum-powered grasper, and a tube feet array with 20 suction cups on their tips. The fully soft underlying body and vacuum manifold allows the large arrays of suction cups to conform and thus adapt to irregular, compliant, and deforming surfaces.

title: Case Studies Surveying with Augmented Reality Adopting Technical and Employer Based Data for Maintenance and Performance at Diversified Areas
authors: Krishna Priya M, Angeline Prasanna G, Sangeetha M, Gayathri R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Trends in Advanced Computing (ICCTAC)
abstract: Augmented reality (AR) is a visual based interactive technology which is helping people from kids to employees with their respective learning and working areas. This paper discusses the role of augmented reality is in supporting people geographical boundaries. It also highlights how augmented reality is applied with various domains such as educational, medical, industrial, sales, product launching with automobile and other electronic deliverables. The augmented reality area is empowering the work force with better visual learning methods during training. The case study on automobile industry is done, from which the results are analyzed and derived. It shows the better executed data results in number, less manual work involved, easy documentation and practicing training sessions in real world scenario. The best results of implementing and using augmented reality in many application areas shows the better return on investment (ROI).

title: Real-Time Simulation of Deformable Tactile Sensors and Objects in Robotic Grasping using Graph Neural Networks with Inductive Biases
authors: Guillaume Duret, Frederik Heller, Danylo Mazurak, Tim Schneider, Alap Kshirsagar, Florence Zara, Jan Peters, Liming Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: Physical simulation of deformable bodies is crucial for robotic manipulation, particularly for applications involving deformable objects and deformable tactile sensors. While Finite Element Method (FEM) simulations provide high accuracy for modeling deformable objects and tactile sensors, they are prohibitively expensive for real-time applications, with simulation times often exceeding practical limits for robotic control and learning. This paper presents a novel Graph Neural Network (GNN) framework that accelerates the simulation of tactile sensors by factors of $10^{2}-10^{4}$ compared to FEM, while maintaining high physical accuracy. Our approach addresses limitations in existing GNN-based physics learning through inductive biases. The key contributions include: (1) extending FEM simulation to deformable tactile sensors in grasping scenarios, (2) incorporating novel inductive biases through tetrahedral features and global graph features to improve information propagation and training stability, and (3) demonstrating the first successful application of GNN simulation for tactile sensors with generalization to unseen objects. Additionally, the inductive biases reduce prediction errors by up to 45% compared to baseline approaches. This work enables real-time simulation of soft tactile sensors and deformable objects for robotic applications, including deformation and stress prediction. All simulation and training code can be found at https://tacgraspnets.github.io/.

title: Cooperative Dynamic Positioning for Multiple Uncrewed Surface Vehicles Under Switching-Edge Deception Attacks
authors: Lili Li, Mengjie Li, Hong Sang, Bo Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal and Information Processing over Networks
abstract: This paper investigates cooperative dynamic positioning (CDP) for multiple uncrewed surface vehicles (USVs) under switching-edge deception attacks (SEDAs). The SEDAs can not only disconnect connected links but also fabricate malicious connections where no physical link exists. Existing secure control schemes, which predominantly concentrate on data tampering and disruptions in communication edges, become ineffectual for this new SEDAs. These existing control schemes lack the ability to detect and respond to this attack pattern that manipulates the communication edge. To address SEDAs, an injected edge energy function is used to construct a model that can accurately describe the changes in communication edges during this attack. Then, a detection mechanism is developed to effectively monitor the reliability of communication edges and data. Finally, based on this detection mechanism, the cooperative controller is designed to ensure CDP of multiple USVs. The validity and outperformance of the secure control scheme are confirmed by an example simulation.

title: From Twitter to Reddit: Cross-Domain Indonesian Sarcasm Detection with Pretrained Transformers
authors: Auryn Larissa Sabrina, Darren Cornelius Citra Wijaya, Meiliana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: Sarcasm is a complex linguistic phenomenon that challenges natural language processing (NLP) systems, especially when models are transferred across online platforms with different linguistic styles. This study examines the cross-domain generalization ability of transformer-based models for Indonesian sarcasm detection, focusing on how a Twitter-trained sarcasm model performs on Reddit data. Using the w11wo/reddit_indonesia_sarcastic dataset containing 14,116 annotated comments, three pretrained models were fine-tuned and evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and macro-F1 metrics: XLM-RoBERTa-base, IndoBERT-base, and Multilingual BERT. Experimental results show that XLMRoBERTa achieves 0.8052 accuracy and 0.7162 macro-F1, IndoBERT attains the best performance with 0.8067 accuracy and 0.7224 macro-F1, while Multilingual BERT records the lowest scores with 0.7766 accuracy and 0.6883 macro-F1. These findings indicate that transformer-based can capture sarcasm cues across Indonesian social media platforms, while also emphasizing the need for domain adaptation to improve robustness in handling subtle sarcastic expressions.
