title: Fast and Adaptive Near-field Scanning Measurement Based on Complete Meshing Algorithm and Field Spatial Fluctuation Criterion
authors: Quan Huang, Yangyang Zhou, Yusong Xie, Zhentao Huang, Wenxiao Fang, Lei Wang, Guoguang Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This article presents a fast and adaptive near-field scanning measurement based on complete meshing algorithm and field spatial fluctuation criterion. The scanning approach shows a remarkable advantage over traditional methods in reducing the total scanning time and the number of scanned points while maintaining the desired constructed accuracy. Before the scanning, the complete meshing algorithm (CMA) is proposed to completely mesh the scanning plane into a series of disjoint sets, with the grid points within each set uniformly distributed across the scanning plane to ensure diversity during exploration. In the scanning, a field spatial fluctuation (gradient and variance change) criterion in the variable neighborhood is proposed for scanning selection. can balance exploration and exploitation with high efficiency, with all hyperparameters set adaptively. The proposed scanning measurement approach is validated in both simulation and experiment of near-field scanning, and the efficiency and accuracy outperforms conventional approaches in both efficiency and construction accuracy under the same constrain conditions. Experimental results show that, on a scanning plane with 181×181 grid points, the proposed scanning measurement method reduces scanning time by 98% compared to full scanning and by 45% relative to greedy scanning, with a total computation time of only 8.56 seconds. Moreover, the constructed field achieves a structural similarity index (SSIM) of 0.90 with respect to the full scanning while using merely 0.37% of the total measurement points.

title: Performance Analysis of RIS-Aided NOMA-Based Backscatter Communication with Imperfect CSI and SIC
authors: Yongjun Xu, Wenming Lv, Hao Ni, Qianbin Chen, Chongwen Huang, Zishan Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA)-based backscatter communication has shown a great potential for improving spectral efficiency and reducing power consumption for large-scale connectivity. On the other hand, reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) has emerged as a preeminent technology to overcome the impact of obstacle blocking and achieve smart wireless communications. In this paper, we focus on the performance analysis problem for a RIS-aided NOMA-based backscatter communication system with imperfect channel state information (CSI) and successive interference cancellation (SIC), where a base station sends communication signals to multiple users with the aid of RIS. To evaluate the impacts of imperfect CSI and SIC on system performance, the probability density function and cumulative distribution function of the cascaded channels under Nakagami-m fading models are derived based on the method of moments and the central limit theorem. Furthermore, the closed-form expressions of users' outage probability and ergodic capacity are obtained under imperfect CSI and imperfect SIC. Simulation results show an excellent match between the analytical and simulation results, which verifies the accuracy of the derived expressions. Moreover, the results demonstrate the superiority of NOMA over orthogonal multiple access and indicate that the system performance can be improved by adjusting the reflection amplitude coefficients, the number of RIS reflecting elements, and the target rates.

title: Miniaturized 28-GHz Bandpass Filter Using Self-Coupled Structure in IPD Process
authors: Dongzhe Pan, Qiangqiang Xie, Jun Liu, Bin You
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: A compact quasi-elliptic bandpass filter (BPF) based on self-coupled quarter-wavelength resonators (SCQWRs) is proposed. The SCQWR is realized by vertically folding a microstrip line to form a self-coupled structure, which significantly reduces the circuit footprint and enhances the mixed coupling between resonators, enabling a quasi-elliptic response with two transmission zeros (TZs). Source–load coupling is further introduced through layout optimization to generate an additional controllable TZ. An equivalent circuit model is developed to explain the operating mechanism of the filter. For experimental verification, a second-order quasi-elliptic BPF is designed and fabricated based on the GaAs-IPD process. The fabricated filter exhibits a center frequency of 28 GHz, an insertion loss of 2.16 dB, and a 3-dB fractional bandwidth (FBW) of 12.9%. The maximum stopband attenuation is 41 dB with a compact core area of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.034~\lambda _{0} \times 0.025 \lambda _{0}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>.

title: LLM-Meta-SR: In-Context Learning for Evolving Selection Operators in Symbolic Regression
authors: Hengzhe Zhang, Qi Chen, Bing Xue, Wolfgang Banzhaf, Mengjie Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Evolutionary Computation
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have revolutionized algorithm development, yet their application in symbolic regression, where algorithms automatically discover symbolic expressions from data, remains limited. In this paper, we propose a meta-learning framework that enables LLMs to automatically design selection operators for evolutionary symbolic regression algorithms. We first identify two key limitations in existing LLM-based algorithm evolution techniques: lack of semantic guidance and code bloat. The absence of semantic awareness can lead to ineffective exchanges of useful code components, while bloat results in unnecessarily complex components; both can hinder evolutionary learning progress or reduce the interpretability of the designed algorithm. To address these issues, we enhance the LLM-based evolution framework for meta-symbolic regression with two key innovations: a complementary, semantics-aware selection operator and bloat control. Additionally, we embed domain knowledge into the prompt, enabling the LLM to generate more effective and contextually relevant selection operators. Our experimental results on symbolic regression benchmarks show that LLMs can devise selection operators that outperform nine expert-designed baselines, achieving state-of-the-art performance. Moreover, the evolved operator can further improve a state-of-the-art symbolic regression algorithm, achieving the best performance among 28 symbolic regression and other machine learning algorithms across 116 regression datasets. This demonstrates that LLMs can exceed expert-level algorithm design for symbolic regression.

title: Result Identification and Error Repair Method for Generator Stator Ground Fault Active Arc Suppression
authors: Yikai Wang, Chenxiao Tang, Jian Qiao, Haibin Huang, Yuanhao Liu, Tong Wang, Zengping Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: The active arc suppression method based on an external injection source is now the primary technique for mitigating the hazards of generator single-phase ground faults. It can precisely compensate for the double-frequency fault current caused by ground faults. However, when the injected compensation is inaccurate, the method may fail to suppress the arc or even worsen the fault. Current research lacks methods for identifying active arc suppression results and the corresponding error repair. This paper proposes a method for identifying arc suppression results and error repair in generator stator ground faults based on variation in phase leakage current. The identification criterion is built on the variation characteristic of the phase leakage current during the entire fault process. This allows accurate judgment of the arc suppression results, with differentiated treatment strategies applied accordingly. In cases of unsuccessful arc suppression, the compensation error is calculated, and compensation is dynamically adjusted for reliable suppression. PSCAD/EMTDC simulation platform tests and dynamic model experiments verify that the proposed arc suppression result identification method can accurately identify the arc suppression result, and that the error of the arc suppression error calculated by the proposed error repair method is below 1%, which validates the accuracy and engineering practicality of the proposed method.

title: An ENS-Based Three-Channel Receiver for Null-Free Power Pickup in Multipolar UAV-IPT Systems
authors: Yongkang Qiao, Yueshi Guan, Jianwei Mai, Yijie Wang, Dianguo Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This study addresses the critical challenge of coupling nulls in multipolar inductive wireless power transfer (IPT) systems for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). A novel receiver architecture is proposed, integrating an orthogonal three-channel coil structure with a two-stage Euclidean Norm Synthesizer (ENS). In contrast to conventional methods, the proposed system captures the three-dimensional magnetic field vector and synthesizes its Euclidean norm to achieve spatially continuous power pickup. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed design effectively eliminates the coupling nulls inherent in conventional dual-channel receivers. Furthermore, compared to the three-channel linear summation topology, the proposed ENS-based design significantly suppresses output voltage ripple by approximately 28.09% (reducing it from 48.55% to 20.46%). Moreover, the proposed system maintains a high mean transfer efficiency of 90.92%, yielding a robust, null-free, and low-ripple power supply suitable for reliable autonomous UAV charging.
