abstract: Scientific cameras are responsible for a large number of high-precision observation tasks in fields such as astronomy and space exploration. High-speed and stable data transmission can significantly improve observation efficiency and facilitate real-time acquisition and analysis of scientific data. The primary camera as the core imaging module of modern large telescopes often adopts sensor mosaicking technologies to ensure both the telescope’s field of view and imaging precision. Simultaneous data acquisition from multiple sensor modules leads to an exponential increase in data volume, imposing higher requirements on system bandwidth, low latency, and data integrity. In this paper, a reliable data transmission protocol tailored for mosaic cameras is designed based on UDP, together with a high-performance flow control algorithm. Ultimately, a customized transmission system oriented to mosaic cameras is implemented. The proposed system achieves a reliable effective throughput of 9.5 Gbps over a 10-gigabit link, reaching 95% of the link capacity, while the protocol is simplified compared to TCP, making it more suitable for implementation on FPGA-based data acquisition systems. The proposed system has been successfully deployed on the primary camera of the Wide Field Survey Telescope (WFST), supporting daily survey operations and the acquisition of large-scale scientific data.

title: Charge collection prediction and analysis in 3nm FinFET using the ambipolar diffusion with a-cutoff model
authors: Sayah El Hajji, Gilles Gasiot, Thomas Thery, Vincent Correas, Nicholas J. Pieper, Yoni Xiong, Jenna B. Kronenberg, Jean-Luc Autran, Bharat L. Bhuva, Davide Pandini, Victor Malherbe, Philippe Roche
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Simulation of the radiation response of advanced 3D CMOS technologies, such as FinFET and Gate-all-around technologies, requires more expressive charge transport and collection models. This is necessary to adapt to complex geometries, while keeping computational efficiency suitable for industrial Monte-Carlo workloads. In this work, the ambipolar diffusion with a-cutoff (ADC) model is reviewed and identified as a suitable candidate for such purpose. For the first time, a practical implementation able to simulate seamlessly complex and various standard cells is proposed. This refined model is then integrated in the industrial Monte-Carlo simulator TIARA and applied to 3nm FinFET flip-flop designs. Charge collection mechanisms in FinFET are discussed, along with the influence of process and design features, including comparison between FinFET generations.

title: Event-Triggered Formation Control of Quadrotor UAVs With Adaptive Finite-Time Disturbance Observer Under Switching Topologies
authors: Yang Hu, Zhiqiang Miao, Dongbo Zhang, Yaonan Wang, Wei He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article investigates distributed formation tracking control for multiple quadrotor UAVs subject to switching topologies with external disturbances and mass uncertainties. We propose an adaptive finite-time disturbance observer combining time-varying bandwidth with fractional-power correction terms to outperform conventional linear observers. A Lyapunov-based event-triggered mechanism governs topology switching via formation energy metrics, guaranteeing minimum dwell time and finite switching frequency. Stability analysis characterizes convergence rates for both fixed and switching topologies through Lyapunov construction. Simulations comparing against four recent baselines demonstrate up to an 80% reduction in formation error, while hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) experiments validate the real-time implementability and robustness of the proposed framework under embedded implementation constraints.

title: Cognitive Advantage: How Artificial Intelligence is Redefining Competitive Advantage
authors: Xavier Ferràs-Hernández
publish: IEEE Engineering Management Review
abstract: The rapid diffusion of artificial intelligence (AI), and particularly large language models (LLMs), is challenging established theories of competitive advantage by introducing non-human cognitive capabilities into the core of organizational decision-making. While existing research largely conceptualizes AI as a digital resource or an enabler of operational efficiency, this article argues that such perspectives fail to capture its deeper strategic implications. We propose the concept of “cognitive advantage” as a distinct and emergent form of competitive advantage, defined as an organization's systematic ability to perceive, interpret, and decide better than competitors through the deep integration of AI systems, proprietary data, and human judgment. Adopting an exploratory and inductive research design, the study analyzes a set of recent, illustrative cases of global firms across multiple industries—including consultancy, banking, retail, mobility, pharmaceuticals, and aerospace—that have partnered with major AI suppliers since 2023. Drawing on insights from organizational cognition, behavioral strategy, and AI research, the analysis identifies four interrelated abilities through which cognitive advantage materializes: augmented perception, superior interpretation, reduced decisional latency, and cumulative organizational learning. The article contributes to strategy and innovation research by positioning cognitive advantage as a new typology of competitive advantage that complements, but cannot be reduced to, existing frameworks such as the resource-based view or dynamic capabilities. For managers, the findings highlight the need to move beyond digital transformation toward deliberate cognitive transformation as a source of sustained competitive performance.

title: A Twin-FeFET MLC CIM with Enhanced Voltage and Process Variation Tolerance for Edge Intelligence MAC and AP
authors: Zhihao Li, Shuo Yang, Lubin Xiang, Xiaobo Gong, Xiaohang Chen, Wei Hu, Zhiting Lin, Xiulong Wu, Chunyu Peng
publish: IEEE Journal on Emerging and Selected Topics in Circuits and Systems
abstract: This paper introduces a voltage and process variation-tolerant non-volatile computing-in-memory (CIM) architecture based on the co-design of neural networks and circuit implementations. By exploiting the adjustable threshold voltage (VTH) of ferroelectric field-effect transistors (FeFETs), the proposed architecture features an active square-law compensation mechanism within a 2-FeFET-1-Transistor-1-Capacitor (2F-1T-1C) single cell (S-Cell) to perform highly linear multiply-accumulate (MAC) computations. To further enable high-precision operations and global average pooling (AP), we propose a twin-cell (T-Cell) extension (FeMAPIM). The T-Cell leverages an asymmetric capacitive ratio (4:1) that not only drastically reduces the average per-cell capacitor area overhead but also acts as a spatial filter for variations. The architecture is comprehensively evaluated across cell and array levels. System-level assessments on VGG-GAP, ResNet-20, and MobileNetV2 using CIFAR datasets confirm that the proposed macro tightly suppresses hardware-induced accuracy degradation, delivering highly robust inference for edge intelligence.

title: Multi-Armed Bandit Learning for ISAC Systems in Time-Varying Environments
authors: Giorgio Taricco
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems aim to jointly perform data transmission and environmental sensing, leading to coupled and often conflicting design objectives. This paper investigates a lightweight online learning framework for ISAC based on multi-armed bandit (MAB) algorithms. A finite beam codebook is used for sequential beam selection, while the sensing–communication trade-off is captured through a normalized reward combining communication rate and a CRLB-based sensing metric. To address time-varying environments, a sliding-window UCB strategy is adopted for beam selection and the trade-off parameter is adapted online on a slower timescale. Numerical results under abrupt changes, gradual drift, and multiple change points show that the proposed approach tracks environmental variations and improves dynamic-regret performance over fixed-α and stationary baselines.

title: ASP-DAC 2026: Strengthening Asia’s Expanding Role in Design Automation Research
authors: Tsung-Yi Ho, Seokhyeong Kang, Takashi Sato
publish: IEEE Design & Test
abstract: The 31st Asia and South Pacific Design Automation Conference (ASP-DAC 2026) was held on 19--22 January 2026, at the Hong Kong Disneyland Hotel. As the premier international forum for electronic design automation (EDA) and VLSI systems research in the Asia and South Pacific region, ASPDAC continues to play a critical role in advancing methodologies that span from device technologies to system-level design.

title: A Feynman-Sprinkler-Like Electromagnetic Wind Energy Harvester for High-Speed Wind Fields
authors: Jiafeng Sun, Yuanyi Wang, Xie Xie, Ying Gong, Li Jin, Wen Zhao, Yan Peng, Biao Wang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Distributed Internet of Things (IoT) nodes powered by wind energy harvesters (WEHs) have enabled long-term, maintenance-free environmental monitoring in remote and extreme scenarios. However, the large fluctuations in wind speed may cause structural fatigue and dynamical instability of conventional WEHs. To achieve stable and durable operation under high wind speeds (up to 25 m/s), this study reports a Feynman-sprinkler-like WEH (FSL-WEH). In contrast to conventional WEHs that rely on turbine blades directly driven by the wind, the FSL-WEH harnesses the reaction force generated by airflow expelled through Feynman nozzles to produce rotational motion. Simulation and experiments indicate that the FSL-WEH achieves a maximum power output of 4.34 W and a peak power density of 66.77 kW/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> at a wind speed of 25 m/s. Moreover, the FSL-WEH successfully powers an environmental monitoring system, which wirelessly transmits temperature and humidity data to connected terminals, validating the operational reliability and durability of the device in extremely high wind speeds and high-humidity conditions. This study provides a promising solution for scavenging wind energy to power distributed environmental sensor networks under high-wind, high-humidity conditions.

title: A novel unstressed chipless RFID sensor based on metamaterial absorbers for crack sensing and IoT applications
authors: Kang Jiang, Songtao Xue, Liyu Xie, Guochun Wan, Zhuoran Yi
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Metamaterial absorbers, an emerging technology, are predominantly employed to reduce the radar cross section (RCS) of objects. This paper presents a novel unstressed chipless RFID sensor based on metamaterial absorbers, enabling passive wireless monitoring of crack widths on highly reflective metal surfaces without the requirement of background calibration. The proposed sensor integrates two elements based on a U-shaped resonator array, whose relative displacement is employed to characterize the crack width, effectively addressing the defect that the sensors based on monolithic patch are prone to fracture under force when monitoring large deformation. In wireless interrogation, the sensor utilizes the square loop consisting of two U-shaped resonators to absorb electromagnetic waves, encoding crack width information in the reflected signal's spectrum, thereby cleverly avoiding the interference of the reflected signal to the conventional chipless RFID backscattered signal. The equivalent circuit model of the sensor is established to analyze the absorption mechanism and sensing principle. Both simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the sensor’s resonant frequency exhibits an approximately linear increase corresponding to the widening of the crack. Furthermore, the experimental results also prove that increasing the number of sensor’s unit cells can enhances the signal absorption capacity, deepens the notch depth of reflected signals, and consequently extends the wireless reading distance. The proposed sensor based on metamaterial absorbers has the advantages of simple structure, low cost and stable wireless reading performance, which provides a competitive scheme for large-scale distributed sensor deployment in Internet of Things (IoT) and structural health monitoring (SHM).

title: SAMT-VIR: Visual-Inertial-Ranging Localization Based on Single-Anchor-Multi-Tag
authors: Kaiwen Yu, Boli Zhou, Hongbin Fang, Jian Xu
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Accurate and robust localization is essential for autonomous robotic systems operating in infrastructure-limited environments. Visual–inertial odometry (VIO) provides high short-term accuracy but suffers from cumulative drift, while ultra-wideband (UWB) ranging offers absolute distance constraints yet requires multiple anchors or careful deployment. To address these limitations, this paper presents SAMT-VIR, a Single-Anchor Multi-Tag Visual–Inertial–Ranging localization framework that achieves drift-bounded pose estimation with minimal environmental infrastructure. The proposed system tightly couples monocular vision, inertial sensing, and high-frequency multi-tag UWB ranging within a unified factor-graph optimization. Robot states are propagated to UWB measurement timestamps to fully exploit dense range observations; simultaneously, UWB tag-to-body extrinsics are explicitly modeled, and the anchor position is determined through optimization, thereby eliminating systematic ranging biases and enabling automatic anchor self-calibration. A UWB measurement validation mechanism, together with a relative transformation constraint is further introduced to enhance robustness under degraded UWB conditions. Extensive evaluations on public datasets and real-world indoor and outdoor experiments demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms state-of-the-art VIO and VIR approaches in localization accuracy, drift suppression, and robustness, while maintaining real-time performance on embedded hardware. These results validate the effectiveness of multi-tag extrinsic-aware UWB fusion for reliable localization in practical robotic deployments.

title: Sono-Magnetic Synergistically Enhanced Electroactive Bacteria Sensors for Rapid Water Toxicity Detection
authors: Huaifeng Cui, Guixiong Liu, Yanhong Ge
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Electroactive bacteria (EAB) sensors show significant potential for water toxicity monitoring due to their intuitive signal generation and integrative sensitivity to complex contaminants. However, their performance is often constrained by limited membrane permeability, slow biofilm development, and uneven biofilm distribution. To address these limitations, this study proposes a sono–magnetic synergistic optimization strategy based on simultaneous ultrasonic pretreatment of bacteria and samples combined with magnetic–field–induced in situ biofilm formation, and elucidates the enhancement mechanisms through integrated multiphysics simulations and experiments. At the cellular scale, a coupled fluid–bubble–cell membrane model demonstrates that low-frequency ultrasound induces localized stress concentration and transient membrane deformation, thereby markedly enhancing transmembrane mass transfer. Experimentally, at a power density of 0.3 W/cm², the toxicity response inhibition rate (IR) increases from 16.46% (no ultrasound condition) to 31.58%. At the interfacial scale, comparative analyses show that porous magnetic electrodes, relative to integral and array configurations, provide superior magnetic–field uniformity and promote the formation of continuous electroactive biofilms with more uniform thickness; under sono–magnetic synergy, the IR is further increased to 39.5%. Overall, the proposed strategy achieves coordinated optimization from the cellular microscale to the electrode–interface scale, significantly enhancing sensor sensitivity and stability. This work provides a solid theoretical foundation and technical support for the development of high-performance devices for online water toxicity monitoring.

title: An 18.4Gb/s/pin Simultaneous Bidirectional Transceiver for Post HBM4 Using 4-Phase Hybrid Timing Alignment and Dual Equalization
authors: Minchang Kim, Hankyu Chi, Sooncheol Kwon, Icsu Oh, Seulgi Kim, Heewoong Song, Daeho Yun, Jaehong Jeong, Daejoon Kim, Kichul Hong, Jeonghyun Park, Woo-Seok Choi, Hae-Kang Jung, Hoyoung Son, Inchul Jeong, Hyungsoo Kim, Jonghwan Kim
publish: IEEE Solid-State Circuits Letters
abstract: This letter presents a Simultaneous Bi-Directional (SBD) transceiver for post HBM4. It is difficult to increase the data rate due to poor channel characteristics of the silicon interposer and limited physical area of IO in HBM interface. SBD signaling is attractive because it doubles the per-pin bandwidth without increasing the data rate. The SBD transceiver was demonstrated using 4-phase hybrid timing alignment and dual equalization. The bidirectional signals are aligned on both sides through CH reach optimization at the target frequency. This simplifies the bidirectional signals on both sides and reduces echo effects. The hybrid scheme using 4-phase clocking mitigates RX margin reduction due to TX replica mismatch. And dual equalization scheme is proposed to compensate bidirectional ISI of SBD signal. The prototype transceiver is implemented using RDL FOWLP (fan-out wafer level package) and fabricated in a 1b-nm DRAM process with 70um bump pitch such as HBM4. The transceiver supports the SBD operation up to 18.4Gb/s/pin on 6mm traces, similar in length to HBM4 system. The transceiver achieves the highest per-pin bandwidth compared to previous works.

title: Distributed Non-Uniform Scaling Control of Multi-Agent Formation via Matrix-Valued Constraints
authors: Tao He, Gangshan Jing
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: Distributed formation maneuver control refers to the problem of maneuvering a group of agents to change their formation shape by adjusting the motions of partial agents, where the controller of each agent only requires local information measured from its neighbors. Although this problem has been extensively investigated, existing approaches are mostly limited to uniform scaling transformations. This article proposes a new type of local matrix-valued constraints, via which non-uniform scaling control of position formation can be achieved by tuning the positions of only two agents (i.e., leaders). Here, the non-uniform scaling transformation refers to global scaling the position formation with different ratios along different orthogonal coordinate directions. Moreover, by defining scaling and translation of attitudes, we propose a distributed control scheme for scaling and translation maneuver control of joint position-attitude formations. It is proven that the proposed controller achieves global convergence, provided that the sensing graph among agents is a 2-rooted bidirectional graph. Compared with the affine formation maneuver control approach, the proposed approach leverages a sparser sensing graph, requires fewer leaders, and additionally enables scaling transformations of the attitude formation. A simulation example demonstrates our theoretical results.

title: Adversarially Robust Quantum-Classical Neural Network for Malware Detection
authors: Kathan P. Vyas, Dev K. Patel, Rahul Tanna, Sameer G. Kulkarni
publish: Proceedings of the Workshop on Advances in Malware Analysis and Software Security
abstract: None

title: A Hybrid Centralized and Decentralized Framework for Traffic Management in Advanced Air Mobility
authors: Alexandra Newcomb, Omar Ochoa
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: The dynamic and safe routing of aircraft within Unmanned Aircraft Systems Traffic Management (UTM) is a key issue for the development of Advanced Air Mobility (AAM). The future framework of AAM will involve many autonomous vehicles occupying the same airspace with differing levels of priority, requiring scalable, efficient, and safe solutions. Machine Learning (ML) is a key candidate for this area, specifically through the usage of Reinforcement Learning (RL) for dynamically routing aircraft and resolving potential spatial and temporal conflicts that occur. However, to incorporate a system utilizing ML into the safety-critical environment of AAM, rigorous guarantees of safety are necessary to maintain the operational safety of the National Airspace System. Therefore, formal methods must be explored to build a safety harness around the RL model. This theoretical paper provides a survey of current solutions in the areas of RL for UTM, Safe RL, and Safe RL for UTM. After establishing the current state of the art and providing necessary background information, a Hybrid Centralized and Decentralized (HC/Dc) framework is proposed integrating formal methods into Deep RL to dynamically and safely route AAM traffic. The scope of this work focuses on concept development for a dynamic and safe Air Traffic Management framework for AAM. The centralized component handles pre-tactical flight planning, whereas the decentralized component performs tactical low-level conflict resolution. Both components integrate RL and formal methods to ensure efficiency and safety. In the future, the proposed framework will be validated through a series of simulation experiments on a proof-of-concept prototype.

title: Acoustic Rhizome Fungal Infection Detection for Turmeric
authors: T. Jayachandran, Dhashnamoorthi P, Kelvin Mc'Donald N, Mithreshwaran S M
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Early detection of fungal infections on the rhizomes of the turmeric crop is significant in avoiding the deterioration of the crop. In the conventional approach, the condition of the rhizome is checked by manually digging the plant and then checking the condition. This approach affects the growth of the crop, requires more manual work, and also harms the crop. In order to avoid the drawbacks associated with the conventional approach, a vibration method is suggested for the detection of fungal infections on the turmeric crop without digging the plant from the soil. The suggested method uses a pointed probe inserted into the soil with controlled mechanical vibrations. The response to these vibrations can be captured using a sensor module, and the analysis of the changes in the pattern of the signal can be made. It can be noted that the rhizomes that are healthy are firm, while the rhizomes that are infected with fungal growth will experience softening. Therefore, the pattern of the response to the vibrations can be different for the healthy rhizomes compared to the infected ones. This can help in the early detection of the infection even before the symptoms appear on the surface. Therefore, the proposed method can help in minimizing the disturbance to the crops and the overall labour requirement.

title: Enhancing Distribution System Resilience via Adaptive Control Mode Switching of Soft Open Points
authors: Shunyao Zhang, Yiheng Bian, Di Zhang, Gengfeng Li
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: Extreme weather events pose severe threats to the secure operation of new distribution systems. Although Soft Open Points (SOPs) are increasingly recognized for their flexibility, existing post-disaster restoration models often treat them as idealized power transfer channels, neglecting their comprehensive power electronic characteristics under extreme conditions. To bridge this research gap, this paper first proposes a novel mathematical model of SOPs that rigorously characterizes their inherent power electronic dynamics. By formulating these characteristics, the model captures both the instantaneous fault-blocking response for precise electrical isolation at the moment of impact and the adaptive control mode transitions for voltage support within load islands and inter-feeder power regulation throughout the recovery process. Subsequently, a multi-resource coordinated recovery strategy driven by these power electronic behaviors is developed within a two-stage framework, consisting of the resist and response stage and the restoration stage. This strategy achieves deep operational synergy among SOPs, distributed generators (DGs), remote-controlled switches (RCSs), and circuit breakers (CBs). Simulation results on a modified IEEE 70-node system verify that the proposed strategy effectively resists fault impacts, minimizes the fault-affected area, and minimizes load shedding losses, significantly enhancing overall resilience compared to traditional methods.

title: Behavior Analysis of Charged Lunar Regolith Simulants Lifted by Metal Wheel in Vacuum Environment
authors: Tadashi Matsuura, Hiroaki Kawamura, Shinichiro Noda, Nobutaka Tanishima, Natsu Fujioka
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: This paper investigates the lofting behavior of lunar regolith simulant particles caused by wheel rotation under vacuum conditions, aiming to support the design and operation of the Pressurized Rover (PR) for future lunar missions such as NASA's Artemis program. Lunar dust, characterized by its fine, abrasive texture and strong adhesion driven by van der Waals and electrostatic forces, presents significant challenges to rover reliability during long-duration missions. Understanding how regolith behaves under lunar-like conditions is essential for predicting dust dispersion and mitigating its impact on critical systems. To characterize the mechanical properties of the LHS-1 regolith simulant, two experiments were conducted under vacuum: a dynamic angle of repose test and a uniaxial compression test. The dynamic angle of repose test revealed that particle behavior under vacuum differs markedly from atmospheric conditions, particularly at higher rotational speeds. While angles of repose remained stable in vacuum beyond 10 rpm, they decreased significantly in air, indicating fluidization effects caused by atmospheric drag. Similarly, avalanche angle trends diverged between the two environments. The uniaxial compression test showed that the unconfined yield strength (UYS) of the simulant decreases under vacuum, likely due to the absence of humidity and liquid bridge forces that enhance cohesion in air. Using these experimental results, Discrete Element Method (DEM) simulations were calibrated through global optimization using the Differential Evolution (DE) algorithm. A scaled wheel test was then performed inside a vacuum chamber, and particle motion was visualized using Particle Image Velocimetry (PIV). Observations revealed two distinct behaviors: rearward ejection immediately after wheel touchdown and upward lofting of particles during rotation. The upward motion is hypothesized to result from triboelectric charging between the wheel and simulant, generating Coulomb forces that, combined with centrifugal forces, influence particle trajectories. Coupled DEM-Multi-Body Dynamics (MBD) simulations successfully reproduced major scattering patterns but did not replicate the circumferential motion observed in physical tests, likely due to limitations in particle size and electrostatic modeling. A force-balance analysis suggests that particles in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15-21 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> range experience a balance between Coulomb and centrifugal forces, enabling levitation near the wheel surface. This study highlights the complex interplay of mechanical, electrostatic, and dynamic forces governing dust behavior on the lunar surface. The findings contribute to a better understanding of dust-related risks for lunar rover systems and emphasize the need for further research on triboelectric charging under vacuum using actual rover materials, as well as refinement of simulation models to improve predictive accuracy.

title: Cognitwin: A Neuro-Digital Synthesis for Predictive Decision Intelligence in Smart Ecosystems
authors: Frederick Ruby Helen, Kattupalli Sudhakar, Rajendra Kodamanchili, Mamta Bansal, Naveen Kumar Sanku, Lakshmana Phaneendra Maguluri
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: The integration of Digital Twins (DTs) with cognitive modeling presents a novel paradigm for enhancing intelligent decision-making in complex, real-time environments. In this paper, Cognitwin, a hybrid neuro-digital model is presented that combines physical systems high-fidelity digital replicas with human-like cognitive processing mechanisms to allow proactive, explainable, and adaptive decision-making. The system architecture integrates physicsbased and data-driven modeling, cognitive architectures (e.g., ACT-R), as well as reinforcement learning and graph neural networks (GNNs) to produce actionable intelligence. Uncertainty-aware perception relies on sensor fusion and DempserShafer theory, whereas the prediction part of learning is based on LSTM and Transformer models. The performance in ten smart areas such as healthcare, mobility and energy has shown better results where the average accuracy is 95.7, F1score of 0.94 and the latency is less than 200 ms in most applications. The importance of the integrated feedback and cognitive modules has shown to be confirmed by an ablation study which has proven that the integrated cognitive and feedback modules are essential in producing strong accuracy. Furthermore, the system has the decision quality rating of 0.94 following the reinforcement learning policy refinement processes. Comparative analysis shows that GNN is better than LSTM in causal reasoning tasks and justifies with lower MSE values and higher causal scores. Cognitwin provides a bidirectional synchronization process that is real time between the physical and the virtual-cognitive layer and creates continuity in learning and in situational awareness.

title: ScaleWave: Breaking Through Resource Bottlenecks to Scale Up Serverless Computing at the Edge
authors: Summit Shrestha, Zheng Song, Eli Tilevich, Zhengquan Li, Christine Julien, Probir Roy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Communications (PerCom)
abstract: As demand grows, serverless computing systems must scale to meet increasing throughput requirements. Cloud computing easily achieves scalability by allocating abundant and elastic resources. In contrast, edge computing pre-deploys scarce and inelastic resources on site. However, edge applications often need to scale dramatically to handle bursty demand. Because they typically serve fewer users with highly variable workloads, our study shows that peak usage may require up to 8× more resources to be pre-deployed across edge nodes than in a centralized cloud. We observe that a typical serverless request can often be satisfied by different implementations, with dissimilar resource consumption profiles. When an edge application fails to scale up, the culprit is often a single bottleneck resource being fully consumed, with other resources readily available. Motivated by this observation, we introduce SCALEWAVE, a middleware for seamlessly scaling up with different implementations to fully utilize all available resources to achieve scalable serverless computing at the edge. Supporting multiple implementations, however, introduces new challenges for conventional auto-scaler designs. Reactive strategies tend to yield suboptimal performance, while proactive methods struggle with compatibility. SCALEWAVE overcomes these limitations by proactively distributing traffic across implementations, while leveraging existing autoscalers to manage instance scaling reactively for each one. Our evaluations using real workload traces on a heterogeneous cluster of edge devices demonstrate that our design allows edge applications to serve 2x more requests with minimal latency and achieve 50% more successful requests during workload bursts — showcasing substantial gains in scalability and resource efficiency.

title: Updated Model of Geometric Effects in Digital Micromirror Devices for Spacecraft Attitude Control
authors: Jonathan Messer
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: This paper presents an updated model for describing the solar radiation pressure force generated by Digital Micromirror Devices for spacecraft attitude control. Specifically, the work expands the solar radiation pressure model to include the effects of self-shadowing and non-incident light pathways occurring at high angles of solar incidence. Using non-sequential ray-tracing simulations in Ansys OpticStudio on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{3} \times \mathrm{3}$</tex> array of micromirrors, this study quantifies the light loss due to interactions between adjacent micromirrors. It estimates the impact on both normal and tangential solar radiation pressure vector components. These results represent a significant step toward high-fidelity force modeling and the eventual realization of precision solar radiation pressure-based attitude control systems.

title: Sign Language Convertor for Deaf and Mute People
authors: C. A. Subasini, S. Ahkshayaa, M. Arumugathai Alias Devi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: The goal of this website is to offer a thorough and entertaining sign language conversion tool specifically made for deaf and mute individuals. Deaf and mute people frequently encounter communication barriers that make it difficult for them to engage with the hearing community in a natural way. In order to close this gap, this project offers a Sign Language Converter that translates gestures in sign language into spoken Gujarati or English and vice versa. The system recognizes and deciphers sign language gestures recorded by a camera using sophisticated machine learning algorithms and computer vision techniques. Additionally, the solution facilitates speech-to-sign and text-to-sign translation, allowing hearing people to interact with the deaf and mute community in an efficient manner. The system uses gesture recognition models that have been trained on datasets of Indian Sign Language (ISL) and American Sign Language (ASL) to provide accurate translations for sign-to-text conversion. To serve a wide range of users, the multilingual feature enables translations in Gujarati and English.

title: Cross-Coupled Pneumatic–Cable–Driven Soft Robotic Arm for Overhead Manipulation
authors: Charles Y. Froessel, Tomoyuki Noda, Hidaka Asai, Tatsuya Teramae, Guanran Pei, Haewon Jeong, Josie Hughes
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: Overhead-suspended robotic arms require both high payload capacity and agile motion, yet soft arms have difficulty achieving these capabilities simultaneously. Conventional approaches have relied either on using multiple anchored soft arms or reinforcing the structure, which inevitably increases stiffness and compromises compliance. We present a three-degree-offreedom pneumatic-cable-driven soft arm that achieves vertical lift with fast actuation, for both soft objects and high payloads while maintaining compliance for safe human interaction. Inspired by human lifting strategies, the arm performs a twophase motion: first forming a compliant S-shaped deformation in selectable vertical planes for under-grasping, and then axially straightening for efficient load transfer. Coordinated pressurization of three pneumatic muscles reproduces both concentric and eccentric contraction behaviors, enabling smooth transition from compliant grasping to stiff lifting. The arm’s trimmed helicoid architecture and cross-coupled cable routing allow each actuator to influence multiple joints and fingers, functioning analogously to multi-articular muscles. The lightweight 3Dprinted prototype was experimentally tested for lifting payloads of up to 10 kg and manipulating soft objects, demonstrating adaptable and robust overhead operation suitable for industrial, agricultural, and assistive applications.

title: Analysing the Predictive Accuracy of Machine Learning Models in Stock Price Forecasting
authors: S. T Surulivel, Nandhini Priya A, Dhivya V, S Selvabaskar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Stock price prediction poses significant difficulties owing to the unpredictable nature of financial markets and constantly shifting price trends. This study investigates and compares three widely-used supervised learning algorithms Linear Regression, Random Forest, and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) with respect to their ability to forecast the following day's stock closing price. A decade-long dataset spanning November 2015 through December 2025, sourced from Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) shares traded on the NSE, forms the basis of this study. Input features include opening price, daily high and low values, trade volume, and previously observed closing prices as lagged variables. Four statistical benchmarks MAE, RMSE, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>, and Directional Accuracy (DA) are adopted to measure and compare the forecasting reliability of each algorithm. The results indicate that Linear Regression outperforms the other two models, recording an MAE of 36.17, RMSE of 49.64, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.98, and DA of 71.54 %. These results suggest that when stock prices exhibit a predominantly linear pattern over time, straightforward regression techniques may surpass sophisticated ensemble-based methods in short-horizon price prediction tasks.

title: Scalable Detection of Fake Product Reviews Using Big Data Analytics
authors: Masum Ahmed Mozumder, Tanvinur Rahman Siam, Md. Sabbir Ahmed Ekhon, Mohammad Shamsul Arefin, M. M. Musharaf Hussain
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: The increasing presence of fake product reviews on e-commerce platforms poses a significant challenge to consumer trust and decision-making. Detecting such reviews becomes difficult when dealing with large-scale data due to processing and storage constraints. This study presents a big data analytics–based approach for fake product review detection using the Hadoop ecosystem and the MapReduce programming model. A large-scale dataset containing approximately 10 million product reviews is processed in a distributed environment to ensure scalability and efficiency. Several machine learning models, including Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Linear Support Vector Machine, Decision Tree, Voting Classifier, and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), are applied to classify reviews as genuine or fake. Experimental results show that the evaluated models achieve accuracies ranging from 73.7% to 94.0%, with Logistic Regression and the MLP demonstrating strong performance on large-scale data. Evaluation using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrices, ROC curves, and precision–recall curves confirms the effectiveness of integrating machine learning techniques with Hadoop-based big data analytics for large-scale fake review detection.

title: A Human-Centered Analysis of AI Awareness: Investigating User Vulnerabilities to Deepfakes and Voice Cloning
authors: Felix Jerome Hidajat, Yana Erlyana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: The rapid advancement of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) has significantly transformed audiovisual content production, yet it has simultaneously enabled the proliferation of deepfakes and voice cloning as potent forms of digital manipulation. While technical detection algorithms continue to evolve, a critical gap remains in understanding the human element of this security threat, specifically the detectionperception gap where algorithmic alerts fail to translate into user resilience. This study provides a human-centered analysis of AI awareness, contributing a validated set of functional requirements for a modular interactive multimedia system designed to mitigate the cognitive vulnerabilities of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=40$</tex> Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z}$</tex> users. The research employs a requirements engineering approach to map “truth bias,” which is the inherent tendency to trust audiovisual stimuli, against the AISAS (Attention, Interest, Search, Action, Share) model. By framing the interaction flow through a Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) lens, the study identifies specific pivot points in information verification. Findings reveal a significant delta in user performance, with a 36 % increase in deepfake detection accuracy and a 47 % increase in voice cloning awareness following interaction with a prototype based on these requirements. Statistical validation using paired t-tests and Cohen's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{d}$</tex> indicates that interactive “side-by-side” comparison modules and cognitive “nudges” effectively bridge the awareness gap. This research contributes to the fields of Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) and Software Engineering by translating cognitive failure points into a structured architectural blueprint for “human firewalls” that prioritize user empowerment over passive technical exposition

title: AI-Enabled MBRT Based System for Rapid Microbial Detection in Milk
authors: V. Anbumani, Prethikaa D, Sanjay S, Sandhiya P
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Milk spoilage is an issue that frequently happens in the dairy supply chain. It is due to the fact that microbe populations can increase very fast, which leads to a loss of safety as well as quality. However, for the most part, the methods of detecting spoilage that are employed nowadays are slow, require a lot of manual checking, and are not suitable for real, time or automated monitoring. The Methylene Blue Reduction Test (MBRT) is one of the tests which determines how fast the milk loses its blue color that is dependent on the microbial content present in milk and spoiled sample loses the color faster. This paper presents an AI, improved MBRT, based system that is aimed to enable fast detection of microbes in milk and is environmentally friendly and energy efficient. The system integrates MBRT with AI and it performs three main steps: image capture, feature extraction, and the prediction of the microbial load. Machine learning algorithms are employed to monitor the change of color in milk samples, while a Random Forest algorithm designates unique IDs to samples and observes their reduction rates over time for the accurate evaluation. This project encompassed raw and processed milk samples kept at 37° C, which were, according to the color change, divided into Fresh, Mildly Spoiled, and Spoiled categories, with quicker transitions indicating a higher microbial count. The processed data are recorded with timestamps in a database, allowing for early spoilage alerts through the machine learning model. The system achieved an average detection accuracy of 96.04 %, with a slight drop in performance under very high microbial density conditions due to overlapping transition phases. However, the system was almost real, time and showed great potential as a scalable and practical tool for quick milk quality monitoring, thus proving the feasibility of combining AI with MBRT for efficient microbial detection.

title: WESAT Mission: Solar Irradiance and UV Index Variability in Space vs Ground in Trivandrum, India
authors: Lizy Abraham, Resmi R, Surya Jayakumar
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: WESAT (Women Engineered SATellite) is India's first women-engineered satellite, launched on January 1, 2024, at 9:10 AM IST with India's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60^{\text{th}}$</tex> PSLV mission PSLV-C58 from Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC), Sriharikota. The payload is crafted to measure the intensity of Ultraviolet (UV) Radiations and Solar Irradiance in space, tracing the PSLV Orbital Experiment Module (POEM) trajectory at 350 km above sea level. The WESAT payload is designed to measure these critical parameters, allowing for the calculation of the Ultraviolet Index (UVI), which facilitates comparison between space-based data and corresponding ground measurements taken from Earth's surface. By bridging these datasets, the mission aims to enhance our understanding of environmental variability and contribute to improved climate modeling. Normally, UV Radiation Intensity data are obtained from multispectral satellite metadata (spectroradiometers) from meteorological satellites such as MODIS, TEMIS and NOAA, where UV intensity is indirectly estimated by filtering the corresponding spectrum bands. With the launch of WESAT, exclusive point-source data is received for UV Radiation Intensity, corresponding to the direct measurement of UVI in space, which is the first of its kind, compared to the multispectral satellite metadata which indirectly estimates the UVI. Unlike conventional satellites that require batteries or external power, WESAT operates entirely on the photovoltaic effect, enabling its instruments to convert incident sunlight into measurement power directly. Due to this feature, the payload did not have to wait until POEM reached its orbital path at 350 km to start functioning. As soon as the PSLV heat shield separated at 113.68 km (approximately 3 minutes after launch), the payload was exposed to the external environment and automatically began transmitting telemetry data. This altituderesolved dataset is one-of-a-kind, since no other payload begins data transmission prior to orbital insertion. Complementing the satellite, a Ground Monitoring Station has been established at Thiruvananthapuram (Trivandrum), Kerala, India. The station records real-time UV Radiation and Solar Irradiance, Temperature, and Humidity. For meaningful comparison, the space data was timestamped to Earth-based measurements, ensuring synchronization. A comparative analysis of multiple parameters, including UV radiation intensity and solar radiation intensity (solar irradiance) was conducted. By integrating space-based and ground-based datasets, WESAT mission elucidates the influence of atmospheric filtering on UV intensity, characterizes conditions leading to extreme UVI values, and provides an understanding of solar radiation variability in the lower atmosphere.

title: A Design and Analysis Method for an Inverse-Coupled Inductor Core Used in Interleaved Boost Converters
authors: Yu-Chen Liu, Tuo-Chen Lin, Tzu-Chieh Hsu
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: The interleaved structure is widely employed in multiphase PFC, boost, and buck converters. Its primary advantages lie in effectively distributing the phase currents, reducing power loss and thermal stress, and significantly suppressing input and output current ripples. By implementing coupled inductors within an interleaved topology, the circuit performance can be further enhanced. When independent inductors are integrated into a shared magnetic path to form coupled inductors, the coupling can be categorized as either forward or inverse. This study focuses on the application of inverse-coupled inductors in interleaved boost converters using low-permeability powder cores without additional air gaps, and analyzes their impact on input current ripple.

title: Leakage-Safe Machine Learning for Retail Demand Forecasting and Inventory Optimization via Hybrid Ensembling
authors: Hemalatha P, Abhinav K, Jaswanth G, Manoj S, B. Balaji
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: To operate under the non-stationary market conditions, effective inventory control needs both precise and operational demand forecasts. The paper discusses a machine learning-based demand forecasting system to optimize inventory based on three goals: (i) the design of a leakage-safe, time-series pipeline with strict sales-data curation and business-oriented feature engineering, (ii) the benchmarking and selection of forecasting models with time-aware validation and business-oriented error measures (iii) the enhancement of production reliability with a hybrid ensemble and the explainability of inventory planning. It starts with the preparation of data, such as missing-value treatment, outlier capping, and the leakage-safe temporal encoding (calendar effects, seasonality, and lag/rolling statistics). To relate forecasts to inventory behavior, we construct features based on transaction history, like sell-through rate and days-of-supply indicators and proxy measures of price elasticity based on the co-movements of prices and volume. Model learning and selection come after rolling/expanding-window validation in order to retain the time causality and measure the robustness between demand regimes. Our evaluation of sparse linear structure by multiple linear regression and Lasso, non-linear interactions by Random Forest, and high-order gradient-boosted patterns by LightGBM, is under MAE, RMSE, and MAPE/SMAPE. A hybrid ensemble is a performance-weighted collection of the best base learners that help in reducing variance and alleviating drift to generate better demand prediction when refilling stocks. Lastly, it is also supported by interpretability through feature-importance analysis, residual diagnostics, and visualization that allow the planner to detect important demand drivers and systematic error modes. The suggested framework enhances the forecast fidelity and facilitates stockout minimization and overstock management through data-driven procurement and replenishment planning.

title: Optimisation of Multi-Level Inverter System for Harmonics Reduction Using Hybrid Artificial Bee Colony and Fire Fly Algorithms
authors: Ibraheem Auwal, Mohammed Ahmed, Mohammed Babayo, Abdullahi Lanre Amoo, Sani Sabo Mohammed, Abdurrazak Sabo
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: The presentation was on the application the hybrid of artificial bee colony (ABC) and firefly (FA) algorithms with the aim of achieving a better reduction of harmonic magnitude in a twenty-one level inverter system. Harmonics generation has been a challenging issue in direct current (DC) to alternating current (AC) converters (inverters). Employing multilevel inversion is one of the approaches for suppressing the effect of harmonics and can be highly improved by selecting the firing angles of the switching power electronic devices optimally which are usually the transistors. The work examined the ability of the combination of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{A B C}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{F A(A B C F A)}$</tex> for harmonic reduction in a single-phase 21-levels asymmetric cascaded H-bridged inverter system by optimizing the switching angles of the power transistors such that the Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) is minimized. The study was conducted by simulation using the MATLAB/SIMULINK environment. Results showed that the ABCFA algorithm was better than the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{A B C}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{F A}$</tex> in terms of convergence and harmonic reduction. It converges faster by about 30 % with better optimum values yielding the lowest THD which was below 5%, indicating between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{10-20 \%}$</tex> improvement over when either the ABC or FA was considered alone for modulation indices (MI) between 0.4 to 1.2.

title: Benchmarking Time Series Forecasting Models on Seasonal and Non-Seasonal Data: A Case Study of PM10 Air Pollution and IDX Composite
authors: Christopher Andreas, Yohana Jocelyn Guntur, Felicia Joshlyn Purnomo, Rudi Limantara, Satria Adi Nugraha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: This study conducts a cross-domain benchmarking of time series forecasting models using two real-world datasets with contrasting temporal characteristics: PM10 air pollution data representing seasonal environmental time series and the Indonesia Composite Index (IDX Composite) representing non-seasonal financial data. Three forecasting paradigms are evaluated: statistical models (ARIMA/SARIMA), machine learning (Support Vector Regression), and deep learning (Long Short-Term Memory). A consistent experimental protocol is applied using multiple out-of-sample forecasting horizons (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7,14,28$</tex>, and 56 days) and performance metrics including RMSE, MAE, and MAPE. The results show that SARIMA achieves superior performance for medium- and long-term PM10 forecasting due to its capability to model annual seasonality, while LSTM performs competitively for short-term environmental prediction. In contrast, ARIMA consistently outperforms SVR and LSTM across all forecasting horizons for IDX Composite data, indicating that linear autoregressive structures adequately capture non-seasonal financial dynamics. These findings demonstrate that forecasting performance is strongly influenced by data characteristics and that higher model complexity does not necessarily yield better accuracy. The study provides empirical guidance for model selection in seasonal and non-seasonal time series applications.

title: IoT - Based Smart Gloves for Speech Impaired Person with Emergency Alert System
authors: Prakash P, Dineshkumar B, S. Rupikaa, S. Sasireka, A.l Pavithra, S. Vishnuvardhan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: The new system offers a smart emergency alerting and medical monitoring device that works through the internet of things (IoT) and is specifically intended for people who cannot talk or manually activate a regular alert system during an emergency. Using an accelerometer (the MPU6050) to detect certain pre-set hand signals, the device can send out an emergency signal automatically when it detects alarming or unusual physical activity. The ESP32 microcontroller integrates gesture detection, temperature and heart rate monitoring, voice alerts, and GSMbased messaging in order to send out alerts. Whenever the user performs an emergency gesture or the system detects an irregularity among any of the user's vital signs, the system will generate an immediate voice alert, and the user's GPS location will be sent via text messages to pre-registered emergency contacts. This automatic, hands-free, and ‘real-time’ means of sending alerts to emergency services greatly reduce the response time to any emergency situation, thereby improving overall safety for elderly individuals, patients, and those working in dangerous or hazardous environments. The technologies employed in this device (i.e. gesture recognition, vital monitoring, wireless communication, and IoT integration) create a small, dependable, and permanent means of responding to emergencies.

title: Law Comrade: Intelligent Interpretation of Law
authors: Shrii Varsha B S, Sreena M, Indumathi S, Insol Rajasekar S, Rajan C
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Law Comrade: Intelligent Interpretation of Law is a novel platform designed to address the dual challenges of legal information inaccessibility and insecure client-advocate communication in India. The system vertically integrates two key components: (1) a public-facing, conversational AI query system built using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) on a defined legal corpus, such as the Indian Penal Code, and (2) a secure, multi-tenant portal for private client-advocate collaboration. The methodology involved designing a secure web architecture with distinct user roles, implementing real-time chat, and developing a secure document-handling pipeline for private AI analysis. The prototype was successfully developed and evaluated, demonstrating robust performance in its core functionalities, including the RAG query system and secure, low-latency communication. The result is a validated proof concept for an integrated legal-tech architecture, demonstarting the feasibility of bridging the gap between public legal information and private professional consultation. The platform additionally emphasizes strong compliance and trust safeguards, incorporating jurisdiction-aware responses, DPDPaligned consent flows, and explicit differentiation between educational legal information and professional legal advice.

title: A State-Resilient Framework for Uninterrupted Digital Payment Processing
authors: K. Venkatesh, R.T. Ram Kumar, S.B. Sathuragiri, T.M. Mithul, S. Yojit Vignesh
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: With the emergence of digital payment systems, the financial inclusion process has been transformed in the manner it is being undertaken and the system is likely to break down when faced with unstable connectivity and overloading of servers. The existing technology, be it blockchain or centralized, should always be connected to the internet, and thus will not serve semi-urban and rural locations. The difficulty is encountered in the attempts to provide smooth digital payment systems in case the network fails. Anchor app solution to this is the implementation of a resilient queuing system of the state that saves the transactions of the users offline until connectivity is restored after which the transactions are synchronized. The goal is to have fault-tolerant, inclusive and scalable digital payment systems that can be utilized by everybody irrespective of the stability of the network. The rationale behind the solution is the increasing requirements of systems that do not fail at the point of service delivery and those that are able to safeguard against the issues of loss of transaction, duplication of transaction and other issues of accessibility. The solution brings together off-chain synchronization, rollback protection, multi-account management, and cryptographic verification in attempt to offer a hybrid offline-first digital payment system.

title: Biobutanol Blended with Water as an Alternative Fuel for a Spark-Ignition Engine
authors: Mattana Santasnachok, Ekkachai Sutheerasak, Worachest Pirompugd, Pramote Laipradit, Charoen Chinwanitcharoen, Sathaporn Chuepeng
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: Biobutanol is emerging as an alternative resource to bioethanol due to its superior fuel properties to bioethanol. Therefore, the research proposes the novelty of biobutanol mixed with water as an alternative fuel for a SI engine. For testing fuel properties, biobutanol should not be mixed with more than 30% water, because it will cause rapid separation and severe engine knock. Its heating value and air-fuel ratio reduced by 63 % and 30 % compared to neat biobutanol, respectively. SI engine experiments using biobutanol-water blends were conducted under constant speed and varying load. Results showed that parameters of performance and emission changed. Biobutanol mixed with water up to 30% increased BTE by 8% and 3%, but BSFC increased by 52% and 29% compared with gasohol 91 and pure biobutanol, respectively. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> increased by 4% and 6%, NO enlarged by 67% and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 2 \%}$</tex>, CO reduced by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 3 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 \%}$</tex>, and UHC decreased by 61% and 50% compared with gasohol 91 and neat biobutanol, respectively.

title: Multi-material Direct Ink Writing and Embroidery for Stretchable Wearable Sensors
authors: Lukas Cha, Ryman Hashem, Ria Prakash, Tanguy Declety, Wenze Zhang, Liang He
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: The development of wearable sensing systems for sports performance tracking, rehabilitation, and injury prevention has driven a growing demand for smart garments that combine comfort, durability, and accurate motion detection. This paper presents a textile-compatible fabrication workflow that integrates multi-material direct ink writing with automated embroidery to create stretchable strain sensors directly embedded onto garments. The process combines sequential multimaterial printing of a silicone-carbon grease-silicone stack with automated embroidery that provides both mechanical fixation and electrical interfacing in a single step. The resulting hybrid sensor demonstrates stretchability up to 120% strain while maintaining electrical continuity, with approximately linear behaviour up to 60% strain $\left(R^{2}=0.99\right)$, a gauge factor of 31.4, and a hysteresis of 22.9%. Repeated loading-unloading tests over 80 cycles show baseline and peak drift of 0.135% and 0.236% per cycle, respectively, reflecting moderate cycle-to-cycle stability. Mechanical testing further confirms that the silicone-fabric interface remains intact under large deformation, with failure occurring in the textile rather than at the stitched boundary. As a preliminary proof of concept, the sensor was integrated into wearable elbow and knee sleeves for joint angle monitoring, showing a clear correlation between normalised resistance change ($\Delta R / R$) and bending angle. By addressing both mechanical fixation and electrical interfacing through embroidery-based integration, this approach provides a reproducible and scalable pathway for incorporating printed stretchable electronics into textile systems for motion capture and soft robotic applications.

title: Investigation on Improved PSFB Soft-Switching Technology
authors: Fang Fei
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: The trend toward higher switching frequencies is a crucial direction in the development of power converters. Switching actions often generate significant overshoots in voltage and current, thereby raising switching losses and intensifying EMI,becoming a primary obstacle to further frequency improvement. To address this problem, this study explores soft-switching methods for phase-shifted full-bridge converters and presents an enhanced ZVZCS topology.In the presented topology, capacitors are paralleled featuring ZVS in the leading leg and series-diode-based ZCS in the lagging leg enable ZCS. An improved PWM control strategy is employed to precisely match the resonant process with the switching actions. Based on an analysis of typical soft-switching circuits, the operating modes and the soft-switching implementation mechanism of the proposed topology are elucidated. The key parameters, including the high-frequency transformer, resonant components, and output filter network, are designed accordingly. A simulation model is established using Saber software for verification, and an experimental prototype is developed for measurement. Simulation and experimental results confirm that the proposed topology achieves ZVS in the leading leg and ZCS in the lagging leg. Voltage spikes are significantly reduced, and current spikes are effectively suppressed.The converter maintains high efficiency across the full load range. The described improved topology offers a favorable trade-off between ZVZCS performance and implementation simplicity, providing a valuable reference for the engineering design of high-frequency power converters [1].

title: Enabling Localization and Mapping for Heterogeneous Robots in Orbit-to-Surface Teleoperation Setup
authors: Xiaozhou Luo, Marco Sewtz, Timo Bachmann, Nesrine Batti, Sant Brinkman, Jörg Butterfaß, Emiel Den Exter, Tristan Ehlert, Werner Friedl, Thomas Gumpert, Philipp G. Knestel, Anne Köpken, Viktor Langofer, Florian S. Lay, Ajithkumar N. Manaparampil, Luisa Mayershofer, Antonin Raffin, Anne Reichert, Nicole Roshardt, Annika Schmidt, Florian Schmidt, Lioba Schürmann, Daniel Seidel, Riccardo Giubilato, Rudolph Triebel, Adrian S. Bauer, Daniel Leidner, Rute Luz, Peter Schmaus, Thomas Krüger, Neal Y. Lii
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: Future human spaceflight beyond low Earth orbit (LEO) will require robotic assistance to reduce astronaut workload while enhancing research capabilities. To advance humanoid service and assistance robotics for future exploration missions, the Surface Avatar ISS telerobotic technology demonstration mission, a collaboration between the German Aerospace Center (DLR) and the European Space Agency (ESA), demonstrates such concepts by enabling astronauts aboard the International Space Station (ISS) to remotely control heterogeneous robots in a Martian analog environment. Reliable localization and mapping are critical for autonomous capabilities in environments where established methods, such as the Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS), are unavailable or unreliable. This paper presents a visual simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) framework designed for diverse robotic platforms, including DLR's humanoid Rollin' Justin, quadruped Bert, and ESA's Interact rover. DLR's stationary Enhanced LAnder Functional AssistaNT (ELAFANT) and ESA's Spot quadruped, relying on a proprietary navigation stack, are excluded from the study. Building on Rollin' Justin's initial implementation, the framework was extended to robots with limited sensing capabilities using a camera-agnostic SLAM pipeline. A unified mapping approach enables consistent localization across platforms and incremental map extension, supporting real-time performance in large-scale environments. The implementation was validated in six orbit-to-ground telerobotic sessions, confirming reliable performance and scalability. Results from these sessions demonstrate the robustness of the approach and its ability to maintain reliable localization across multiple robots in a challenging, GNSS-denied setting.

title: TCP Performance Analysis for Cislunar Networks
authors: Qinqing Zhang, Ayush Dusia, Yow-Jian Lin, Joseph Ishac, Sandra Schroeder
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: This paper describes the analysis and evaluation of the Transmission Control Protocol (TCP) performance over cislunar networks which are characterized by large bandwidth-delay-product (BDP). There has been a strong interest in utilizing the Internet Protocol (IP) and protocol suites (e.g. TCP/IP) for space communication networks. TCP is widely deployed and extensively used in terrestrial networks for many applications. The original TCP flow control and congestion control algorithms were designed to be robust and adaptive to different network conditions. However, many terrestrially configured TCP will experience performance degradation due to the very high BDP of space communication networks. Flow control algorithms have evolved in the past decades, including new TCP congestion control algorithm variants. These variants do not change the TCP protocol structure and are interoperable with each other. In this paper, three TCP variants are examined and selected for analysis: TCP NewReno, TCP LinuxReno and H-TCP, which are mostly suitable for cislunar networks. In addition, tuning of some TCP configuration parameters are explored and several beneficial TCP options and extensions are discussed. The performance analysis and evaluation are based on network simulation using the well-known NS3 simulation tool to generate quantitative results. The NS3 TCP module implementation was modified for evaluating different congestion control algorithms and TCP options. The detailed simulation results provide insights on parameter configurations and algorithm selection such that TCP can perform well over cislunar networks.

title: Research on Key Equipment Selection and Protection Technology for Substations in Sandy Gobi Desert Environments
authors: Tian Jing, Dan Chen, Pengfei Wang, Siyu Pan, Jianning Liu
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: This work focuses on the compound threats posed by extreme low temperatures and strong wind-blown sand to substation equipment in Sandy Gobi Desert (SGD) areas. It systematically analyzes the damage mechanisms of low temperature and strong wind-blown sand, emphasizing their synergistic effects. Based on this analysis, key equipment selection schemes for transformers, Gas Insulated Switchgear (GIS) equipment, and enclosure structures are proposed. Simultaneously, a "Prevention-Blocking-Monitoring" tri-integrated protection technology system is constructed, encompassing measures such as graded dust prevention, intelligent temperature and humidity regulation, and long-term anti-corrosion. This research provides a practical technical pathway for substation equipment in SGD areas to cope with extreme environmental challenges, holding significant practical value for ensuring the reliability of power infrastructure in SGD new energy bases and supporting the national energy transition strategy.

title: Reduced-order Neural Modeling with Differentiable Simulation for High-Detail Tactile Perception
authors: Yuhu Guo, Zhikai Shen, Jiasheng Qu, Chenghao Qian, Yuming Huang, Bin Chen, Guoxing Fang
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)
abstract: Tactile perception is key to dexterous manipulation, yet simulating high-resolution elastomer deformation remains computationally prohibitive. Finite element methods (FEM) deliver high fidelity but demand costly remeshing, while Material Point Methods (MPM) suffer from heavy particlememory tradeoffs. We propose a reduced-order neural simulation framework that couples coarse-grained MPM dynamics with an implicit neural decoder to reconstruct sub-particle tactile details from compact latent states. The framework learns a continuous deformation manifold from paired high- and lowresolution simulations, enabling physically consistent, differentiable inference. Compared to the TacIPC, our method achieves over 65% faster simulation and 40% lower memory usage, while maintaining better geometric fidelity. In tactile rendering and 3D surface reconstruction, our methods further improve accuracy by 25% and produce realistic depth images and surface mesh within a faster inference speed. These results demonstrate that the proposed reduced-order neural model enables high-detail, physically grounded tactile simulation with substantial efficiency gains for robotic interaction and optimization.

title: Automated Intravenous Infusion Control Based on Urine Density Monitoring
authors: Surenthar S, Bhuvanesh D, Sunandra V D, Naveeth Kumar R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Infusion therapy IV is a vital clinical procedure of administering fluids and medication to patients. Traditionally, IV therapy requires manual monitoring and adjustment by medical staff and this adds to workload and also performance of medical staff might error regarding over-infusion, under-infusion or failure to promptly change fluid choice. Though urine density is an effective biomarker of renal activity and hydration level, automated fluid control systems can almost never include it. This research proposes an automated IV infusion system that causes selection of intravenous fluids depending on the density of the urine. A load cell that has been integrated with a HX711 amplifier is used to measure urine density in real time. A microcontroller of ESP32 compares the data and processes it by comparing it with preset values. The result of this is what determines the kind of solenoid valves to be turned on to deliver the right fluid at a constant time. The value of the urine density is displayed on an LCD panel so convenience. The proposed design prevents clinical mistakes, ensures the accurate fluid therapy, and decreases the workload of nurses.

title: Design and Verification of a One-to-Three Routing Module for On-Chip Communication
authors: Sri Bharani Kumar S, Poovarasan R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Design and Verification of a One-to-Three Routing Module for On-Chip Communication presents the development of a compact router architecture in Verilog HDL, highlighting its design, implementation, and verification aspects. The router integrates input registers, address based routing logic, FIFO buffers, and a finite state machine to manage control and synchronization. A self-checking testbench developed in Xilinx validates the design under multiple scenarios, including directed and random packet injection, buffer overflow, and corner case handling. Simulation results demonstrate low latency, high throughput, and reliable packet delivery, confirming that the proposed router meets performance and scalability requirements for modern System on Chip and Network on Chip applications.

title: An IoT-Based Gesture Control System for Mouse, Touch, Typing using Relative Positioning
authors: Jyoti Neeli, Advaith V, Aditya K, Albin Antony M, Abhilash J Loni
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Trends in Advanced Computing (ICCTAC)
abstract: Gesture-based human–pc interaction has gained growing attention as an opportunity to conventional input devices along with keyboards and mice. This paper presents an IoT-based gesture control gadget that allows cursor navigation using a wearable inertial dimension unit (IMU) and an ESP32 microcontroller. The device captures wrist movements using the IMU sensor, converting the identified motions into cursor movement using a relative positioning technique. Sensor data is sent wirelessly to a laptop application via UDP communication, enabling real-time gesture processing and cursor control through a Qt-based interface. To assess system performance, experiments were conducted to measurecommunication latency, cursor accuracy, and gesture recognition reliability. The prototype achieved an average system latency of around 40–50 ms under normal operating conditions, demonstrating stable real-time cursor interaction. The proposed device offers an affordable and portable solution for gesture-based cursor control, showcasing the potential of IMU-based wearable interfaces for human–computer interaction. Future developments will focus on expanding the system to support additional interaction methods, such as gesture-enhanced text input, and implementing advanced motion filtering techniques to enhance stability and precision in relative positioning.

title: A Voltage Violation Evaluation Method for Distribution System with Distributed PVs Based on Feeder Classification
authors: Can Chen, Xiao Wang, Xiaoyang Deng, Tianyu Su
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: With the advancement of the dual-carbon strategy in China, distributed renewable energy has been increasingly applied in the power system. With a high penetration of distributed photovoltaic generations into distribution networks, the power flow reverse feeding and voltage violation may probably occur, which adversely affects the safe and stable operation of distribution system. Traditional power flow calculation method for voltage evaluation is usually based on sufficient measurements and accurate network topology parameters, which cannot be implemented in remote distribution networks with insufficient measurements. In this paper, a voltage violation evaluation method is proposed based on limited measurements. Firstly, a hierarchical evaluation index system for feeder voltage evaluation is performed. Then, a feeder classification method is proposed based on combined Analytic Hierarchy Process(AHP) and fuzzy C-means(FCM). Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified based on the actual test case bed in Jibei Power Grid.

title: The Optimization of Multi-Energy Industrial Park Considering Production Flexibility and Multi-Market Participation
authors: Yuchen Fang, Lingyang Li, Minglei Bao, Hengyu Hui, Chao Guo
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: With the increasing integration of electricity and ancillary service markets, multi-energy industrial parks face new opportunities in participating in multi-markets. However, existing studies either focus on integrated energy system operation under given signals or model industrial production as aggregated loads, neglecting proactive market participation and detailed process constraints strategies. To overcome this limitation, the paper develops an integrated optimization model for the production scheduling and energy market participation of industrial parks. It jointly considers detailed industrial production processes and multi-market energy trading decisions. A multi-market decision framework is established for participation in electricity energy, peak-shaving ancillary service, natural gas, and heat markets. Energy procurement plans are coordinated with capacity bidding decisions in the ancillary service market to minimize operating costs. Meanwhile, an industrial process model is constructed to describe multi-state production modes, task constraints, and different energy consumption characteristics. Production flexibility constraints are considered to ensure consistency between trading strategies and actual production schedules.
