publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: This paper presents an experimental investigation into the influence of nanoparticles on the electro-optic response in propylene carbonate using the Kerr effect. Experiments were conducted using a parallel-plate electrode configuration under DC electric field that the induced birefringence was analysed through variations in transmitted light intensity. Aluminum oxides were dispersed in the propylene carbonate at concentrations of 0.0025 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{vol} {\%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.005 \text{vol} {\%}$</tex>. Experimental results demonstrate a shift of first intensity ratio peaks by 6.6% and 13.7% for the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0025 \text{vol} {\%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.005 \text{vol} {\%}$</tex> concentration respectively compared to pure propylene carbonate. Consequently, by adding the nanoparticles resulted in a reduction of the Kerr constant by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 3. 5 {\%}}$</tex> and 22.7% with the corresponding first intensity ratio peak toward higher electric field strengths. This indicates that nanoparticles modify the local electric field distribution and molecular polarisability within the liquid.

title: Weakly Supervised Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring via State-Anchored Frozen Large Language Model Adaptation
authors: Yisheng Gao, Bo Liu, Yutong Wu, Wenpeng Luan, Haishen Liang, Kai Wang
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: Non-intrusive load monitoring (NILM) disaggregates household aggregate power into per-appliance profiles without dedicated sub-meters. Conventional supervised approaches require dense frame-level power annotations obtainable only through per-device sub-metering, which is expensive and impractical at scale. In contrast, two cheaper forms of weak supervision are widely accessible: inexact supervision in the form of window-level activity labels, and incomplete supervision in the form of limited binary on/off state labels. We proposed the State-Anchored Hard-Decoupled Architecture (SA-HDA), a three-stage framework that explicitly separates appliance state detection from power amplitude estimation, assigning each sub-task to its appropriate supervision signal. A CRNN trained under the multiple-instance learning framework handles state detection from inexact and incomplete labels; a frozen large language model (LLM) with a compact adapter handles amplitude calibration from scarce power labels; a physics-constrained hard gate anchors LLM outputs to CRNN state decisions at inference. Experiments on UK-DALE across five appliances demonstrated consistent reductions in MAE and SAE over a state-only baseline, with F1 preserved by construction.

title: SpeechAI: A Two-Stage Wav2Vec 2.0 Transformer Pipeline for Automated Stuttering Detection and Dysfluency Classification
authors: Baalakumar N N, Prabakar S, Rajalakshmi T, Farjhana L, Keerthika T, Narmadha A S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Stuttering, affecting <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{1 \%}$</tex> of adults worldwide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{7 0}$</tex> million globally), remains challenging to assess objectively due to reliance on subjective manual annotation of dysfluencies (blocks, repetitions, prolongations, interjections). This paper presents SpeechAI, a productiongrade two-stage deep learning pipeline for automated stammering detection and dysfluency classification, deployed as an interactive Streamlit web application. The system was trained on the Wav2Vec2-base transformer architecture (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 95 \mathrm{M}$</tex> parameters) and fine-tuned on the UCLASS dataset (4,712 clinical 3-second audio segments at 16 kHz from individuals who stutter). The system obtains 83.8% binary accuracy (Fluent/Stutter) and 58.2% accuracy on the 5-class dysfluency task. The methodology consists of the following steps: Minimal DSP processing (mono mixdown, resampling to 16 kHz, and fixed-length segmentation of 3 seconds), followed by the Wav2Vec2 features and layer-wise finetuning of the backbone (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{LR}=3 \mathrm{e}-5$</tex>) and the head (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{LR}=1 \mathrm{e}-4$</tex>). The inference takes 250 ms (GPU) / 1.2 s (CPU), and the confidence-calibrated output provides therapy recommendations based on the evidence. The system maintains a 60 -session analytics dashboard tracking stammer prevalence (28% in demo data, matching clinical rates). Results demonstrate SOTA performance on UCLASS, outperforming single-stage multi-class baselines by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 7-8 \%$</tex> accuracy while reducing compute 40% via conditional Stage 2 activation. Stage <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \mathrm{F} 1=0.82$</tex>, AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.89$</tex>, while macro- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 5 6}$</tex> in Stage 2 reflects inherent multi-label overlap issues. Innovations lie in clinician-friendly deployment with real-time feedback, type-based interventions (“Diaphragmatic Breathing” for blocks, “DAF Therapy” for sound repetitions), and strong generalization from reading to spontaneous speech. SpeechAI closes the research-to-deployment and research-to-clinical tools divide, supporting scalable screening, monitoring, and individualized teletherapy in stuttering therapy. Planned future work includes multilingual support (XLSR-53), frame-level localization, and outcome prediction.

title: AI-Based Predictive Vulnerability Assessment Framework in 6G Network Infrastructures
authors: Anandaraj M D, Jai Arul Jose G, Amala Arul Reji A, Muneshwara M S, T. Ebanesar, A. Alphonse John Kenneth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Trends in Advanced Computing (ICCTAC)
abstract: Currently, Sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks are being designed to provide blistering data-rates and indeed seamless inter-device interconnections. Here we introduce a predictive vulnerability-detection framework, which will leverage the power of a hybrid deep-learning model to foresee network-security threats and prevent them before they can interfere with business. The framework keeps checking the key operational metrics, including packet count, number of bytes sent and received, the CPU load, and memory usage, in order to identify the trend that can potentially be vulnerable. In order to achieve the spatial connections between the network nodes and the change of traffic over time, we combine a Graph Neural Network with a Transformer model. In our experimental analysis, this GNN-Transformer combination is significantly more than the traditional methods, such as RF (Random Forests) and LSTM (Long Short Term Memory) networks, with a prediction accuracy of 97.8%. The results of this study have a sound foundation in developing autonomous and predictive cybersecurity systems that will enhance the dependability, flexibility and reliability of the future 6G communication infrastructures.

title: Two-Stage Hybrid Optical Flow Classifier for Realtime Self-Injurious Behaviour Surveillance in Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder
authors: Siddarth Vinnakota, Krittika Roy, Made Karthikeya, R. Srinath
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a disorder that affects the development of the brain, leading to difficulties in communication and social interaction. Children with ASD often experience Self-Injurious Behaviours (SIB), which include actions such as banging the head on the floor (Hard SIB), as well as softer repetitive behaviours like arm flapping, self-spinning, and tip-toe walking, etc. They may also exhibit communication behaviours such as echolalia. As a result, SIB incidents usually go unnoticed until an individual is harmed. In our paper, we introduce our Two-Stage Hybrid SIB Detection and Alert System that uses standard monocular video feeds. It uses deterministic optical flow screening in the first stage. This stage is used to quickly detect high confidence Hard SIB and Safe SIB in less than 5 milliseconds. It uses SVM, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting classification in the second stage. Features like Flow Acceleration, Motion Entropy, Escalation Rate, Flow Variance Trend, and Head Acceleration are used to detect escalation behaviour that is associated with SIB. Our system achieves 91.5% overall accuracy and 93.1% Hard SIB recall using stratified 5-fold cross-validation on the SSBD dataset, with all class AUC scores above 0.90. As soon as Hard SIB is detected, an immediate alert is sent to the caregiver.

title: Photovoltaic Hosting Capacity Assessment Using Attention Mechanism and Model-free Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yu Zhang, Yuanhang Huang, Hongshegn He, Na Rong
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: Aiming at problem that traditional PV Hosting Capacity (HC) assessment methods relied too much on detailed physical models and led to limitation of computational complexity, a PV hosting capacity assessment method combining attention mechanism and model-free deep reinforcement learning was proposed. A Deep Neural Network (DNN) was constructed to predict node voltages and was combined with Soft Actor Critic (SAC) algorithm for HC assessment, and interactive attention mechanism adaptively focused on intrinsic correlation between state-action pairs to improve estimation accuracy of Q-value and training stability. Case study was unfolded in a real power distribution network and was compared with physical modeling method based on DIgSILENT and model-free method without adaptive time-step weighting. Test results verify that the presented framework attains normalized mean absolute error (NMAE) of 0.0452, normalized root mean square error (NRMSE) of 0.0618, mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 5.93%, and R-squared (R2) of 0.9305. Furthermore, maximum voltage deviation is limited to ±3V, which confirms method validity.

title: Space Interferometry Formation Design Using Relative Orbital Elements: The STARI Mission
authors: Antonio Rizza, Ethan Foss, John Monnier, James W. Cutler, Simone D'Amico
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: The STarlight Acquisition and Reflection toward Interferometry (STARI) mission is a NASA-funded technology demonstrator designed to advance key technologies for future space-based interferometry. It consists of two 8U CubeSats flying in close formation in a Sun synchronous terminator orbit, effectively simulating one arm of a distributed interferometer. STARI will, for the first time, successfully demonstrate the reflection and beam transfer of starlight from one spacecraft to another, as well as its injection into a single-mode optical fiber. The mission design, currently in Phase A, is led by University of Michigan, with the Space Rendezvous Laboratory of Stanford University responsible for the trajectory design and on-board Guidance, Navigation, and Control (GNC) system design. This paper presents the mission requirements, concept of operations and swarm trajectory design for the mission, together with a preliminary computation of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta V$</tex> budget. In contrast to current state of the art approaches for space interferometry, the proposed solution is based on a representation of observation requirements in the phase-space defined by relative orbital elements. The proposed approach enables high duty cycles for astronomical observations with very limited fuel consumption under navigation, control and dynamic uncertainties. Moreover, the methodology also provides a clear and elegant geometrical interpretation of the relative orbital state as configuration parameter to define the inertial direction of observation. The mission utilizes frequent transitions between science orbits, which allow for nearly uninterrupted viewing of an inertially fixed target, and standby orbits, which are used for engineering tasks such as reaction wheel desaturation, ground communication, and science replanning. Due to the lack of radial-normal separation between the two spacecraft in their science orbit, this transfer restores passive safety during routine engineering tasks and contingency. The proposed mission design leverages closed-form impulsive control schemes to achieve station keeping and swarm reconfiguration with fuel-optimal solutions. Finally, the choice of a Sun synchronous absolute orbit enables scanning of different portions of the sky during the mission lifetime without changing the relative configuration. The STARI CubeSats require unprecedented level of navigation and control accuracy to achieve mission objectives pushing the need for a fully autonomous six-degrees-of-freedom Guidance Navigation and Control (GNC) system. The success of the STARI mission will accelerate the development of space interferometry missions, pave the way for accessible and systematic exoplanets detection by means of distributed space systems, and enable novel capabilities for spacecraft proximity operations.

title: Multi-Physics Simulation Code LS-LUCA for Innovative Space Transportation System Design
authors: Keiichiro Fujimoto, Kaname Kawatsu, Hiroaki Amakawa
publish: 2026 IEEE Aerospace Conference
abstract: The complexity and scale of space transportation systems, including reusable launch vehicles and human spaceflight systems, have been increasing rapidly, making system design significantly more challenging and requiring consideration of a wide range of factors. Multi-disciplinary coupled physics analyses and probabilistic evaluations are essential even in the early design phase. This study addresses these challenges by introducing a system design framework centered on multi-physics coupling and the development of a simulation code, LS-LUCA (Leading Unified Code for Analysis). LS-LUCA integrates aerodynamics, thermal, structural, control, and safety assessment models, combining high-fidelity and reduced-order models to enable rapid and comprehensive evaluations during the conceptual design stage. The framework also incorporates automation and user-friendly features to minimize dependency on expert knowledge and accelerate design iteration. Furthermore, the framework has been extended toward an AI-assisted engineering concept, where natural language interaction, automated computation, and knowledge capture are combined to support early-phase design. This approach aims to enable traceable and reproducible design iterations through a dialog-based workflow, reducing expert dependency and accelerating evaluations. A proof-of-concept implementation has been developed to demonstrate feasibility and potential benefits. Two representative application cases are presented. The first demonstrates the feasibility assessment of reusable rocket flyback, highlighting the effectiveness of multi-disciplinary concurrent engineering. The second illustrates a probabilistic evaluation of cluster parachute dynamics for human space transportation, addressing uncertainties in initial conditions and atmospheric disturbances. The results confirm that LS-LUCA enables fast and reliable evaluations for complex space transportation systems, contributing to improved competitiveness in the global space industry.

title: Adaptive Support Vector Machine Based for Optimal Sizing of Renewable Generation and Battery Storage for Standalone Households
authors: Sina Shakeri, Hossein Ansari, Amin Mahmoudi, Solmaz Kahourzade
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel adaptive support vector machine based demand-side management strategy for the optimal sizing of battery storage (BS), wind turbine (WT), and photovoltaic (PV) in a standalone household. The proposed strategy uses day-ahead forecasts of wind speed and solar irradiance and the battery state of charge level to reduce electricity consumption when renewable generation is expected to be low. Additionally, it minimizes the levelized cost of electricity (LCOE) by minimizing the system’s net present cost (NPC) over the project lifespan. The NPC includes replacement present value, capital present value, salvage value of the system components, and maintenance present value. To evaluate the efficiency of the proposed method, a typical case study from South Australia with three different configurations of BS, PV, and WT is considered. The results demonstrate that a system including BS, PV, and WT is the best configuration that achieves the lowest LCOE and eliminates load curtailment. Furthermore, the proposed method enhances system reliability by eliminating load curtailment (from 6 days to zero) and extends battery lifetime by 3 times through intelligent battery management.

title: Spatio-Temporal Collaborative Optimization Strategy for Electric Vehicle Charging and Swapping Oriented to Distribution Network Operation
authors: Zhiwei Wang, Wei Jiang, Xinyun Guo, Aihua Zhou, Min Xu, Zhiqi Xu
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: The massive integration of Electric Vehicles (EVs) introduces significant spatio-temporal stochasticity to distribution networks, intensifying the operational coupling between transportation and power systems. Uncoordinated charging behaviors often exacerbate peak loads and voltage violations. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a spatio-temporal collaborative optimization strategy for EV charging and swapping, explicitly oriented towards distribution network operation. First, a coupled transportation-power model is established to quantify the impact of EV kinematics on power flow dynamics. Subsequently, an evaluation method based on Distribution Locational Marginal Pricing (DLMP) is introduced to assess the grid’s sensitivity to load variations and its requirement for flexibility. By decomposing DLMP into energy, loss, and congestion components, a dynamic cyclic optimization framework is constructed. This framework utilizes a multi-dimensional pricing mechanismincorporating grid status, traffic congestion, and station queuingto guide EVs towards grid-friendly decisions. Case studies on a real-world Wuhan road network coupled with the IEEE 118-node system demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy. Results show that the approach successfully alleviates grid congestion, reduces network losses by 5.9%, and lowers generation costs by 3.8%, achieving a win-win operation for both systems.

title: Cybershield - Phishing Detection Browser Plugin: A Machine Learning Approach to Web Security
authors: Manu L, R. Jayanthi, Charan Manju Gond
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: One of the most widespread and destructive cybersecurity threats in the online environment are phishing attacks, which affect people and organizations by using fake emails and malicious websites [1]. Although there are traditional antiphishing tools, they are still ineffective in regard to the more advanced attack vectors [2]. The article focuses on describing CyberShield, a browser extension that uses machine learning and deep learning models to identify phishing attacks in real-time [3]. The suggested architecture combines the URL feature extraction, content analysis and sophisticated machine learning algorithms such as XGBoost, Random Forest and neural network models to deliver identification rates above 99 % [4], [5]. The proposed architecture can be deployed to a secure browser, which does not reduce its performance and does not affect user protection [7]. The present piece of work describes the system architecture, feature engineering approach, machine learning models that are used and experimental validation findings and illustrates the effectiveness of CyberShield in resisting the changing phishing threats [10].

title: Technical Research on Modification of Auxiliary Boiler System in Hot Standby Condition for Nuclear Power Plant
authors: TingYan Xie, Xiaoliang Fu, YingFeng Xiong
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems such as cavitation, component damage, and pressure fluctuations of the main feedwater pumps caused by low-flow operation under the long-term hot standby condition of the auxiliary boiler system in a nuclear power plant, this paper proposes a modification scheme of adding a dedicated hot standby pump and supporting pipelines through fault analysis, root cause tracing, scheme design, and simulation verification. The modification scheme optimizes equipment selection and system flow, and the feasibility and safety of the scheme are verified by hydraulic calculation, net positive suction head (NPSH) analysis and water hammer simulation. The results show that after modification, the system can maintain stable flow and pressure under hot standby condition, avoids main pump damage caused by low-flow operation, and that the pipeline stress and water hammer force both meet the requirements of the ASME B31.1 standard, which significantly improves the reliability and stability of the XCA system(Auxiliary Boiler System in Nuclear Power Plants). This research provides a technical reference for the optimization of hot standby conditions of similar auxiliary systems in nuclear power plants.

title: The Balancing Group Mechanism in Germany and Its Implications for the Development of China's Electricity Market
authors: Qin Yabin, Cao Yuanyuan, Zou Peng, Xie Yi, Zhang Chao, Li Yumin
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: With the continuous increase in the proportion of renewable energy and the accelerated entry of a vast and diverse range of new market players, power market balance are becoming increasingly prominent. When drawing on successful international cases of power market construction, Germany's power market has achieved the allocation of balance costs to the entire society through its unique balancing group mechanism. This mechanism effectively improves Germany's ability to absorb renewable energy without significantly increasing the scale of real-time balancing markets and backup power groups. Therefore, in the context of the new power system, this article first discusses the basic situation of Germany's power system in detail, then analyzes the German balancing settlement group model, including the definition of balancing groups, participating entities, and operational advantages. It then introduces the operating mechanism of Germany's balancing groups, provides examples of imbalance settlement, and finally elaborates on the important reference and studying role for the design of China's power market and power balance mechanism system, and presents the conclusions of this paper.

title: Experimental Study and Preventive Measures for Galvanic Corrosion of Duplex Stainless Steel Seawater Butterfly Valves
authors: Chen Yang, Yang Shaowei, Fu Xiaoliang, Wang Guobin
publish: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
abstract: This study aims to determine the cause of corrosion of disc plates through methods such as galvanic corrosion testing, metallographic analysis, and composition analysis. And based on the principle of galvanic corrosion, measures to prevent galvanic corrosion are formulated, including spraying ceramic coatings, adding insulation bushings, and adding decouplers. When duplex stainless steel comes into contact with titanium alloy and is exposed to seawater, it is prone to galvanic corrosion. By using methods such as valve plate spraying ceramic coating, fastener outer lining with polyetheretherketone liner, and adding decouplers, it is possible to effectively isolate the galvanic corrosion circuit and prevent equipment from experiencing galvanic corrosion. The relative magnitude of the natural corrosion potential of the two materials is not determined.

title: Wind Turbine Risk Assessment Method Based on Cascade Fuzzy Inference System Model
authors: Bowen Jiang, Chenhao Sun, Yaoding Wang
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration rate of wind power, accurate assessment of wind turbine operation risk has become a key issue to ensure the safe and economical operation of the power system. This paper proposes a risk assessment method for wind turbines based on Cascade Fuzzy Inference System model (CFIS). Firstly, a feature library is constructed based on historical operation and maintenance records, and the fuzzy input features are obtained by using the probability density distribution function. Then, the joint fuzzy inference structure is introduced to decompose the global rule base into several cascading subsystems, which effectively reduces the amount of operation. Finally, the final quantitative risk index is obtained by the center of gravity method. Through numerical examples, the proposed method can effectively improve the accuracy and robustness of wind turbine risk assessment, and provide a feasible tool for condition maintenance decision-making and operation risk control.

title: Optimal Damping Design of Virtual Synchronous Generators Considering Grid Strength Variations
authors: Jaehun Jung, Inchan Hong, Seungyeop Baek, Seungmin Jung, Yeuntae Yoo
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: The increasing penetration of inverter-based resources has highlighted the importance of Grid-Forming(GFM) control strategies, in which the Virtual Synchronous Generator (VSG) is widely recognized as a promising solution for enhancing system stability in weak grids. In VSG control, the damping coefficient plays a crucial role in shaping the active power dynamics, suppressing oscillations, and ensuring stable synchronization with the grid. However, the optimal value of the damping coefficient varies significantly depending on the grid strength, particularly as characterized by the Short-Circuit Ratio (SCR) and the line impedance X/R ratio. Although several model-based approaches for tuning VSG damping have been proposed, few studies provide a systematic, data-driven methodology that quantifies how the optimal damping changes across different grid conditions. To address this challenge, this work proposes a simulation-based optimization approach that determines the optimal VSG damping coefficient across a wide range of SCR and X/R conditions. The damping value is identified by minimizing a time-domain objective function obtained from PSCAD simulations, where both a conventional error-integral metric and a derivative-weighted overshoot penalty are evaluated. The resulting optimal damping values are visualized as a three-dimensional surface to reveal their dependence on the grid characteristics. Comparative stepresponse analyses show that the proposed overshoot-penalized damping significantly reduces transient oscillations relative to the conventional tuning method. Finally, the optimization results are presented as a design guideline map to facilitate practical parameter tuning for varying grid environments.

title: AI - Powered Waste Segregation Bin Using Raspberry Pi and MobileNetV2 for Accurate and Real-Time Waste Classification
authors: R. Deebika, Malatthi Sivasundaram, T. Sathya, A. Dhanushya, S. Jonna, S. P. Harish
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Applications Theme: Healthcare and Internet of Things (AIMLA)
abstract: The main objective of the project is to build a smart waste segregation system that uses AI and MobileNetV2 deep learning model along with IoT-based hardware to classify biodegradable and non-biodegradable waste. In this study, two groups are compared. Image processing with SVM and KNN algorithms is used for waste classification and serves as the baseline for evaluation. This work proposes a deep learning model called MobileNetV2 with a camera to classify waste images. The classification output is sent to Raspberry pi, which controls servo motors for automated waste segregation. The system uses ultrasonic sensors to track bin levels and GSM/IoT modules to send alert notifications. The experimental results showed an average classification accuracy of 92.6 %, indicating improved segregation efficiency. Manual handling was reduced by 70 %. The monitoring of the bins in real-time has prevented overflow in 95 %. The waste classification and compartment activation took an average of less than 2.1 seconds to respond. Conclusion. The combination of the MobileNetV2 deep learning model with IoT and embedded systems brings a huge advantage to waste segregation in terms of accuracy and efficiency.

title: Hybrid CEEMDAN-EWT-Transformer-GAN Framework for Very-Short-Term Wind Power Forecasting
authors: Pandu Wicaksono, Panji Arisaputra, Anindhita Dewabharata, Sulistyo Heripracoyo, Gabriella Angelina Widjaja
publish: 2026 International Conference on Current Research in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (ICCRAIDS)
abstract: Very-short-term wind power forecasting is essential for ensuring power grid stability, yet the inherent intermittency and high volatility of wind resources frequently hinder its accuracy. This study proposes a hybrid forecasting framework that integrates a two-stage signal decomposition technique, Complete Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition with Adaptive Noise (CEEMDAN) and Empirical Wavelet Transform (EWT), with a Transformer-based deep learning architecture and Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP) for robust data augmentation. This study evaluates the proposed framework using real-world datasets from France and Turkey, representing distinct meteorological characteristics. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed CEEMDAN-EWT-Transformer-GAN model effectively captures complex temporal dependencies, achieving a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 3.82% on the French dataset. Furthermore, the model showed significant robustness in the highly volatile Turkish dataset, where the hybrid approach successfully mitigated errors from standalone decomposition models, reducing MAPE from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 6. 7 8 \%}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6. 6 8 \%}$</tex>. The Diebold-Mariano test confirms that the proposed framework significantly outperforms traditional baseline models (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.05$</tex>) on the France dataset. In contrast, results on the Turkey dataset are not statistically significant. These results offer a reliable solution for high-precision energy management systems.

title: Improved LVRT Control Strategy of Voltage Support for GFM Inverter Based on Adaptive Virtual Impedance
authors: Haobo Zhang, Jian Chen, Xiaolin Li, Wen Zhang
publish: 2026 16th International Conference on Power, Energy, and Electrical Engineering (CPEEE)
abstract: With the increasing depth of research into grid-forming (GFM) inverters, their ability to maintain a certain level of voltage support capability during low voltage ride-through (LVRT) periods has become of significant importance. This paper proposes a low-voltage ride-through control strategy based on virtual impedance, aiming to achieve a certain degree of voltage support during low-voltage ride-through events. Firstly, by designing the impedance ratio of the virtual impedance during asymmetric faults, the maximum phase current output of the gridforming inverter system is limited to the current threshold, ensuring current does not exceed limits. Secondly, by controlling the value of the virtual impedance, precise control of positive and negative sequence voltages at the grid connection point is achieved, thereby attaining precise voltage support. Finally, simulations and experiments validate the effectiveness of current limiting and precise voltage support in grid-forming inverters during asymmetric faults.

title: Tawasol: An AI-Enabled System for Real Time Translation of Arabic Sign Language in Healthcare
authors: Shahad Alsofyani, Nadine Akkari, Ammar Abdullah, Abdulrahman Jameel, Weam Gahtan, Abdullah Tero, Sobhy Alagha
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI Innovations and Industry (ICAIII)
abstract: Effective communication between healthcare professionals and non-verbal patients remains a critical challenge in clinical environments. This paper presents Tawasol, an AI-enabled system for real-time Arabic Sign Language (ArSL) translation designed to facilitate communication between non-verbal patients and healthcare workers. The proposed system integrates computer vision techniques using MediaPipe hand-landmark detection with a MobileNetV2-based deep learning model implemented in TensorFlow to recognize hand gestures and convert them into written Arabic text. Gesture data are captured using an Intel RealSense RGB camera, and 21 hand-landmark features are extracted and processed for classification. The system was trained on a dataset of gesture images and evaluated using an independent test set representing 20% of the dataset (2,568 images). Experimental results demonstrate a recognition accuracy of 99.7%, with real-time inference latency below 20 ms per frame, enabling smooth interaction in healthcare settings. In addition, a usability evaluation conducted with 20 participants achieved an average usability score of 90%, indicating strong acceptance among potential users. The main contributions of this work include: the design of a real-time ArSL translation system tailored for healthcare communication, integration of deep learning-based gesture recognition with secure medical data management, and empirical evaluation demonstrating high recognition accuracy and real-time performance.

title: Context, Reasoning, and Hierarchy: A Cost-Performance Study of Compound LLM Agent Design in an Adversarial POMDP

title: Blockchain-enhanced Secure RFID Authentication for Anti-counterfeiting in Pharmaceutical Supply Chains
authors: Tayyaba Tariq, Wen-Chung Kuo, Khalid Mahmood, Muhammad Faizan Ayub, Muhammad Asad Saleem, Amina Salhi, Asma Hassan Alshehri
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Counterfeit medicines are produced to deceptively represent authentic and original products, posing severe risks to consumer health and safety. In the modern consumer-centric healthcare system, technologies such as Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) and blockchain are increasingly deployed to ensure real-time tracking, transparency, and trustworthiness of pharmaceutical products. RFID technology has revolutionized the management and tracking of medicines from production to delivery. The combination of RFID and blockchain significantly improves system efficiency, providing both real-time tracking and transparency. However, the transmission of sensitive data over public channels exposes these consumer-oriented systems to critical security threats, including physical cloning, masquerading, and desynchronization attacks. In recent years, various authentication protocols have emerged to address security concerns. However, many of these protocols often fail to offer robust security against potential security threats. Therefore, we introduce a blockchain-enhanced RFID authentication protocol to mitigate these risks for anti-counterfeiting in PSC. Moreover, we exploit PUF to offer extra security against cloning attacks. We analyze and validate the devised protocol through formal and informal security analyses. The results show that it performs efficiently against potential security threats such as physical cloning, masquerading, and desynchronization attacks. Notably, the proposed protocol significantly reduces computation and communication costs by 30.39% and 39.16%, respectively, as compared to other related protocols.

title: Hardware-Friendly Fully Quantized Mamba-2 Model and Its FPGA-Based Accelerator
authors: Hiroshi Fuketa, Masakazu Hioki
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this paper, we propose a fully quantized Mamba-2 model to enable efficient large language model (LLM) processing for edge AI. As a promising next‑generation architecture, Mamba offers a scalable alternative to Transformer-based models with better suitability for edge AI. In the proposed model, the weights and activations are quantized to ternary and 8-bit integer (INT8) precision, respectively, eliminating the need for floating-point operations. Additionally, the non-linear functions included in the original Mamba model, such as SiLU, softplus, exponential, and normalization functions, are replaced with hardware-friendly alternatives, including ReLU, power-of-two functions, and table-lookup-based normalization. These substitutions substantially reduce computational and hardware complexity while largely preserving model accuracy through the proposed two-stage quantization-aware training. Furthermore, we propose an FPGA-based accelerator tailored to the proposed fully quantized Mamba-2 model. The proposed accelerator is implemented on an Ultra96-V2 board and consists primarily of a LUT-based vector-matrix multiplication unit and a DSP-based vector-vector multiplication unit. The former computes the products of INT8-precision vectors and ternary-precision matrices, whereas the latter computes the products of INT8-precision vectors. Implementation and measurement results show that the proposed accelerator achieves a 17% reduction in the FPGA resource cost and a 3.6-fold improvement in energy efficiency compared with conventional FPGA-based LLM accelerators, owing to the hardware‑friendly design.
